<script type="application/ld+json"> 4: { 5: "@context": "https://schema.org", 6: "@type": "HowTo", 7: "name": "ast-grep Pattern Library for Code Consolidation", 8: "description": "This directory contains a comprehensive collection of ast-grep rules for detecting code patterns that are candidates for consolidation across repositories.", 9: "dateModified": "2026-01-19T02:09:57.563Z", 10: "inLanguage": "en-US" 11: } 12: </script> 13: 14: 15: This directory contains a comprehensive collection of ast-grep rules for detecting code patterns that are candidates for consolidation across repositories. 16: 17: ## Directory Structure 18: 19: ``` 20: .ast-grep/ 21: ├── sgconfig.yml # Master configuration 22: ├── duplicate-error-handling.yml # Legacy rule (moved to rules/) 23: ├── fs-operations.yml # Legacy rule (moved to rules/) 24: └── rules/ 25: ├── api/ # API and HTTP patterns 26: │ ├── auth-checks.yml 27: │ ├── error-responses.yml 28: │ ├── express-route-handlers.yml 29: │ └── request-validation.yml 30: ├── async/ # Asynchronous patterns 31: │ ├── await-patterns.yml 32: │ └── promise-chains.yml 33: ├── config/ # Configuration patterns 34: │ ├── config-objects.yml 35: │ └── env-variables.yml 36: ├── database/ # Database operation patterns 37: │ ├── connection-handling.yml 38: │ ├── prisma-operations.yml 39: │ └── query-builders.yml 40: ├── logging/ # Logging patterns 41: │ ├── console-statements.yml 42: │ └── logger-patterns.yml 43: └── utilities/ # Utility function patterns 44: ├── array-map-filter.yml 45: ├── object-manipulation.yml 46: ├── string-manipulation.yml 47: ├── type-checking.yml 48: └── validation.yml 49: ``` 50: 51: **Total Rules:** 18 active rules across 6 categories 52: 53: ## Rule Categories 54: 55: ### 1. Utilities (5 rules) 56: 57: **Purpose:** Detect common utility operations that could be consolidated 58: 59: - **array-map-filter.yml** - Array iteration methods (map, filter, reduce, forEach, find, findIndex) 60: - **object-manipulation.yml** - Object operations (keys, values, entries, assign, spread, JSON) 61: - **string-manipulation.yml** - String operations (trim, case, split, replace, slice, substring) 62: - **type-checking.yml** - Type checking patterns (typeof, instanceof, Array.isArray) 63: - **validation.yml** - Validation logic (null checks, error throws, early returns) 64: 65: ### 2. API Patterns (4 rules) 66: 67: **Purpose:** Identify API-related patterns for standardization 68: 69: - **express-route-handlers.yml** - Route handler patterns (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, PATCH) 70: - **auth-checks.yml** - Authentication/authorization patterns (401/403 responses, token checks) 71: - **error-responses.yml** - API error response patterns (status codes, error objects) 72: - **request-validation.yml** - Request validation patterns (body, params, query validation) 73: 74: ### 3. Database (3 rules) 75: 76: **Purpose:** Track database access patterns for repository pattern consolidation 77: 78: - **prisma-operations.yml** - Prisma ORM patterns (findUnique, create, update, delete, etc.) 79: - **query-builders.yml** - Raw SQL query patterns (SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE) 80: - **connection-handling.yml** - Connection management (connect, disconnect, pooling) 81: 82: ### 4. Configuration (2 rules) 83: 84: **Purpose:** Find configuration access that should be centralized 85: 86: - **env-variables.yml** - Direct process.env access (should use config module) 87: - **config-objects.yml** - Configuration object patterns 88: 89: ### 5. Async Patterns (2 rules) 90: 91: **Purpose:** Detect asynchronous code patterns for consistency 92: 93: - **await-patterns.yml** - Async/await with try-catch patterns 94: - **promise-chains.yml** - Promise chains (.then, .catch, Promise.all, etc.) 95: 96: ### 6. Logging (2 rules) 97: 98: **Purpose:** Track logging patterns for standardization 99: 100: - **console-statements.yml** - Console.log usage (should be replaced with structured logger) 101: - **logger-patterns.yml** - Structured logger usage patterns 102: 103: ## Usage 104: 105: ### Scan a Single Directory 106: 107: ```bash 108: # Scan with all rules 109: ast-grep scan sidequest/ 110: 111: # Scan with specific rule 112: ast-grep scan -r .ast-grep/rules/config/env-variables.yml sidequest/ 113: 114: # Scan multiple directories 115: ast-grep scan sidequest/ sidequest/output/condense/ directory-scan-reports/ 116: ``` 117: 118: ### Scan by Category 119: 120: ```bash 121: # Scan for all utility patterns 122: ast-grep scan -r .ast-grep/rules/utilities/ sidequest/ 123: 124: # Scan for all API patterns 125: ast-grep scan -r .ast-grep/rules/api/ sidequest/ 126: 127: # Scan for all database patterns 128: ast-grep scan -r .ast-grep/rules/database/ sidequest/ 129: ``` 130: 131: ### Output Formats 132: 133: ```bash 134: # JSON output (for programmatic processing) 135: ast-grep scan --json sidequest/ 136: 137: # Default pretty output 138: ast-grep scan sidequest/ 139: 140: # Count matches only 141: ast-grep scan sidequest/ | grep -c "pattern" 142: ``` 143: 144: ## Test Results (on sidequest/ directory) 145: 146: | Rule Category | Matches Found | Notes | 147: |---------------|---------------|-------| 148: | Logger patterns | 3,405 | Very high usage of structured logging ✅ | 149: | Object manipulation | ~50+ | JSON.stringify, Object.entries common | 150: | Env variables | ~20+ | Some direct process.env access found | 151: | Async/await | ~15+ | Good try-catch error handling patterns | 152: | Array operations | TBD | Need to test | 153: | File system ops | ~20+ | Many fs.readFile, fs.writeFile patterns | 154: 155: ## Integration with Duplicate Detection Pipeline 156: 157: ### Phase 1: Pattern Discovery 158: 1. Run all rules across target repositories 159: 2. Collect matches with file locations and line numbers 160: 3. Group by pattern type 161: 162: ### Phase 2: Similarity Analysis 163: 1. Extract AST nodes for each match 164: 2. Compare structural similarity 165: 3. Calculate consolidation scores 166: 167: ### Phase 3: Recommendations 168: 1. Generate consolidation suggestions 169: 2. Prioritize by frequency and impact 170: 3. Suggest abstraction tier (local util, shared package, MCP, agent) 171: 172: ## Adding New Rules 173: 174: ### Rule Template 175: 176: ```yaml 177: id: rule-name 178: language: javascript # or typescript, python, etc. 179: rule: 180: any: # or 'all', 'pattern', 'not', etc. 181: - pattern: $PATTERN1 182: - pattern: $PATTERN2 183: message: Description of what was found 184: severity: info # or warning, error 185: note: Additional context for consolidation analysis 186: ``` 187: 188: ### Best Practices 189: 190: 1. **Use descriptive IDs:** `category-specific-name` (e.g., `database-prisma-operations`) 191: 2. **Provide context:** Include helpful notes about consolidation potential 192: 3. **Test thoroughly:** Verify rule matches intended patterns without false positives 193: 4. **Use appropriate severity:** 194: - `info` - Pattern tracking for analysis 195: - `warning` - Anti-pattern or should be refactored 196: - `error` - Code that must be changed 197: 5. **Document expected matches:** Add examples in rule comments 198: 199: ### Testing New Rules 200: 201: ```bash 202: # Test rule syntax 203: ast-grep test .ast-grep/rules/your-rule.yml 204: 205: # Test against sample code 206: echo 'const x = process.env.FOO' | ast-grep -p 'process.env.$VAR' 207: 208: # Scan specific directory 209: ast-grep scan -r .ast-grep/rules/your-rule.yml path/to/test/ 210: ``` 211: 212: ## Pattern Syntax Reference 213: 214: ### Meta Variables 215: 216: - `$VAR` - Matches a single AST node 217: - `$$$` - Matches zero or more nodes 218: - `$$$ARGS` - Named multi-node match 219: - `$_VAR` - Non-capturing (performance optimization) 220: 221: ### Matchers 222: 223: ```yaml 224: rule: 225: pattern: $EXACT_MATCH # Single pattern 226: any: [$PAT1, $PAT2] # Match any pattern (OR) 227: all: [$PAT1, $PAT2] # Match all patterns (AND) 228: not: { pattern: $PAT } # Negation 229: inside: { pattern: $CONTEXT } # Must be inside context 230: ``` 231: 232: ### Advanced Patterns 233: 234: ```yaml 235: # Match repeated variables (detect duplicates) 236: pattern: $A == $A 237: 238: # Match multi-line code blocks 239: pattern: | 240: if ($COND) { 241: $$$BODY 242: } 243: 244: # Match specific structures 245: pattern: | 246: class $CLASS { 247: $METHOD($$$ARGS) { 248: $$$BODY 249: } 250: } 251: ``` 252: 253: ## Next Steps 254: 255: 1. **Expand rule library:** 256: - React/Vue component patterns 257: - Test framework patterns 258: - Module import/export patterns 259: 260: 2. **Create test suite:** 261: - Sample code for each pattern 262: - Expected match counts 263: - Automated rule validation 264: 265: 3. **Build aggregation tool:** 266: - Collect all scan results 267: - Deduplicate matches 268: - Generate consolidated reports 269: 270: 4. **Integrate with pydantic:** 271: - Define data models for matches 272: - Structure results for analysis 273: - Enable pipeline processing 274: 275: ## Resources 276: 277: - [ast-grep Documentation](https://ast-grep.github.io/) 278: - [Pattern Syntax Guide](https://ast-grep.github.io/guide/pattern-syntax.html) 279: - [Rule Configuration](https://ast-grep.github.io/guide/rule-config.html) 280: - [Playground](https://ast-grep.github.io/playground.html) 281: 282: --- 283: 284: **Created:** 2025-11-11 285: **Last Updated:** 2025-11-23 286: **Version:** 1.0.01: ruleDirs: 2: - name: Set up Python ${{ matrix.python-version }} 28: uses: actions/setup-python@v5 29: with: 30: python-version: ${{ matrix.python-version }} 31: cache: 'pip' 32: - name: Install Node.js dependencies 33: run: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile 34: - name: Install ast-grep (best effort) 35: run: pnpm add -g @ast-grep/cli@latest || echo "⚠️ ast-grep installation failed (non-critical)" 36: continue-on-error: true 37: - name: Install Python dependencies 38: run: | 39: python -m pip install --upgrade pip 40: pip install -r requirements.txt 41: - name: Verify Python pipeline availability 42: run: | 43: echo "Verifying Python pipeline dependencies..." 44: python3 -c "import pydantic; print(f'✅ pydantic {pydantic.__version__} available')" 45: python3 -c "import sys; print(f'✅ Python {sys.version} available')" 46: - name: Verify setup 47: run: pnpm run verify 48: - name: Run TypeScript type checking 49: run: pnpm run typecheck 50: - name: Run unit tests 51: run: pnpm test 52: - name: Run integration tests 53: env: 54: NODE_ENV: test 55: CI: true 56: run: pnpm run test:integration 57: continue-on-error: true 58: - name: Check dashboard files exist 59: run: | 60: if [ -d public ]; then 61: test -f public/index.html && test -f public/dashboard.css && test -f public/dashboard.js 62: echo "✅ Dashboard files verified (public/)" 63: elif [ -d frontend ]; then 64: echo "✅ Dashboard uses frontend/ build (validated separately)" 65: else 66: echo "⚠️ No dashboard directory found (skipping)" 67: fi 68: - name: Test Python pipeline integration 69: run: | 70: echo "Testing Python pipeline stages (3-7)..." 71: python3 << 'PYEOF' 72: import sys 73: sys.path.insert(0, 'sidequest/pipeline-core') 74: from models.code_block import CodeBlock, SourceLocation 75: from models.duplicate_group import DuplicateGroup 76: print('✅ Pydantic models imported successfully') 77: PYEOF 78: python3 << 'PYEOF' 79: import sys 80: sys.path.insert(0, 'sidequest/pipeline-core') 81: from similarity.structural import calculate_structural_similarity 82: score, method = calculate_structural_similarity('function a() { return 1; }', 'function b() { return 1; }') 83: assert 0 <= score <= 1, f'Invalid score: {score}' 84: print(f'✅ Structural similarity working (score: {score:.2f}, method: {method})') 85: PYEOF 86: echo "✅ Python pipeline integration verified" 87: lint: 88: name: Lint and Code Quality 89: runs-on: ubuntu-latest 90: steps: 91: - name: Checkout code 92: uses: actions/checkout@v4 93: - name: Set up pnpm 94: uses: pnpm/action-setup@v4 95: with: 96: version: 9 97: - name: Set up Node.js 98: uses: actions/setup-node@v4 99: with: 100: node-version: '24.x' 101: cache: 'pnpm' 102: - name: Install dependencies 103: run: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile 104: - name: Install ast-grep (best effort) 105: run: pnpm add -g @ast-grep/cli@latest || echo "⚠️ ast-grep installation failed (non-critical)" 106: continue-on-error: true 107: - name: TypeScript type checking 108: run: pnpm run typecheck 109: - name: Check for security vulnerabilities 110: run: pnpm audit --audit-level=high 111: continue-on-error: true 112: build-validation: 113: name: Validate Build & Static Assets 114: runs-on: ubuntu-latest 115: steps: 116: - name: Checkout code 117: uses: actions/checkout@v4 118: - name: Set up pnpm 119: uses: pnpm/action-setup@v4 120: with: 121: version: 9 122: - name: Set up Node.js 123: uses: actions/setup-node@v4 124: with: 125: node-version: '24.x' 126: cache: 'pnpm' 127: - name: Install dependencies 128: run: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile 129: - name: Install ast-grep (best effort) 130: run: pnpm add -g @ast-grep/cli@latest || echo "⚠️ ast-grep installation failed (non-critical)" 131: continue-on-error: true 132: - name: Validate static assets 133: run: | 134: if [ -d public ]; then 135: grep -q "AlephAuto Dashboard" public/index.html || (echo "❌ Dashboard title not found" && exit 1) 136: SIZE=$(stat -f%z public/dashboard.css 2>/dev/null || stat -c%s public/dashboard.css) 137: if [ $SIZE -lt 1000 ]; then 138: echo "❌ CSS file too small: $SIZE bytes" 139: exit 1 140: fi 141: SIZE=$(stat -f%z public/dashboard.js 2>/dev/null || stat -c%s public/dashboard.js) 142: if [ $SIZE -lt 5000 ]; then 143: echo "❌ JS file too small: $SIZE bytes" 144: exit 1 145: fi 146: echo "✅ Static assets validated (public/)" 147: elif [ -d frontend ]; then 148: echo "✅ Dashboard uses frontend/ (build validated via pnpm run build:frontend)" 149: else 150: echo "⚠️ No dashboard directory found (skipping static asset validation)" 151: fi 152: - name: Test API server starts 153: run: | 154: timeout 30 node --strip-types api/server.ts & 155: SERVER_PID=$! 156: echo "Waiting for server to start..." 157: for i in {1..15}; do 158: if curl -sf http://localhost:8080/health > /dev/null 2>&1; then 159: echo "✅ Server ready after ${i} seconds" 160: break 161: fi 162: if [ $i -eq 15 ]; then 163: echo "❌ Server failed to start within 15 seconds" 164: kill $SERVER_PID 2>/dev/null || true 165: exit 1 166: fi 167: sleep 1 168: done 169: curl -f http://localhost:8080/health || (echo "❌ Health check failed" && exit 1) 170: kill $SERVER_PID 2>/dev/null || true 171: echo "✅ API server validated" 172: env: 173: NODE_ENV: test 174: JOBS_API_PORT: 8080 1: name: CD - Production Deployment 2: on: 3: workflow_dispatch: 4: jobs: 5: deploy: 6: name: Deploy to Production 7: runs-on: ubuntu-latest 8: environment: production 9: steps: 10: - name: Checkout code 11: uses: actions/checkout@v4 12: - name: Set up pnpm 13: uses: pnpm/action-setup@v4 14: with: 15: version: 9 16: - name: Set up Node.js 17: uses: actions/setup-node@v4 18: with: 19: node-version: '20.x' 20: cache: 'pnpm' 21: - name: Set up Python 22: uses: actions/setup-python@v5 23: with: 24: python-version: '3.11' 25: cache: 'pip' 26: - name: Install Node.js dependencies 27: run: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile --prod 28: - name: Install Python dependencies 29: run: | 30: python -m pip install --upgrade pip 31: pip install -r requirements.txt 32: - name: Install Doppler CLI 33: run: | 34: curl -sLf --retry 3 --tlsv1.2 --proto "=https" 'https://packages.doppler.com/public/cli/gpg.DE2A7741.key' | sudo apt-key add - 35: echo "deb https://packages.doppler.com/public/cli/deb/debian any-version main" | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/doppler-cli.list 36: sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install doppler 37: - name: Install PM2 globally 38: run: pnpm add -g pm2 39: - name: Validate deployment files 40: run: | 41: echo "Validating deployment artifacts..." 42: test -f api/server.ts || test -f api/server.js || (echo "❌ API server not found" && exit 1) 43: grep -q '"dashboard"' package.json || (echo "❌ Dashboard script not found" && exit 1) 44: echo "✅ All deployment files validated" 45: - name: Configure Doppler 46: run: | 47: echo "${{ secrets.DOPPLER_TOKEN }}" | doppler configure set token --scope / --silent 48: env: 49: DOPPLER_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.DOPPLER_TOKEN }} 50: - name: Deploy with rsync 51: uses: burnett01/rsync-deployments@6.0.0 52: with: 53: switches: -avzr --delete --exclude='.git*' --exclude='node_modules' --exclude='venv' --exclude='tests' 54: path: ./ 55: remote_path: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_PATH }} 56: remote_host: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_HOST }} 57: remote_user: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_USER }} 58: remote_key: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_SSH_KEY }} 59: - name: Install dependencies on server 60: uses: appleboy/ssh-action@v1.0.0 61: with: 62: host: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_HOST }} 63: username: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_USER }} 64: key: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_SSH_KEY }} 65: script: | 66: cd ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_PATH }} 67: mkdir -p logs 68: command -v pnpm || npm install -g pnpm 69: doppler run -c prd -- pnpm install --frozen-lockfile --prod 70: python3 -m venv venv 71: source venv/bin/activate 72: pip install --upgrade pip 73: pip install -r requirements.txt 74: - name: Restart services with PM2 75: uses: appleboy/ssh-action@v1.0.0 76: with: 77: host: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_HOST }} 78: username: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_USER }} 79: key: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_SSH_KEY }} 80: script: | 81: cd ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_PATH }} 82: if pm2 list | grep -q "aleph-dashboard\|aleph-worker"; then 83: echo "Services found, restarting with config/ecosystem.config.cjs..." 84: doppler run -c prd -- pm2 restart config/ecosystem.config.cjs 85: else 86: echo "Services not found, starting with config/ecosystem.config.cjs..." 87: doppler run -c prd -- pm2 start config/ecosystem.config.cjs 88: fi 89: pm2 save 90: pm2 status 91: - name: Health check 92: uses: appleboy/ssh-action@v1.0.0 93: with: 94: host: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_HOST }} 95: username: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_USER }} 96: key: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_SSH_KEY }} 97: script: | 98: echo "Waiting for services to start..." 99: sleep 10 100: if redis-cli ping | grep -q "PONG"; then 101: echo "✅ Redis is healthy" 102: else 103: echo "⚠️ Redis not available (caching disabled)" 104: fi 105: RESPONSE=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" http://localhost:8080/health) 106: if [ "$RESPONSE" -eq 200 ]; then 107: echo "✅ Dashboard is healthy" 108: else 109: echo "❌ Dashboard health check failed (HTTP $RESPONSE)" 110: exit 1 111: fi 112: RESPONSE=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" http://localhost:8080/api/health/doppler) 113: if [ "$RESPONSE" -eq 200 ]; then 114: echo "✅ Doppler health monitor is active" 115: else 116: echo "❌ Doppler health monitor check failed (HTTP $RESPONSE)" 117: exit 1 118: fi 119: PIPELINE_COUNT=$(curl -s http://localhost:8080/api/status | jq '.pipelines | length') 120: if [ "$PIPELINE_COUNT" -ge 7 ]; then 121: echo "✅ Dashboard returns $PIPELINE_COUNT pipelines (E1 verified)" 122: else 123: echo "❌ Dashboard only returns $PIPELINE_COUNT pipelines, expected >= 7" 124: exit 1 125: fi 126: REPOMIX_RESPONSE=$(curl -s 'http://localhost:8080/api/sidequest/pipeline-runners/repomix/jobs?limit=10') 127: REPOMIX_STATUS=$(echo $REPOMIX_RESPONSE | jq -r '.total // "skip"') 128: if [ "$REPOMIX_STATUS" != "skip" ] && [ "$REPOMIX_STATUS" != "null" ]; then 129: echo "✅ Pagination endpoint working (E2 verified)" 130: else 131: echo "⚠️ Pagination endpoint returned no data (may be normal for new deployment)" 132: fi 133: - name: Notify deployment success 134: if: success() 135: run: | 136: echo "🚀 Deployment successful!" 137: echo "📊 Dashboard: http://${{ secrets.DEPLOY_HOST }}:8080/" 138: echo "❤️ Health: http://${{ secrets.DEPLOY_HOST }}:8080/health" 139: - name: Rollback on failure 140: if: failure() 141: uses: appleboy/ssh-action@v1.0.0 142: with: 143: host: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_HOST }} 144: username: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_USER }} 145: key: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_SSH_KEY }} 146: script: | 147: cd ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_PATH }} 148: echo "⚠️ Deployment failed, attempting rollback..." 149: pm2 resurrect 150: echo "Rollback complete. Please check logs with: pm2 logs" 1: name: Deploy to GitHub Pages 2: on: 3: push: 4: branches: [main] 5: paths: 6: - 'public/**' 7: - '.github/workflows/pages.yml' 8: workflow_dispatch: 9: permissions: 10: contents: read 11: pages: write 12: id-token: write 13: concurrency: 14: group: "pages" 15: cancel-in-progress: false 16: jobs: 17: build: 18: name: Build Dashboard 19: runs-on: ubuntu-latest 20: steps: 21: - 17: 18: // Project root is one level up from config/ 19: const PROJECT_ROOT = path.resolve(__dirname, '..'); 20: const LOGS_DIR = path.join(PROJECT_ROOT, 'logs'); 21: 22: module.exports = { 23: apps: [ 24: /** 25: * Dashboard & API Server 26: * Runs in fork mode (single instance) to prevent port conflicts 27: * Note: Cluster mode disabled due to EADDRINUSE errors with WebSocket server 28: */ 29: { 30: name: 'aleph-dashboard', 31: script: 'api/server.ts', 32: cwd: PROJECT_ROOT, 33: instances: 1, // CHANGED: Single instance to prevent port conflicts (was 2) 34: exec_mode: 'fork', // CHANGED: Fork 47: REDIS_PORT: process.env.REDIS_PORT || '6379', 48: SENTRY_DSN: process.env.SENTRY_DSN || '', 49: SENTRY_ENVIRONMENT: process.env.SENTRY_ENVIRONMENT || 'production', 50: // CRITICAL: Include Homebrew paths for npx/node availability in child processes 51: // This prevents "spawn npx ENOENT" errors in workers (E4 bugfix 2025-11-25) 52: PATH: process.env.PATH || '/opt/homebrew/bin:/opt/homebrew/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/bin:/bin:/usr/sbin:/sbin' 53: }, 54: 55: // Logging (max_size triggers pm2-logrotate) 56: error_file: path.join(LOGS_DIR, Use node interpreter explicitly to prevent "fork/exec permission denied" errors 63: // This tells PM2 to run: node api/server.ts 64: // NOT: ./api/server.ts (which would require shebang + executable permissions) 65: // Environment variables from process.env, set by doppler run 66: interpreter: 'node', 67: 68: // Restart behavior - ENHANCED to prevent restart loops 69: min_uptime: '30s', // CHANGED: Process must stay up 30s to be considered stable (was 10s) 70: max_restarts: 5, // CHANGED: Reduced from 10 to prevent infinite loops 71: restart_delay: 8000, // CHANGED: Increased from 4s to 8s to allow port cleanup 72: exp_backoff_restart_delay: 100, // NEW: Enable exponential backoff on restarts 73: 74: // Monitoring - ENHANCED for better error detection 75: listen_timeout: 10000, // CHANGED: Increased from 3s to 10s for slower startups 76: kill_timeout: 10000, // CHANGED: Increased from 5s to 10s for graceful shutdown 77: 78: // NEW: Stop restarting if it crashes within min_uptime 3 times in a row 79: stop_exit_codes: [0], // Only stop on clean exit (code 0) 80: 81: // NEW: Throttle restarts to prevent rapid crash loops 82: vizion: false // Disable git integration Error('Failed to cancel job'); 67: } 68: } catch (error) { 69: console.error('[App] Failed to cancel job:', error); 70: alert(`Failed to cancel job: ${error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'Unknown apiService.retryJob(jobId); 77: if (result.success) { 78: console.log('[App] Job retried successfully:', result); 79: updateJob(jobId, { status: 'queued' as any, retryCount: (activeJobs.find(j => j.id === jobId)?.retryCount ?? 0) + 1 }); 80: } else { 81: throw new Error('Failed to retry job'); 82: } 83: } catch (error) { 84: console.error('[App] Failed to retry job:', error); 85: alert(`Failed to setSelectedPipelineDetail(activityItem.pipelineId); 100: } 101: }, [activity, selectPipeline]); 102: const handleActivityClear = useCallback(() => { 103: console.log('[App] Clear activity'); 104: markActivityRead(); 105: clearActivity(); 106: }, [markActivityRead, clearActivity]); 107: const handleRetry = useCallback(() => { 108: console.log('[App] Retrying connection...'); 109: tailwindcss: {}, 4: autoprefixer: {}, 5: }, 6: } 1: # AlephAuto Dashboard Frontend 2: 3: Real-time monitoring dashboard for the AlephAuto job queue framework. 4: 5: ## Tech Stack 6: 7: - **React 18** - UI framework 8: - **TypeScript** - Type safety 9: - **Vite** - Build tool and dev server 10: - **Tailwind CSS** - Utility-first CSS framework 11: - **Zustand** - State management 12: - **Socket.IO Client** - Real-time WebSocket communication 13: - **Axios** - HTTP client for REST API 14: - **Recharts** - Data visualization 15: - **Lucide React** - Icon library 16: 17: ## Project Structure 18: 19: ``` 20: frontend/ 21: ├── src/ 22: │ ├── components/ # React components 23: │ ├── hooks/ # Custom React hooks 24: │ ├── services/ # API and WebSocket services 25: │ ├── store/ # Zustand state management 26: │ ├── types/ # TypeScript type definitions 27: │ ├── App.tsx # Root component 28: │ ├── main.tsx # Entry point 29: │ └── index.css # Global styles (Tailwind) 30: ├── public/ # Static assets 31: ├── index.html # HTML entry point 32: ├── vite.config.ts # Vite configuration 33: ├── tsconfig.json # TypeScript configuration 34: ├── tailwind.config.js # Tailwind CSS configuration 35: └── package.json # Dependencies and scripts 36: ``` 37: 38: ## Getting Started 39: 40: ### Prerequisites 41: 42: - Node.js >= 18.0.0 43: - npm or yarn 44: 45: ### Installation 46: 47: ```bash 48: cd frontend 49: npm install 50: ``` 51: 52: ### Development 53: 54: Start the development server: 55: 56: ```bash 57: npm run dev 58: ``` 59: 60: The dashboard will be available at `http://localhost:5173` 61: 62: The Vite dev server is configured to proxy API requests: 63: - `/api/*` → `http://localhost:8080` 64: - `/ws` (WebSocket) → `ws://localhost:8080` 65: 66: ### Build 67: 68: Create a production build: 69: 70: ```bash 71: npm run build 72: ``` 73: 74: Preview the production build: 75: 76: ```bash 77: npm run preview 78: ``` 79: 80: ### Type Checking 81: 82: Run TypeScript type checking without emitting files: 83: 84: ```bash 85: npm run typecheck 86: ``` 87: 88: ## Environment Variables 89: 90: Create a `.env.local` file for local development (optional): 91: 92: ```env 93: VITE_API_URL=http://localhost:8080 94: VITE_WS_URL=ws://localhost:8080 95: ``` 96: 97: ## Implementation Status 98: 99: ### ✅ Completed 100: 101: - [x] Project setup and configuration 102: - [x] Dependencies installation 103: - [x] TypeScript configuration 104: - [x] Tailwind CSS configuration 105: - [x] Vite build configuration 106: - [x] Type definitions (Pipeline, Job, Activity, SystemStatus) 107: - [x] Basic project structure 108: 109: ### 🔄 In Progress 110: 111: - [ ] Zustand store implementation 112: - [ ] WebSocket service 113: - [ ] API service layer 114: - [ ] Core components (Header, Pipeline, JobQueue, Activity) 115: - [ ] Real-time data flow 116: 117: ### 📋 Planned 118: 119: - [ ] Documentation viewer component 120: - [ ] Responsive design testing 121: - [ ] Accessibility audit (WCAG AA) 122: - [ ] Performance optimization 123: - [ ] Production deployment 124: 125: ## Next Steps 126: 127: Following the implementation guide in `docs/DASHBOARD_IMPLEMENTATION_GUIDE.md`: 128: 129: 1. **Implement Zustand Store** (`src/store/dashboard.ts`) 130: - Pipeline state management 131: - Job queue state 132: - Activity feed state 133: - System status state 134: 135: 2. **Create WebSocket Service** (`src/services/websocket.ts`) 136: - Connection management with reconnection logic 137: - Event handlers for job lifecycle 138: - System status updates 139: 140: 3. **Build API Service** (`src/services/api.ts`) 141: - REST endpoints for pipelines, jobs, scans 142: - Error handling and retries 143: - Type-safe responses 144: 145: 4. **Create Core Components** 146: - Layout component (grid structure) 147: - Header (system status, settings) 148: - PipelineStatus (pipeline cards with progress) 149: - JobQueue (active and queued jobs) 150: - RecentActivity (activity feed) 151: 152: 5. **Connect Real-Time Updates** 153: - WebSocket integration hook 154: - Polling fallback for connection issues 155: - State synchronization 156: 157: ## Documentation 158: 159: Full design and implementation documentation available in: 160: - `docs/DASHBOARD_SUMMARY.md` - Executive overview 161: - `docs/DASHBOARD_UI_DESIGN.md` - Complete UI specification 162: - `docs/DASHBOARD_IMPLEMENTATION_GUIDE.md` - Technical implementation guide 163: - `docs/DASHBOARD_VISUAL_MOCKUPS.md` - Design tokens and mockups 164: - `docs/DASHBOARD_QUICK_REFERENCE.md` - Developer cheat sheet 165: 166: ## API Integration 167: 168: The dashboard connects to the AlephAuto backend API running on port 8080: 169: 170: **Health Check** 171: - `GET /health` - System health status 172: 173: **Pipelines** 174: - `GET /api/pipelines` - List all pipelines 175: - `GET /api/pipelines/:id` - Get pipeline details 176: 177: **Jobs** 178: - `GET /api/jobs?status=running` - Get active jobs 179: - `GET /api/jobs?status=queued` - Get queued jobs 180: - `GET /api/jobs/:id` - Get job details 181: - `GET /api/jobs/:id/logs` - Get job logs 182: - `POST /api/jobs/:id/cancel` - Cancel a job 183: - `POST /api/jobs/:id/retry` - Retry a failed job 184: 185: **Scans** 186: - `POST /api/scan` - Trigger a new scan 187: - `GET /api/scan/:id` - Get scan results 188: 189: ## Contributing 190: 191: When adding new features: 192: 1. 36: } catch (error) { 37: return { 38: path: filePath, 39: fullPath, 40: error: error.message, 41: isValid: false, 42: }; 43: } 44: } 45: async function fixFilePermissions(filePath) { 46: const fullPath = path.join(ROOT, filePath); 47: try { 48: await fs.chmod(fullPath, 0o644); 49: return { success: true, path: filePath }; 50: } catch (error) { 51: return { success: false, path: filePath, error: error.message }; 52: } 53: } 54: function reportInvalidFiles(invalid: Awaited<ReturnType<typeof checkFilePermissions>>[]) { 55: console.log(`⚠️ Found ${invalid.length} files with incorrect permissions:\n`); 69: const fixes = await Promise.all(executable.map(r => fixFilePermissions(r.path))); 70: const successful = fixes.filter(f => f.success); 71: const failed = fixes.filter(f => !f.success); 72: if (successful.length > 0) { 73: console.log(`✅ Fixed ${successful.length} files:`); class SemanticAnnotator: 220: def __init__(self, collect_timing: bool = False) -> None: 221: self.collect_timing = collect_timing 222: self.timing: dict[str, TimingMetrics] = {} 223: if collect_timing: 224: try: 225: from ..utils.timing import TimingMetrics as TM 226: except ImportError: 227: from utils.timing import TimingMetrics as TM 228: self.timing = { 229: "operations": TM("operations"), 230: "domains": TM("domains"), 231: "patterns": TM("patterns"), 232: "data_types": TM("data_types"), 233: "intent": TM("intent"), 234: } 235: def get_timing_report(self) -> dict[str, dict]: 236: return {name: m.to_dict() for name, m in self.timing.items() if m.count > 0} 237: def extract_annotation(self, block: CodeBlock) -> SemanticAnnotation: 238: code = block.source_code 239: tags = block.tags if hasattr(block, "tags") else [] 240: category = block.category if hasattr(block, "category") else "unknown" 241: if hasattr(category, "value"): 242: category = category.value 243: if self.collect_timing: 244: try: 245: from ..utils.timing import Timer 246: except ImportError: 247: from utils.timing import Timer 248: with Timer(self.timing["operations"]): 249: operations = self._extract_operations(code) 250: with Timer(self.timing["domains"]): 251: domains = self._extract_domains(code, tags) 252: with Timer(self.timing["patterns"]): 253: patterns = self._extract_patterns(code) 254: with Timer(self.timing["data_types"]): 255: data_types = self._extract_data_types(code) 256: with Timer(self.timing["intent"]): 257: intent = self._infer_intent(operations, domains, patterns) 258: else: 259: operations = self._extract_operations(code) 260: domains = self._extract_domains(code, tags) 261: patterns = self._extract_patterns(code) 262: data_types = self._extract_data_types(code) 263: intent = self._infer_intent(operations, domains, patterns) 264: return SemanticAnnotation( 265: category=category, 266: operations=operations, 267: domains=domains, 268: patterns=patterns, 269: data_types=data_types, 270: intent=intent, 271: ) 272: def _extract_operations(self, code: str) -> set[str]: 273: operations: set[str] = set() 274: for compiled_pattern, op in _COMPILED_ALL_OPERATIONS: 275: if compiled_pattern.search(code): 276: operations.add(op) 277: return operations 278: def _extract_domains(self, code: str, tags: list[str]) -> set[str]: 279: domains: set[str] = set() 280: text = code + " " + " ".join(tags) 281: for compiled_pattern, domain in _COMPILED_DOMAIN: 282: if compiled_pattern.search(text): 283: domains.add(domain) 284: return domains 285: def _extract_patterns(self, code: str) -> set[str]: 286: patterns: set[str] = set() 287: for compiled_pattern, name in _COMPILED_CODE_PATTERNS: 288: if compiled_pattern.search(code): 289: patterns.add(name) 290: return patterns 291: def _extract_data_types(self, code: str) -> set[str]: 292: data_types: set[str] = set() 293: for compiled_pattern, dtype in _COMPILED_DATA_TYPES: 294: if compiled_pattern.search(code): 295: data_types.add(dtype) 296: return data_types 297: def _infer_intent( 298: self, 299: operations: set[str], 300: domains: set[str], 301: patterns: set[str], 302: ) -> str: 303: parts: list[str] = [] 304: if operations: 305: parts.append("+".join(sorted(operations))) 306: if domains: 307: parts.append("on:" + "+".join(sorted(domains))) 308: if patterns: 309: parts.append("with:" + "+".join(sorted(patterns))) 310: return "|".join(parts) if parts else "unknown" 1: """Type stubs for block using function-based deduplication. 241: Returns True if block was processed (added or skipped as duplicate). 242: """ 243: function_key = f"{block.location.file_path}:{function_name}" 244: if function_key not in seen_functions: 245: seen_functions[function_key] = block 246: unique_blocks.append(block) 247: return True 248: # Already seen this function - keep the earlier occurrence 249: existing_block = seen_functions[function_key] 250: if block.location.line_start < existing_block.location.line_start: 251: unique_blocks.remove(existing_block) 252: seen_functions[function_key] = block 253: unique_blocks.append(block) 254: else: 255: _debug( 256: f"dedup: skipping duplicate {function_name} at line {block.location.line_start} (kept line {existing_block.location.line_start})" 257: ) 258: return True 259: def _try_add_by_location( 260: block: CodeBlock, 261: seen_locations: set[str], 262: unique_blocks: list[CodeBlock], 263: ) -> None: 264: """Add block using location-based deduplication.""" 265: location_key = f"{block.location.file_path}:{block.location.line_start}" 266: if location_key not in seen_locations: 267: seen_locations.add(location_key) 268: unique_blocks.append(block) 269: def deduplicate_blocks(blocks: list[CodeBlock]) -> list[CodeBlock]: 270: """Remove duplicate code blocks from the same location and function. 271: Deduplicate Pattern Matches (priority four) 272: ast-grep patterns can match the same code multiple times within a function. 273: This removes duplicates based on file:function_name, keeping only the earliest match. 274: """ 275: seen_locations: set[str] = set() 276: seen_functions: dict[str, CodeBlock] = {} 277: unique_blocks: list[CodeBlock] = [] 278: for i, block in enumerate(blocks): 279: function_name = _get_function_name_from_tags(block.tags) 280: if i < BlockExtraction.DEBUG_LOG_DEDUP_LIMIT: 281: _debug( 282: f"dedup block {i}: {block.location.file_path}:{block.location.line_start}, func={function_name}" 283: ) 284: if function_name: 285: _try_add_by_function(block, function_name, seen_functions, unique_blocks) 286: else: 287: _try_add_by_location(block, seen_locations, unique_blocks) 288: removed = len(blocks) - len(unique_blocks) 289: if removed > 0: 290: print( 291: f"Deduplication: Removed {removed} duplicate blocks ({len(seen_functions)} unique functions, {len(seen_locations)} unique locations)", 292: file=sys.stderr, 293: ) 294: return unique_blocks 295: def _create_code_block(match: Dict, repository_info: Dict) -> CodeBlock: 296: """Create a CodeBlock from a pattern match (H4 fix: extracted helper).""" 297: # Generate unique block ID 298: block_key = f"{match['file_path']}:{match['line_start']}" 299: block_hash = hashlib.sha256(block_key.encode()).hexdigest()[ 300: : BlockExtraction.BLOCK_HASH_LENGTH 301: ] 302: block_id = f"cb_{block_hash}" 303: # Map pattern_id to category 304: category = PATTERN_CATEGORY_MAP.get(match["rule_id"], "utility") 305: source_code = match.get("matched_text", "") 306: function_name = extract_function_name( 307: source_code, 308: file_path=match["file_path"], 309: line_start=match["line_start"], 310: repo_path=repository_info["path"], 311: ) 312: return CodeBlock( 313: block_id=block_id, 314: pattern_id=match["rule_id"], 315: location=SourceLocation( 316: file_path=match["file_path"], 317: line_start=match["line_start"], 318: line_end=match.get("line_end", match["line_start"]), 319: ), 320: relative_path=match["file_path"], 321: source_code=source_code, 322: language=detect_language(match["file_path"]), 323: category=category, 324: repository_path=repository_info["path"], 325: line_count=match.get("line_end", match["line_start"]) - match["line_start"] + 1, 326: tags=[f"{BlockExtraction.FUNCTION_TAG_PREFIX}{function_name}"] 327: if function_name 328: else [], 329: ) 330: def extract_code_blocks( 331: pattern_matches: List[Dict], repository_info: Dict 332: ) -> List[CodeBlock]: 333: """Extract CodeBlock models from pattern matches.""" 334: _debug(f"extract_code_blocks: repository_info={repository_info}") 335: _debug(f"extract_code_blocks: got {len(pattern_matches)} pattern matches") 336: blocks = [] 337: for i, match in enumerate(pattern_matches): 338: if i == 0: 339: _debug( 340: f"first match: file_path={match.get('file_path')}, line_start={match.get('line_start')}" 341: ) 342: try: 343: block = _create_code_block(match, repository_info) 344: if i < BlockExtraction.DEBUG_LOG_BLOCK_LIMIT: 345: _debug( 346: f"block created: file={block.relative_path}, line={block.location.line_start}, tags={block.tags}" 347: ) 348: blocks.append(block) 349: except Exception as e: 350: print( 351: f"Warning: Failed to extract block {i} from {match.get('file_path', 'unknown')}: {e}", 352: file=sys.stderr, 353: ) 354: import traceback 355: traceback.print_exc(file=sys.stderr) 356: continue 357: return blocks 358: def group_duplicates(blocks: List[CodeBlock]) -> List[DuplicateGroup]: 359: """ 360: Group similar code blocks using layer (AST-based) 365: - Semantic equivalence layer (category + tags) 366: """ 367: # Use the multi-layer grouping algorithm 368: groups = group_by_similarity( 369: blocks, if this is a breaking change 387: breaking_changes = _is_breaking_change(group, strategy) 388: suggestion = ConsolidationSuggestion( 389: suggestion_id=f"cs_{group.group_id}", 390: code_example=code_example, 396: impact_score=min(group.impact_score, ROIMultipliers.MAX_SCORE), 397: complexity=complexity, 398: migration_risk=risk, 399: 551: "minimal", 552: ), 553: StrategyRule( 554: StrategyThresholds.DEFAULT_SHARED_MAX, 555: "shared_package", 556: "Utility pattern used {occ} times across {files} files - create shared utility", 557: 567: ] 568: def _apply_strategy_rules( 569: rules: list[StrategyRule], 570: occurrences: int, 571: files: int, 572: ) -> tuple[str, str, str, str]: 573: for rule in rules: 574: if rule.max_occurrences is None or occurrences <= rule.max_occurrences: 575: rationale = rule.rationale_template.format(occ=occurrences, files=files) 576: return (rule.strategy, rationale, rule.complexity, rule.risk) 577: last_rule = rules[-1] 578: return ( 579: last_rule.strategy, 580: last_rule.rationale_template.format(occ=occurrences, files=files), 581: last_rule.complexity, 582: last_rule.risk, 583: ) 584: def == 1: 589: return ( 590: "local_util", 591: f"All {occurrences} occurrences in same file - extract to local function", 592: "trivial", 593: "minimal", 594: ) 595: rules = behavior", True, "10min"), 607: ] 608: elif strategy == "shared_package": 609: steps = [ 610: ("Create shared package/module for utility", False, "1h"), 611: ("Extract and parameterize common logic", "mcp_server": 619: steps = [ 620: ("Design MCP tool interface for functionality", False, "2h"), 621: ("Create MCP server with tool implementation", False, "4h"), 622: ("Add MCP tool schema and documentation", False, "1h"), 623: ("Test MCP tool independently", False, "1h"), 624: ("Update projects to use MCP client", False, "2h"), 625: ("Replace duplicates with MCP tool calls", True, "1h"), 626: ("Add integration tests", False, "2h"), 627: ("Document MCP tool usage", False, "1h"), 628: ] 629: else: 630: steps = [ 631: ("Define agent capabilities and workflow", False, "3h"), 632: ("Design agent prompt and tool access", False, "2h"), 633: ("Implement agent logic and orchestration", False, "8h"), 634: ("Create agent tests and safety checks", False, "3h"), 635: ("Integrate agent with existing systems", False, "4h"), 636: ("Replace complex duplicate logic with agent calls", False, "2h"), 637: ("Monitor agent performance and behavior", False, "ongoing"), 638: ("Document agent usage and limitations", False, "2h"), 639: ] 640: return [ 641: MigrationStep( 642: step_number=i + 1, 643: description=desc, 644: automated=automated, 645: estimated_time=time, 646: ) 647: for i, (desc, "shared_package": 670: return """// Before: Duplicated across files 671: // file1.js: { check logic } 672: // file2.js: { check logic } 673: // After: Shared package 674: import { validateInput } from ROIMultipliers.COMPLEXITY_SIMPLE, 689: "moderate": ROIMultipliers.COMPLEXITY_MODERATE, 690: "complex": ROIMultipliers.COMPLEXITY_COMPLEX, 691: } 692: roi *= complexity_multipliers.get(complexity, 1.0) 693: risk_multipliers = { 694: "minimal": ROIMultipliers.RISK_MINIMAL, 695: "low": ROIMultipliers.RISK_LOW, 696: "medium": ROIMultipliers.RISK_MEDIUM, 697: "high": ROIMultipliers.RISK_HIGH, 698: } 699: roi *= risk_multipliers.get(risk, 1.0) 700: return min(roi, ROIMultipliers.MAX_SCORE) 701: def _is_breaking_change(group: DuplicateGroup, strategy: str) -> bool: 702: if strategy == "local_util": 703: return False 704: if strategy == "shared_package": 705: return group.category in ["api_handler", "auth_check"] 706: return True 707: def _suggest_target_location(group: DuplicateGroup, strategy: str) -> str: 708: if strategy == "local_util": 709: first_file = group.affected_files[0] if group.affected_files else "" 710: if "/" in first_file: 711: dir_path = "/".join(first_file.split("/")[:-1]) 712: return f"{dir_path}/utils.js" 713: return "utils.js" 714: elif strategy == "shared_package": 715: category = group.category 716: if category == "logger": 717: return "complex": EffortHours.COMPLEX, 737: } 738: hours = base_hours.get(complexity, EffortHours.DEFAULT) 739: hours += len(group.affected_files) * EffortHours.PER_FILE_INCREMENT 740: hours += 746: total_repo_lines: int = 0, 747: ) -> Dict[str, Any]: 748: exact_groups = [g for g in groups if g.similarity_method == "exact_match"] 749: structural_groups = [g for g in groups if g.similarity_method == "structural"] 750: semantic_groups = [g for g in groups if g.similarity_method == "semantic"] 751: total_duplicated_lines = sum(g.total_lines for g in groups) 752: potential_loc_reduction = sum( 753: g.total_lines - (g.total_lines // g.occurrence_count) for g in groups 754: ) 755: if total_repo_lines <= 0: 756: total_repo_lines = sum(b.line_count for b in blocks) 757: duplication_percentage = ( 758: (total_duplicated_lines / total_repo_lines * 100) 759: if total_repo_lines > 0 760: else 0.0 761: ) 762: quick_wins = [ 763: g 764: for g in groups 765: if g.occurrence_count <= ExtractionDefaults.QUICK_WIN_MAX_OCCURRENCES 766: and len(g.affected_files) == 1 767: ] 768: high_impact = [ 769: g 770: for g in groups 771: if g.total_lines >= ExtractionDefaults.HIGH_IMPACT_MIN_LINES 772: or g.occurrence_count >= ExtractionDefaults.HIGH_IMPACT_MIN_OCCURRENCES 773: ] 774: blocks_with_tags = sum(1 for b in blocks if b.tags) 775: total_tags = sum(len(b.tags) for b in blocks) 776: blocks_with_tags_percentage = ( 777: round(blocks_with_tags / len(blocks) * 100, 2) if blocks else 0.0 778: ) 779: avg_tags_per_block = round(total_tags / len(blocks), 2) if blocks else 0.0 780: return { 781: "total_code_blocks": len(blocks), 782: "total_duplicate_groups": len(groups), 783: "exact_duplicates": len(exact_groups), 784: "structural_duplicates": len(structural_groups), 785: "semantic_duplicates": len(semantic_groups), 786: "total_duplicated_lines": total_duplicated_lines, 787: "potential_loc_reduction": potential_loc_reduction, 788: "duplication_percentage": round(duplication_percentage, 2), 789: "total_suggestions": len(suggestions), 790: "quick_wins": len(quick_wins), 791: "high_impact_suggestions": len(high_impact), 792: "trivial_suggestions": len( 793: [s for s in suggestions if s.complexity == "trivial"] 794: ), 795: "simple_suggestions": len([s for s in suggestions if s.complexity == "simple"]), 796: "moderate_suggestions": len( 797: [s for s in suggestions if s.complexity == "moderate"] 798: ), 799: "complex_suggestions": len( 800: [s for s in suggestions if s.complexity == "complex"] 801: ), 802: "high_priority_suggestions": len( 803: [s for s in suggestions if s.impact_score >= ScoringThresholds.CRITICAL] 804: ), 805: "blocks_with_tags": blocks_with_tags, 806: "blocks_with_tags_percentage": blocks_with_tags_percentage, 807: "avg_tags_per_block": avg_tags_per_block, 808: } 809: def main(): 810: try: 811: raw_input = "complexity": "trivial", 130: "migration_risk": "low", 131: "breaking_changes": False, 132: "target_location": "src/utils/json.js", 133: "target_name": "writeJsonFile", 134: "loc_reduction": 15, 135: "affected_files_count": 5, 136: "affected_repositories_count": 1, 137: } 138: } 139: } 140: @field_validator("impact_score") 141: @classmethod 142: def round_impact_score(cls, v): 143: return round(v, 2) 144: @computed_field 145: @property 146: def priority(self) -> str: 147: if self.impact_score >= ScoringThresholds.CRITICAL and self.complexity in [ 148: "trivial", 149: "simple", 150: ]: 151: return "critical" 152: elif self.impact_score >= ScoringThresholds.HIGH and self.complexity in [ 153: "trivial", 154: "simple", 155: "moderate", 156: ]: 157: return "high" 158: elif self.impact_score >= ScoringThresholds.MEDIUM: 159: return "medium" 160: else: 161: return "low" 162: @computed_field 163: @property 164: def roi_score(self) -> float: 165: complexity_hours = { 166: ImplementationComplexity.TRIVIAL: EffortEstimates.TRIVIAL, 167: ImplementationComplexity.SIMPLE: EffortEstimates.SIMPLE, 168: ImplementationComplexity.MODERATE: EffortEstimates.MODERATE, 169: ImplementationComplexity.COMPLEX: EffortEstimates.COMPLEX, 170: ImplementationComplexity.VERY_COMPLEX: EffortEstimates.VERY_COMPLEX, 171: } 172: effort = self.estimated_effort_hours or complexity_hours.get( 173: self.complexity, SuggestionDefaults.DEFAULT_EFFORT_FALLBACK 174: ) 175: if effort == 0: 176: return ROIMultipliers.MAX_SCORE 177: roi = (self.impact_score / effort) * SuggestionDefaults.ROI_NORMALIZER 178: return min(round(roi, 2), ROIMultipliers.MAX_SCORE) 179: @computed_field 180: @property 181: def is_quick_win(self) -> bool: 182: return ( 183: self.impact_score >= SuggestionDefaults.QUICK_WIN_MIN_IMPACT 184: and self.complexity 185: in [ImplementationComplexity.TRIVIAL, ImplementationComplexity.SIMPLE] 186: and self.migration_risk in [MigrationRisk.MINIMAL, MigrationRisk.LOW] 187: ) 188: def add_migration_step( 189: self, 190: description: str, 191: code_example: Optional[str] = None, 192: automated: bool = False, 193: estimated_time: Optional[str] = None, 194: ) -> None: 195: step_number = len(self.migration_steps) + 1 196: step = MigrationStep( 197: step_number=step_number, 198: description=description, 199: code_example=code_example, 200: automated=automated, 201: estimated_time=estimated_time, 202: ) 203: self.migration_steps.append(step) 204: def add_benefit(self, benefit: str) -> None: 205: if benefit not in self.benefits: 206: self.benefits.append(benefit) 207: def add_drawback(self, drawback: str) -> None: 208: if drawback not in self.drawbacks: 209: self.drawbacks.append(drawback) 210: def to_markdown_summary(self) -> str: 211: return f"""## {self.target_name or "Consolidation Suggestion"} 212: **Strategy:** {self.strategy.value.replace("_", " ").title()} 213: **Priority:** {self.priority.upper()} 214: **Impact Score:** {self.impact_score}/100 215: **ROI Score:** {self.roi_score}/100 216: ### Rationale 217: {self.strategy_rationale} 218: ### Metrics 219: - **Complexity:** {self.complexity.value} 220: - **Risk:** {self.migration_risk.value} 221: - **LOC Reduction:** {self.loc_reduction or "Unknown"} 222: - **Files Affected:** {self.affected_files_count} 223: - **Breaking Changes:** {"Yes" if self.breaking_changes else "No"} 224: ### Benefits 225: {chr(10).join(f"- {b}" for b in self.benefits) if self.benefits else "None specified"} 226: ### Migration Steps 227: {chr(10).join(f"{i + 1}. {step.description}" for i, step in enumerate(self.migration_steps)) if self.migration_steps else "Not specified"} 228: """ 229: def __hash__(self) -> int: 230: return hash(self.suggestion_id) 231: def __eq__(self, other: object) -> bool: 232: if not isinstance(other, ConsolidationSuggestion): 233: return NotImplemented 234: return self.suggestion_id == other.suggestion_id 1: """Type stubs for total_lines: int 26: affected_files: List[str] 27: affected_repositories: List[str] 28: consolidation_complexity: Optional[str] 29: breaking_changes_risk: Optional[str] 30: created_at: datetime 31: updated_at: datetime 32: notes: Optional[str] 33: metadata: Dict[str, Any] 34: 35: @property 36: def deduplication_potential(self) -> int: ... 37: @property 38: def impact_score(self) -> float: ... 39: @property 40: def is_cross_repository(self) -> bool: ... 41: @property 42: def priority_level(self) -> str: ... 43: def add_member(self, block_id: str) -> None: ... 44: def remove_member(self, block_id: str) -> None: ... 45: def set_canonical(self, block_id: str) -> None: ... 46: def __hash__(self) -> int: ... 47: def __eq__(self, other: object) -> bool: ... 1: # Pydantic Models for Duplicate Detection 2: 3: Data models for structuring duplicate detection and consolidation results using Pydantic v2. 4: 5: ## Overview 6: 7: These models define the data structures used throughout the duplicate detection pipeline, from individual code blocks to complete scan reports with consolidation recommendations. 8: 9: ## Model Hierarchy 10: 11: ``` 12: ScanReport (top-level) 13: ├── RepositoryInfo[] # Scanned repositories 14: ├── ScanConfiguration # Scan settings 15: ├── ScanMetrics # Statistical summary 16: ├── code_block_ids[] # → CodeBlock references 17: ├── duplicate_group_ids[] # → DuplicateGroup references 18: └── suggestion_ids[] # → ConsolidationSuggestion references 19: 20: CodeBlock # Individual code pattern 21: ├── SourceLocation # File + line location 22: ├── ASTNode # AST tree structure 23: ├── LanguageType # Programming language enum 24: └── SemanticCategory # Pattern category enum 25: 26: DuplicateGroup # Similar code blocks 27: ├── member_block_ids[] # CodeBlock references 28: ├── SimilarityMethod # How similarity was determined 29: └── computed: impact_score, priority_level 30: 31: ConsolidationSuggestion # Refactoring recommendation 32: ├── ConsolidationStrategy # Tier: local_util → autonomous_agent 33: ├── ImplementationComplexity 34: ├── MigrationRisk 35: ├── MigrationStep[] # Step-by-step guide 36: └── computed: priority, roi_score, is_quick_win 37: ``` 38: 39: ## Models 40: 41: ### CodeBlock 42: 43: Represents a code pattern detected by ast-grep. 44: 45: | Field | Type | Description | 46: |-------|------|-------------| 47: | `block_id` | str | Unique identifier | 48: | `pattern_id` | str | ast-grep rule ID | 49: | `location` | SourceLocation | File path + line numbers | 50: | `source_code` | str | Raw source code | 51: | `language` | LanguageType | javascript, typescript, python, etc. | 52: | `category` | SemanticCategory | utility, api_handler, database_operation, etc. | 53: | `content_hash` | computed | SHA-256 hash for exact matching | 54: | `structural_hash` | computed | AST-based hash for structural matching | 55: 56: ### DuplicateGroup 57: 58: Groups similar CodeBlocks as consolidation candidates. 59: 60: | Field | Type | Description | 61: |-------|------|-------------| 62: | `group_id` | str | Unique identifier | 63: | `member_block_ids` | List[str] | CodeBlock IDs (min 2) | 64: | `similarity_score` | float | 0.0-1.0 similarity | 65: | `similarity_method` | SimilarityMethod | exact_match, structural, semantic, hybrid | 66: | `occurrence_count` | int | Number of instances | 67: | `deduplication_potential` | computed | Lines removable | 68: | `impact_score` | computed | 0-100 consolidation priority | 69: | `priority_level` | computed | critical, high, medium, low | 70: 71: ### ConsolidationSuggestion 72: 73: Actionable recommendation for consolidating duplicates. 74: 75: | Field | Type | Description | 76: |-------|------|-------------| 77: | `suggestion_id` | str | Unique identifier | 78: | `duplicate_group_id` | str | Target DuplicateGroup | 79: | `strategy` | ConsolidationStrategy | local_util, shared_package, mcp_server, autonomous_agent | 80: | `complexity` | ImplementationComplexity | trivial, simple, moderate, complex, very_complex | 81: | `migration_risk` | MigrationRisk | minimal, low, medium, high, critical | 82: | `migration_steps` | List[MigrationStep] | Step-by-step implementation guide | 83: | `priority` | computed | Based on impact + complexity | 84: | `roi_score` | computed | Return on investment (0-100) | 85: | `is_quick_win` | computed | High impact + low effort + low risk | 86: 87: ### ScanReport 88: 89: Top-level report containing all scan results. 90: 91: | Field | Type | Description | 92: |-------|------|-------------| 93: | `report_id` | str | Unique identifier | 94: | `repositories` | List[RepositoryInfo] | Scanned repos | 95: | `configuration` | ScanConfiguration | Rules and thresholds used | 96: | `metrics` | ScanMetrics | Statistical summary | 97: | `duplication_severity` | computed | minimal, low, moderate, high, critical | 98: | `consolidation_opportunity_score` | computed | 0-100 benefit score | 99: 100: ## Enums 101: 102: ### LanguageType 103: ```python 104: JAVASCRIPT, TYPESCRIPT, PYTHON, JAVA, GO, RUST, C, CPP, CSHARP, PHP, RUBY 105: ``` 106: 107: ### SemanticCategory 108: ```python 109: UTILITY, HELPER, VALIDATOR, API_HANDLER, AUTH_CHECK, DATABASE_OPERATION, 110: ERROR_HANDLER, LOGGER, CONFIG_ACCESS, FILE_OPERATION, ASYNC_PATTERN, UNKNOWN 111: ``` 112: 113: ### SimilarityMethod 114: ```python 115: EXACT_MATCH # Identical code 116: STRUCTURAL # Same AST structure 117: SEMANTIC # Similar logic/behavior 118: HYBRID # Combination of methods 119: ``` 120: 121: ### ConsolidationStrategy 122: ```python 123: LOCAL_UTIL # Utility within single project 124: SHARED_PACKAGE # Shared library across 2-3 projects 125: MCP_SERVER # MCP server for cross-language/tool 126: AUTONOMOUS_AGENT # Complex orchestration requiring AI 127: NO_ACTION # Not worth consolidating 128: ``` 129: 130: ## Usage 131: 132: ### Import Models 133: 134: ```python 135: from sidequest.pipeline_core.models import ( 136: CodeBlock, 137: DuplicateGroup, 138: ConsolidationSuggestion, 139: ScanReport, 140: LanguageType, 141: SemanticCategory, 142: SimilarityMethod, 143: ) 144: ``` 145: 146: ### Create CodeBlock 147: 148: ```python 149: from sidequest.pipeline_core.models import ( 150: CodeBlock, SourceLocation, LanguageType, SemanticCategory 151: ) 152: 153: block = CodeBlock( 154: block_id="cb_001", 155: pattern_id="object-manipulation", 156: location=SourceLocation( 157: file_path="/path/to/repo/src/utils.js", 158: line_start=42, 159: line_end=44 160: ), 161: relative_path="src/utils.js", 162: source_code="JSON.stringify(data, null, 2)", 163: language=LanguageType.JAVASCRIPT, 164: category=SemanticCategory.UTILITY, 165: repository_path="/path/to/repo", 166: line_count=3 167: ) 168: 169: # Access computed hashes 170: print(block.content_hash) # e.g., "a1b2c3d4e5f6..." 171: print(block.structural_hash) # Falls back to content_hash if no AST 172: ``` 173: 174: ### Create DuplicateGroup 175: 176: ```python 177: from sidequest.pipeline_core.models import DuplicateGroup, SimilarityMethod 178: 179: group = DuplicateGroup( 180: group_id="dg_001", 181: pattern_id="object-manipulation", 182: member_block_ids=["cb_001", "cb_002", "cb_003"], 183: similarity_score=0.95, 184: similarity_method=SimilarityMethod.STRUCTURAL, 185: category="utility", 186: language="javascript", 187: occurrence_count=3, 188: total_lines=9, 189: affected_files=["src/utils.js", "src/helpers.js"] 190: ) 191: 192: # Access computed fields 193: print(group.deduplication_potential) # Lines removable: 6 194: print(group.impact_score) # 0-100 priority score 195: print(group.priority_level) # "high" 196: print(group.is_cross_repository) # False 197: ``` 198: 199: ### Create ConsolidationSuggestion 200: 201: ```python 202: from sidequest.pipeline_core.models import ( 203: ConsolidationSuggestion, 204: ConsolidationStrategy, 205: ImplementationComplexity, 206: MigrationRisk 207: ) 208: 209: suggestion = ConsolidationSuggestion( 210: suggestion_id="cs_001", 211: duplicate_group_id="dg_001", 212: strategy=ConsolidationStrategy.LOCAL_UTIL, 213: strategy_rationale="Used within single project, simple utility function", 214: impact_score=75.0, 215: complexity=ImplementationComplexity.TRIVIAL, 216: migration_risk=MigrationRisk.LOW, 217: breaking_changes=False, 218: target_location="src/utils/json.js", 219: target_name="writeJsonFile", 220: loc_reduction=15, 221: affected_files_count=5, 222: affected_repositories_count=1, 223: confidence=0.9 224: ) 225: 226: # Access computed fields 227: print(suggestion.priority) # "critical" 228: print(suggestion.roi_score) # Return on investment 229: print(suggestion.is_quick_win) # True 230: 231: # Add migration steps 232: suggestion.add_migration_step( 233: description="Create writeJsonFile function in src/utils/json.js", 234: code_example="export function writeJsonFile(path, data) { ... }", 235: automated=True, 236: estimated_time="15min" 237: ) 238: 239: # Generate markdown summary 240: print(suggestion.to_markdown_summary()) 241: ``` 242: 243: ### Export for Comparison 244: 245: ```python 246: # Export minimal data for clustering algorithms 247: comparison_data = block.to_dict_for_comparison() 248: # Returns: { block_id, pattern_id, category, language, content_hash, ... } 249: 250: # Export summary for dashboards 251: summary = report.to_summary_dict() 252: # Returns: { report_id, duplication_percentage, quick_wins, ... } 253: ``` 254: 255: ## Validation 256: 257: Models include Pydantic validators: 258: 259: ```python 260: # Line range validation 261: SourceLocation(file_path="x.js", line_start=10, line_end=5) 262: # ValidationError: line_end must be >= line_start 263: 264: # Minimum members validation 265: DuplicateGroup(member_block_ids=["cb_001"]) # Only 1 member 266: # ValidationError: A duplicate group must have at least 2 members 267: 268: # Canonical block validation 269: group.set_canonical("cb_999") # Not in member_block_ids 270: # ValueError: Block cb_999 is not a member of this group 271: ``` 272: 273: ## JSON Schema 274: 275: All models support JSON schema export for API documentation: 276: 277: ```python 278: import json 279: from sidequest.pipeline_core.models import CodeBlock 280: 281: schema = CodeBlock.model_json_schema() 282: print(json.dumps(schema, indent=2)) 283: ``` 284: 285: ## Related Documentation 286: 287: - [Similarity Algorithm](../../../docs/architecture/similarity-algorithm.md) - How duplicates are detected 288: - [Pipeline Data Flow](../../../docs/architecture/pipeline-data-flow.md) - How models flow through pipeline 289: - [AST-Grep Rules](../../../.ast-grep/README.md) - Pattern detection rules 290: 291: --- 292: 293: {required_fields} fields defined") 122: print(f" Sample keys: {', '.join(list(data.keys())[:5])}") 123: print("-" * 60) 124: def test_computed_fields_logic(): 125: """Test the logic of computed fields (without pydantic)""" 126: print("\nTesting computed field logic...") 127: print("-" * 60) 128: # Test DuplicateGroup impact_score calculation 129: def calculate_impact_score(occurrence_count, similarity_score, total_lines): 130: occurrence_factor = min(occurrence_count / 20.0, 1.0) 131: similarity_factor = similarity_score 132: loc_factor = min(total_lines / 100.0, 1.0) 133: score = occurrence_factor * 40 + similarity_factor * 35 + loc_factor * 25 134: return round(score, 2) 135: test_cases = [ 136: (3, 0.95, 9, "Low occurrence, high similarity, small size"), 137: (10, 0.9, 50, "Moderate occurrence, high similarity, moderate size"), 138: (20, 0.8, 100, "High occurrence, good similarity, large size"), 139: ] 140: print("Impact Score Calculation Tests:") 141: for occurrence, similarity, lines, desc in test_cases: 142: score = calculate_impact_score(occurrence, similarity, lines) 143: print(f" {desc}") 144: print(f" Occurrence: {occurrence}, Similarity: {similarity}, Lines: {lines}") 145: print(f" → Impact Score: {score}/100") 146: def calculate_roi_score(impact_score, estimated_hours): 147: if estimated_hours == 0: 148: return 100.0 149: roi = (impact_score / estimated_hours) * 10 150: return min(round(roi, 2), 100.0) 151: print("\nROI Score Calculation Tests:") 152: roi_tests = [ 153: (75.0, 0.5, "High impact, trivial effort"), 154: (50.0, 2.0, "Medium impact, simple effort"), 155: (25.0, 40.0, "Low impact, complex effort"), 156: ] 157: for impact, hours, desc in roi_tests: 158: roi = calculate_roi_score(impact, hours) 159: print(f" {desc}") 160: print(f" Impact: {impact}, Effort: {hours}h") 161: print(f" → ROI Score: {roi}/100") 162: print("-" * 60) 163: def main(): 164: print("\n" + "=" * 60) 165: print("Pydantic Models Structure Test") 166: 'Duplicate Detection Report'; 9: const isInterProject = scanResult.scan_type === 'inter-project'; 10: const html = `<!DOCTYPE html> 11: <html lang="en"> 12: <head> 13: <meta charset="UTF-8"> 14: <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0"> 15: <title>${this._escapeHtml(title)}</title> 16: <style> 17: ${this._getStyles()} 18: </style> 19: </head> 20: <body> 21: <div class="container"> 22: ${this._generateHeader(scanResult, title, isInterProject)} 23: ${this._generateMetrics(scanResult, isInterProject)} 24: ${this._generateSummaryCharts(scanResult, isInterProject)} 25: ${isInterProject ? this._generateCrossRepoSection(scanResult) : ''} 26: ${this._generateDuplicateGroups(scanResult, isInterProject)} 27: ${this._generateSuggestions(scanResult, isInterProject)} 28: ${this._generateFooter()} 29: </div> 30: <script> 31: ${this._getScripts()} 32: </script> 33: </body> 34: </html>`; 35: return html; 36: } 37: static async saveReport(scanResult: ScanResult, outputPath: string, options: HTMLReportOptions = {}): Promise<string> { 38: return saveGeneratedReport(outputPath, this.generateReport(scanResult, options)); 39: } 40: private static _generateHeader(scanResult: ScanResult, title: string, isInterProject: boolean): string { 41: const scanDate = new Date(scanResult.scan_metadata?.scanned_at ?? Date.now()); 42: const duration = scanResult.scan_metadata?.duration_seconds ?? 0; 43: return ` 44: <header> 45: <h1>🔍 ${this._escapeHtml(title)}</h1> 46: <div class="header-meta"> 47: <span class="meta-item"> 48: <strong>Scan Type:</strong> ${isInterProject ? 'Inter-Project' : 'Intra-Project'} 49: </span> 50: <span class="meta-item"> 51: <strong>Date:</strong> ${scanDate.toLocaleString()} 52: </span> 53: <span class="meta-item"> 54: <strong>Duration:</strong> ${duration.toFixed(2)}s 55: </span> 56: </div> 57: </header>`; 58: } 59: private static _generateMetrics(scanResult: ScanResult, isInterProject: boolean): string { 60: const metrics = scanResult.metrics ?? {}; 61: if (isInterProject) { 62: return ` 63: <section class="metrics"> 64: <h2>📊 Scan Metrics</h2> 65: <div class="metrics-grid"> 66: <div class="metric-card"> 67: <div class="metric-value">${metrics.total_repositories_scanned ?? 0}</div> 68: <div class="metric-label">Repositories Scanned</div> 69: </div> 70: <div class="metric-card"> 71: <div class="metric-value">${metrics.total_code_blocks ?? 0}</div> 72: <div class="metric-label">Code Blocks</div> 73: </div> 74: <div class="metric-card highlight"> 75: <div class="metric-value">${metrics.total_cross_repository_groups ?? 0}</div> 76: <div class="metric-label">Cross-Repo Duplicates</div> 77: </div> 78: <div class="metric-card"> 79: <div class="metric-value">${metrics.cross_repository_duplicated_lines ?? 0}</div> 80: <div class="metric-label">Duplicated Lines</div> 81: </div> 82: <div class="metric-card success"> 83: <div class="metric-value">${metrics.shared_package_candidates ?? 0}</div> 84: <div class="metric-label">Shared Package Candidates</div> 85: </div> 86: <div class="metric-card"> 87: <div class="metric-value">${metrics.mcp_server_candidates ?? 0}</div> 88: <div class="metric-label">MCP Server Candidates</div> 89: </div> 90: </div> 91: </section>`; 92: } 93: return ` 94: <section class="metrics"> 95: <h2>📊 Scan Metrics</h2> 96: <div class="metrics-grid"> 97: <div class="metric-card"> 98: <div class="metric-value">${metrics.total_code_blocks ?? 0}</div> 99: <div class="metric-label">Code Blocks Detected</div> 100: </div> 101: <div class="metric-card highlight"> 102: <div class="metric-value">${metrics.total_duplicate_groups ?? 0}</div> 103: <div class="metric-label">Duplicate Groups</div> 104: </div> 105: <div class="metric-card"> 106: <div class="metric-value">${metrics.exact_duplicates ?? 0}</div> 107: <div class="metric-label">Exact Duplicates</div> 108: </div> 109: <div class="metric-card"> 110: <div class="metric-value">${metrics.total_duplicated_lines ?? 0}</div> 111: <div class="metric-label">Duplicated Lines</div> 112: </div> 113: <div class="metric-card success"> 114: <div class="metric-value">${metrics.potential_loc_reduction ?? 0}</div> 115: <div class="metric-label">Potential LOC Reduction</div> 116: </div> 117: <div class="metric-card"> 118: <div class="metric-value">${metrics.quick_wins ?? 0}</div> 119: <div class="metric-label">Quick Wins</div> 120: </div> 121: </div> 122: </section>`; 123: } 124: private static _generateSummaryCharts(scanResult: ScanResult, isInterProject: boolean): string { 125: const suggestions = isInterProject 126: ? (scanResult.cross_repository_suggestions ?? []) 127: : (scanResult.suggestions ?? []); 128: const strategyCounts: Record<string, number> = {}; 129: suggestions.forEach(s => { 130: strategyCounts[s.strategy] = (strategyCounts[s.strategy] ?? 0) + 1; 131: }); 132: const complexityCounts: Record<string, number> = {}; 133: suggestions.forEach(s => { 134: complexityCounts[s.complexity] = (complexityCounts[s.complexity] ?? 0) + 1; 135: }); 136: return ` 137: <section class="charts"> 138: <h2>📈 Distribution</h2> 139: <div class="charts-grid"> 140: <div class="chart-card"> 141: <h3>By Strategy</h3> 142: <div class="chart-bars"> 143: ${Object.entries(strategyCounts).map(([strategy, count]) => ` 144: <div class="chart-bar-row"> 145: <span class="chart-label">${strategy.replace('_', ' ')}</span> 146: <div class="chart-bar-container"> 147: <div class="chart-bar strategy-${strategy}" style="width: ${(count / suggestions.length * 100).toFixed(1)}%"></div> 148: </div> 149: <span class="chart-count">${count}</span> 150: </div> 151: `).join('')} 152: </div> 153: </div> 154: <div class="chart-card"> 155: <h3>By Complexity</h3> 156: <div class="chart-bars"> 157: ${Object.entries(complexityCounts).map(([complexity, count]) => ` 158: <div class="chart-bar-row"> 159: <span class="chart-label">${complexity}</span> 160: <div class="chart-bar-container"> 161: <div class="chart-bar complexity-${complexity}" style="width: ${(count / suggestions.length * 100).toFixed(1)}%"></div> 162: </div> 163: <span class="chart-count">${count}</span> 164: </div> 165: `).join('')} 166: </div> 167: </div> 168: </div> 169: </section>`; 170: } 171: private static _generateCrossRepoSection(scanResult: ScanResult): string { 172: const repos = scanResult.scanned_repositories ?? 'error' : ''}"> 179: <div class="repo-name">${this._escapeHtml(repo.name)}</div> 180: ${repo.error ? ` 181: <div class="repo-error">❌ ${this._escapeHtml(repo.error)}</div> 182: ` : ` 183: <div class="repo-stats"> 184: <span>${repo.code_blocks ?? 0} blocks</span> 185: <span>${repo.duplicate_groups ?? 0} groups</span> 186: </div> 187: `} 188: </div> 189: `).join('')} 190: </div> 191: </section>`; 192: } 193: private static _generateDuplicateGroups(scanResult: ScanResult, isInterProject: boolean): string { 194: const groups = isInterProject 195: ? (scanResult.cross_repository_duplicates ?? []) 196: : (scanResult.duplicate_groups ?? []); 197: const topGroups = groups 198: .sort((a, b) => b.impact_score - a.impact_score) 199: .slice(0, 10); 200: if (topGroups.length === 0) { 201: return ` 202: <section class="duplicates"> 203: <h2>🎯 Duplicate Groups</h2> 204: <p class="empty-state">No duplicate groups detected.</p> 205: </section>`; 206: } 207: return ` 208: <section class="duplicates"> 209: <h2>🎯 Top Duplicate Groups</h2> 210: <div class="duplicates-list"> 211: ${topGroups.map((group, index) => ` 212: <div class="duplicate-card"> 213: <div class="duplicate-header"> 214: <div class="duplicate-rank">#${index + 1}</div> 215: <div class="duplicate-title"> 216: <strong>${this._escapeHtml(group.group_id)}</strong> 217: <span class="duplicate-pattern">${this._escapeHtml(group.pattern_id)}</span> 218: </div> 219: <div class="duplicate-impact ${this._getImpactClass(group.impact_score)}"> 220: ${group.impact_score.toFixed(0)}% impact 221: </div> 222: </div> 223: <div class="duplicate-stats"> 224: <span class="stat-badge"> 225: 📦 ${group.occurrence_count} occurrences 226: </span> 227: ${isInterProject ? ` 228: <span class="stat-badge"> 229: 🔗 ${group.repository_count ?? 0} repositories 230: </span> 231: ` : ''} 232: <span class="stat-badge"> 233: 📄 ${group.affected_files?.length ?? 0} files 234: </span> 235: <span class="stat-badge"> 236: 📏 ${group.total_lines} lines 237: </span> 238: </div> 239: <div class="duplicate-files"> 240: <strong>Affected files:</strong> 241: <ul> 242: ${(group.affected_files ?? 134: roi_statistics?: ROIStatistics; 135: } 136: export class JSONReportGenerator { 137: static generateReport(scanResult: ScanResult, options: JSONReportOptions = {}): Record<string, unknown> { 138: const includeSourceCode = options.includeSourceCode !== false; 139: const includeCodeBlocks = options.includeCodeBlocks !== false; 140: const maxDuplicates = options.maxDuplicates ?? null; 141: const maxSuggestions = options.maxSuggestions ?? null; 142: const isInterProject = scanResult.scan_type === 'inter-project'; 'inter-project' : 'intra-project', 147: metadata: this._generateMetadata(scanResult, isInterProject), 148: metrics: scanResult.metrics ?? {}, 149: summary: this._generateSummary(scanResult, isInterProject), 150: duplicate_groups: this._formatDuplicateGroups( 151: scanResult, 152: isInterProject, 153: maxDuplicates, 154: includeSourceCode 155: ), 156: suggestions: this._formatSuggestions( 157: scanResult, 158: isInterProject, 159: maxSuggestions 160: ) 161: }; 162: if (isInterProject) { 163: report.scanned_repositories = scanResult.scanned_repositories ?? 'inter-project' : 'intra-project', 185: generated_at: new Date().toISOString(), 186: summary: this._generateSummary(scanResult, isInterProject), 187: metrics: { 188: ...(isInterProject ? { 189: repositories_scanned: metrics.total_repositories_scanned ?? 0, 190: total_code_blocks: metrics.total_code_blocks ?? 0, 191: cross_repo_duplicates: metrics.total_cross_repository_groups ?? 0, 192: cross_repo_duplicated_lines: metrics.cross_repository_duplicated_lines ?? 0, 193: total_suggestions: metrics.total_suggestions ?? 0, 194: shared_package_candidates: metrics.shared_package_candidates ?? 0, 195: mcp_server_candidates: metrics.mcp_server_candidates ?? 0 196: } : { 197: code_blocks: metrics.total_code_blocks ?? 0, 198: duplicate_groups: metrics.total_duplicate_groups ?? 0, 199: duplicated_lines: metrics.total_duplicated_lines ?? 0, 200: potential_loc_reduction: metrics.potential_loc_reduction ?? 0, 201: total_suggestions: metrics.total_suggestions ?? 0, 202: quick_wins: metrics.quick_wins ?? 0 203: }) 204: } 205: }; 206: } 207: static async saveReport(scanResult: ScanResult, outputPath: string, options: JSONReportOptions = {}): Promise<string> { 208: const report = this.generateReport(scanResult, options); 209: const json = options.prettyPrint !== false ? []).map(r => r.name) 222: } : { 223: repository_name: repoInfo.name ?? 'Unknown', 224: repository_path: repoInfo.path ?? 'Unknown', 225: total_files: repoInfo.total_files ?? 0, 226: languages: repoInfo.languages ?? [] 227: }), 228: scanned_at: metadata.scanned_at ?? new Date().toISOString(), 229: duration_seconds: metadata.duration_seconds ?? 0, 230: scanner_version: '2.0.0' 231: }; 232: } 233: private static _generateSummary(scanResult: ScanResult, isInterProject: boolean): ReportSummary { 234: const metrics = scanResult.metrics ?? {}; 235: const duplicateGroups = isInterProject 236: ? (scanResult.cross_repository_duplicates ?? []) 237: : (scanResult.duplicate_groups ?? []); 238: const suggestions = isInterProject 239: ? (scanResult.cross_repository_suggestions ?? Run a Scan 34: 35: **JavaScript/Node.js:** 36: ```bash 37: cd ~/code/jobs 38: 39: # Scan for timeout issues 40: node lib/scanners/codebase-health-scanner.js ~/code/myproject --scan timeout 41: 42: # Scan for root directory issues 43: node lib/scanners/codebase-health-scanner.js ~/code/myproject --scan root 44: 45: # Run all scans 46: node lib/scanners/codebase-health-scanner.js ~/code/myproject --scan all --output report.md 47: ``` 48: 49: **Python (simpler, no ast-grep required):** 50: ```bash 51: # Timeout detection only 52: python3 lib/scanners/timeout_detector.py ~/code/myproject 53: 54: # Save to file 55: python3 lib/scanners/timeout_detector.py ~/code/myproject --output timeout-report.md 56: ``` 57: 58: ### 3. Review Results 59: 60: Open the generated markdown report and prioritize fixes by severity: 61: - 🔴 **High:** Fix immediately (can cause production issues) 62: - 🟡 **Medium:** Fix soon (can cause user frustration) 63: - 🟢 **Low:** Nice to have (improves code quality) 64: 65: --- 66: 67: ## What These Scanners Do 68: 69: ### Timeout Pattern Detector 70: 71: **Prevents:** Infinite loading spinners, hanging dashboards, frozen UIs 72: 73: **Detects:** 74: 1. `Promise.race()` without timeout wrappers 75: 2. `setLoading(true)` without `finally` blocks 76: 3. Async functions without error handling 77: 4. Missing timeout utility constants 78: 5. Loading states without safety net timeouts 79: 80: **Real Bug Fixed:** 81: - **Problem:** AnalyticsBot dashboard spinning forever 82: - **Root Cause:** `getAnalyticsSummary()` called `listProjects()` which could hang indefinitely 83: - **Scanner Would Have Caught:** Promise.race() without timeout on line 469 84: - **Fix:** Added `withTimeout()` wrapper with 10-second max 85: 86: ### Root Directory Analyzer 87: 88: **Prevents:** Messy codebases, hard-to-navigate projects, import confusion 89: 90: **Analyzes:** 91: 1. Number of files in root (flags if > 20) 92: 2. File categorization (Python, JS, configs, data, etc.) 93: 3. Import dependencies (Python) 94: 4.
