Run scan for details." 242: exit 1 243: fi 244: ``` 245: 246: ### Option 3: PM2 Scheduled Job 247: 248: Add to `ecosystem.config.cjs`: 249: ```javascript 250: { 251: name: 'health-scan', 252: script: './lib/scanners/codebase-health-scanner.js', 253: args: '~/code/myproject --scan all --output output/health.md', 254: cron_restart: '0 2 * * *', // Daily at 2am 255: autorestart: false 256: } 257: ``` 258: 259: ### Option 4: GitHub Actions 260: 261: Create `.github/workflows/health-scan.yml`: 262: ```yaml 263: name: Codebase Health Scan 264: on: 265: pull_request: 266: branches: [main] 267: 268: jobs: 269: scan: 270: runs-on: ubuntu-latest 271: steps: 272: - uses: actions/checkout@v3 273: - uses: actions/setup-node@v3 274: - run: npm install -g @ast-grep/cli 275: - run: | 276: node lib/scanners/codebase-health-scanner.js . \ 277: --scan timeout \ 278: --output health-report.md 279: - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v3 280: with: 281: name: health-report 282: path: health-report.md 283: ``` 284: 285: --- 286: 287: ## Common Issues & Solutions 288: 289: ### "ast-grep not found" 290: 291: ```bash 292: # Install globally 293: npm install -g @ast-grep/cli 294: 295: # Or use local install 296: npm install @ast-grep/cli 297: npx sg --version 298: ``` 299: 300: ### "Cannot find module" 301: 302: Ensure you're using Node.js 18+ with ESM: 303: ```bash 304: node --version # Should be v18.0.0+ 305: ``` 306: 307: ### Scanner finds nothing 308: 309: Check if patterns match your codebase: 310: ```bash 311: # Test ast-grep manually 312: cd ~/code/myproject 313: sg run --pattern 'Promise.race($$$)' --lang typescript 314: ``` 315: 316: ### Python version doesn't find all issues 317: 318: The Python version is simpler and uses regex. For comprehensive scanning, use the JavaScript version with ast-grep. 319: 320: --- 321: 322: ## Configuration 323: 324: ### Customize Thresholds 325: 326: Edit scanner files to adjust sensitivity: 327: 328: **Root Directory Analyzer:** 329: ```javascript 330: // lib/scanners/root-directory-analyzer.js 331: this.maxRootFiles = 20; // Change to your preference 332: this.thresholds = { 333: pythonFiles: 3, // Warn if > 3 Python files in root 334: shellScripts: 3, 335: jsFiles: 3, 336: configFiles: 5, 337: dataFiles: 0 // Never allow data files in root 338: }; 339: ``` 340: 341: **Timeout Pattern Detector:** 342: ```javascript 343: // lib/scanners/timeout-pattern-detector.js 344: // Add custom patterns in scan() method 345: const customIssues = await this.findCustomPattern(repoPath); 346: ``` 347: 348: --- 349: 350: ## Real Results 351: 352: ### AnalyticsBot Dashboard (Nov 18, 2025) 353: 354: **Problem:** 355: - Dashboard spinning forever 356: - No error messages 357: - Required browser restart 358: 359: **Scan Results:** 360: ``` 361: 🔴 HIGH: src/api/client.ts:261 362: Promise.race() in getAnalyticsSummary() without timeout 363: 364: 🔴 HIGH: src/api/client.ts:469 365: Promise.race() in listProjects() without timeout 366: 367: 🟡 MEDIUM: src/hooks/useDashboardData.ts:46 368: setLoading(true) without finally block 369: ``` 370: 371: **Fixes Applied:** 372: 1. Created `utils/timeout.ts` with `withTimeout()` utility 373: 2. Wrapped `getAnalyticsSummary()` with 10s timeout 374: 3. Added safety net timeout in `useDashboardData` hook 375: 4. Replaced all `Promise.race()` calls with `withTimeout()` 376: 377: **Result:** 378: - ✅ Dashboard loads within 10 seconds guaranteed 379: - ✅ Error message shown after 15 seconds if all else fails 380: - ✅ No more infinite spinners 381: 382: --- 383: 384: ## Next Steps 385: 386: 1. **Run your first scan:** 387: ```bash 388: node lib/scanners/codebase-health-scanner.js ~/code/myproject 389: ``` 390: 391: 2. **Review the generated report** 392: 393: 3. **Fix high severity issues first** 394: 395: 4. **Integrate into your workflow:** 396: - Add to pre-commit hooks 397: - Schedule via PM2 398: - Add to CI/CD pipeline 399: 400: 5. **Customize for your needs:** 401: - Add project-specific patterns 402: - Adjust thresholds 403: - Create custom categories 404: 405: --- 406: 407: ## Documentation 408: 409: **Full Documentation:** 410: - [Detailed Guide](../../docs/codebase-health-scanners.md) 411: 412: **Session Notes:** 413: - Based on AnalyticsBot debugging session 414: - Real production issues → Real automated detection 415: - Patterns extracted from actual fixes 416: 417: **Support:** 418: - Questions? 2: import { createComponentLogger, logStart } from '../../utils/logger.ts'; 3: import { config } from '../../core/config.ts'; 4: import fs from 'fs/promises'; 5: import path from 'path'; 6: import { execSync } from 'child_process'; 7: const logger = createComponentLogger('RepositoryScanner'); 8: export interface RepositoryScannerOptions { 9: outputBaseDir?: string; 10: codeBaseDir?: string; 11: maxConcurrent?: number; 12: } 13: export interface ScanConfig { 14: [key: string]: unknown; 15: } 16: export interface RepositoryInfo { 17: path: string; 18: name: string; 19: git_remote: string | null; 20: git_branch: string | null; 21: git_commit: string | null; 22: } 23: export interface RepomixMetadata { 24: totalFiles: number; 25: totalLines: number; 26: languages: string[]; 27: } 28: export interface RepositoryScanOutput { 29: repository_info: RepositoryInfo & { 30: total_files: number; 31: total_lines: number; 32: languages: string[]; 33: }; 34: file_metadata: unknown[]; 35: scan_metadata: { 36: duration_seconds: number; 37: repomix_output: string | null; 38: timestamp: string; 39: used_repomix: boolean; 40: }; 41: } 42: interface FileFilter { 43: languages?: string[]; 44: } 45: export class RepositoryScanner { 46: private readonly repomixWorker: InstanceType<typeof RepomixWorker>; 47: constructor(options: RepositoryScannerOptions = {}) { 48: this.repomixWorker = new RepomixWorker({ 49: outputBaseDir: options.outputBaseDir ?? config.outputBaseDir, 50: codeBaseDir: options.codeBaseDir ?? config.codeBaseDir, 51: maxConcurrent: options.maxConcurrent ?? config.maxConcurrent 52: }); 53: } 54: async scanRepository(repoPath: string, scanConfig: ScanConfig = {}): Promise<RepositoryScanOutput> { 55: const startTime = Date.now(); 56: logStart(logger, 'repository scan', { repoPath }); 57: try { 58: await this.validateRepository(repoPath); 59: const repoInfo = await this.getRepositoryInfo(repoPath); 60: let metadata: RepomixMetadata = { totalFiles: 0, totalLines: 0, languages: [] }; 61: let repomixOutput: string | null = null; 62: try { 63: const repomixResult = await this.runRepomixScan(repoPath); 64: metadata = await this.parseRepomixOutput(repomixResult.outputFile as string); 65: repomixOutput = repomixResult.outputFile as string; 66: } catch (error) { 67: logger.warn({ error: (error as Error).message }, 'Repomix scan failed, using basic file discovery'); 68: const files = await this.listFiles(repoPath); 69: metadata = { 70: totalFiles: files.length, 71: totalLines: 0, 72: languages: this.detectLanguages(files) 73: }; 74: } 75: const fileMetadata = await this.getFileMetadata(repoPath, scanConfig); 76: const duration = (Date.now() - startTime) / 1000; 77: logger.info({ 78: repoPath, 79: totalFiles: metadata.totalFiles, 80: duration 81: }, 'Repository scan completed'); 82: return { 83: repository_info: { 84: ...repoInfo, 85: total_files: metadata.totalFiles, 86: total_lines: metadata.totalLines, 87: languages: metadata.languages 88: }, 89: file_metadata: fileMetadata, 90: scan_metadata: { 91: duration_seconds: duration, 92: repomix_output: repomixOutput, 93: timestamp: new Date().toISOString(), 94: used_repomix: repomixOutput !== null 95: } 96: }; 97: } catch (error) { 98: logger.error({ repoPath, error }, 'Repository scan failed'); 99: throw new RepositoryScanError(`Failed to scan repository: ${(error as Error).message}`, { 100: cause: error 101: }); 102: } 103: } 104: async validateRepository(repoPath: string): Promise<void> { 105: try { 106: const stats = await fs.stat(repoPath); 107: if (!stats.isDirectory()) { 108: throw new Error('Path is not a directory'); 109: } 110: try { 111: await fs.access(path.join(repoPath, '.git')); 112: } catch { 113: logger.warn({ repoPath }, 'Not a git repository, proceeding anyway'); 114: } 115: } catch (error) { 116: throw new Error(`Invalid repository path: ${(error as Error).message}`); 117: } 118: } 119: async getRepositoryInfo(repoPath: string): Promise<RepositoryInfo> { 120: const name = path.basename(repoPath); 121: const info: RepositoryInfo = { 122: path: repoPath, 123: name: name, 124: git_remote: null, 125: git_branch: null, 126: git_commit: null 127: }; 128: try { 129: const gitDir = path.join(repoPath, '.git'); 130: await fs.access(gitDir); 131: try { 132: info.git_remote = execSync('git config --get remote.origin.url', { 133: cwd: repoPath, 134: encoding: 'utf-8' 135: }).trim(); 136: } catch { 137: logger.debug({ repoPath }, 'No git remote found'); 138: } 139: try { 140: info.git_branch = execSync('git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD', { 141: cwd: repoPath, 142: encoding: 'utf-8' 143: }).trim(); 144: } catch { 145: logger.debug({ repoPath }, 'Could not determine git branch'); 146: } 147: try { 148: info.git_commit = execSync('git rev-parse HEAD', { 149: cwd: repoPath, 150: encoding: 'utf-8' 151: }).trim(); 152: } catch { 153: logger.debug({ repoPath }, 'Could not determine git commit'); 154: } 155: } catch { 156: logger.debug({ repoPath }, 'Not a git repository'); 157: } 158: return info; 159: } 160: async runRepomixScan(repoPath: string): Promise<Record<string, unknown>> { 161: const relativePath = path.relative(config.codeBaseDir, repoPath) || path.basename(repoPath); 162: const job = this.repomixWorker.createRepomixJob(repoPath, relativePath); 163: return await this.repomixWorker.runJobHandler(job) as Record<string, unknown>; 164: } 165: async parseRepomixOutput(outputFile: string): Promise<RepomixMetadata> { 166: try { 167: const content = await fs.readFile(outputFile, 'utf-8'); 168: const metadata = { 169: totalFiles: 0, 170: totalLines: 0, 171: languages: new Set<string>() 172: }; 173: const dirStructMatch = content.match(/<directory_structure>([\s\S]*?)<\/directory_structure>/); 174: if (dirStructMatch) { 175: const structure = dirStructMatch[1]; 176: metadata.totalFiles = (structure.match(/\n\s+[^\s/]+\.[^\s/]+$/gm) ?? []).length; 177: } 178: const fileMatches = content.matchAll(/<file path="([^"]+)">/g); 179: const langMap: Record<string, string> = { 180: '.js': 'javascript', 181: '.ts': 'typescript', 182: '.jsx': 'javascript', 183: '.tsx': 'typescript', 184: '.py': 'python', 185: '.go': 'go', 186: '.rs': 'rust', 187: '.java': 'java', 188: '.rb': 'ruby', 189: '.php': 'php' 190: }; 191: for (const match of fileMatches) { 192: metadata.totalFiles++; 193: const ext = path.extname(match[1]).toLowerCase(); 194: if (langMap[ext]) { 195: metadata.languages.add(langMap[ext]); 196: } 197: } 198: const linesMatch = content.match(/\n/g); 199: metadata.totalLines = linesMatch ? linesMatch.length : 0; 200: return { 201: totalFiles: metadata.totalFiles, 202: totalLines: metadata.totalLines, 203: languages: Array.from(metadata.languages) 204: }; 205: } catch (error) { 206: logger.warn({ outputFile, error }, 'Failed to parse repomix output'); 207: return { 208: totalFiles: 0, 209: totalLines: 0, 210: languages: [] 211: }; 212: } 213: } 214: async getFileMetadata(_repoPath: string, _scanConfig: ScanConfig): Promise<unknown[]> { 215: return []; 216: } 217: async listFiles(repoPath: string, filter: FileFilter = {}): Promise<string[]> { 218: const files: string[] = []; 219: const ignoredDirs = new Set(['node_modules', '.git', 'dist', 'build', '.next']); 220: const langMap: Record<string, string> = { 221: '.js': 'javascript', 222: '.ts': 'typescript', 223: '.jsx': 'javascript', 224: '.tsx': 'typescript', 225: '.py': 'python' 226: }; 227: async function walk(dir: string): Promise<void> { 228: const entries = await fs.readdir(dir, { withFileTypes: true }); 229: for (const entry of entries) { 230: const fullPath = path.join(dir, entry.name); 231: if (entry.isDirectory()) { 232: if (ignoredDirs.has(entry.name)) { 233: continue; 234: } 235: await walk(fullPath); 236: } else { 237: if (filter.languages) { 238: const ext = path.extname(entry.name).toLowerCase(); 239: if (filter.languages.includes(langMap[ext])) { 240: files.push(fullPath); 241: } 242: } else { 243: files.push(fullPath); 244: } 245: } 246: } 247: } 248: await walk(repoPath); 249: return files; 250: } 251: detectLanguages(files: string[]): string[] { 252: const langMap: Record<string, string> = { 253: '.js': 'javascript', 254: '.ts': 'typescript', 255: '.jsx': 'javascript', 256: '.tsx': 'typescript', 257: '.py': 'python', 258: '.go': 'go', 259: '.rs': 'rust', 260: '.java': 'java', 261: '.rb': 'ruby', 262: '.php': 'php' 263: }; 264: const languages = new Set<string>(); 265: for (const file of files) { 266: const ext = path.extname(file).toLowerCase(); 267: if (langMap[ext]) { 268: languages.add(langMap[ext]); 269: } 270: } 271: return Array.from(languages); 272: } 273: } 274: export class RepositoryScanError extends Error { 275: constructor(message: string, options?: { cause?: unknown }) { 276: super(message); 277: if (options?.cause) { 278: (this as unknown as Record<string, unknown>).cause = options.cause; 279: } 280: this.name = 'RepositoryScanError'; 281: } 282: } 1: import * as fs from 'fs/promises'; 2: import * as path from 'path'; 3: export interface RootDirectoryAnalyzerOptions { 4: logger?: Pick<Console, 'info' | 'error'>; 5: maxRootFiles?: number; 6: } 7: export interface AnalyzeOptions { 8: [key: string]: unknown; 9: } 10: export interface RootFile { 11: name: string; 12: path: string; 13: extension: string; 14: } 15: export interface CategorizedFiles { 16: python: RootFile[]; 17: shell: RootFile[]; 18: javascript: RootFile[]; 19: typescript: RootFile[]; 20: config: RootFile[]; 21: data: RootFile[]; 22: documentation: RootFile[]; 23: packageManager: RootFile[]; 24: deployment: RootFile[]; 25: other: RootFile[]; 26: } 27: export interface PythonDependencyInfo { 28: imports_from_root: string[]; 29: imported_by: string[]; 30: can_move: boolean; 31: } 32: export interface PythonImports { 33: fromRoot: string[]; 34: fromPackages: string[]; 35: relative: string[]; 36: } 37: export interface Dependencies { 38: python: Record<string, PythonDependencyInfo>; 39: javascript: Record<string, unknown>; 40: } 41: export interface ImportChange { 42: file: string; 43: old_import: string; 44: new_import: string; 45: } 46: export interface Recommendation { 47: id: string; 48: title: string; 49: description: string; 50: files: RootFile[]; 51: target_directory: string; 52: impact: 'low' | 'medium' | 'high'; 53: requires_import_updates: boolean; 54: import_changes?: ImportChange[]; 55: commands: string[]; 56: path_updates_required?: string; 57: } 58: export interface AnalysisStatistics { 59: total_root_files: number; 60: reduction_potential: number; 61: final_root_files: number; 62: reduction_percentage: number; 63: scan_duration_ms: number; 64: } 65: export interface RootDirectoryAnalysis { 66: root_files: RootFile[]; 67: categorized: CategorizedFiles; 68: dependencies: Dependencies; 69: recommendations: Recommendation[]; 70: statistics: AnalysisStatistics; 71: } 72: type FileCategoryKey = keyof CategorizedFiles; 73: export class RootDirectoryAnalyzer { 74: private readonly logger: Pick<Console, 'info' | 'error'>; 75: private readonly maxRootFiles: number; 76: private readonly thresholds: { 77: pythonFiles: number; 78: shellScripts: number; 79: jsFiles: number; 80: configFiles: number; 81: dataFiles: number; 82: }; 83: constructor(options: RootDirectoryAnalyzerOptions = {}) { 84: this.logger = options.logger ?? console; 85: this.maxRootFiles = options.maxRootFiles ?? 20; 86: this.thresholds = { 87: pythonFiles: 3, 88: shellScripts: 3, 89: jsFiles: 3, 90: configFiles: 5, 91: dataFiles: 0 92: }; 93: } 94: async analyze(repoPath: string, _options: AnalyzeOptions = {}): Promise<RootDirectoryAnalysis> { 95: const startTime = Date.now(); 96: this.logger.info(`[RootDirectoryAnalyzer] Starting analysis: ${repoPath}`); 97: try { 98: const rootFiles = await this.getRootFiles(repoPath); 99: const categorized = this.categorizeFiles(rootFiles); 100: const dependencies = await this.analyzeImportDependencies(repoPath, categorized); 101: const recommendations = this.generateRecommendations(categorized, dependencies); 102: const reductionPotential = recommendations.reduce((sum, r) => sum + r.files.length, 0); 103: const statistics: AnalysisStatistics = { 104: total_root_files: rootFiles.length, 105: reduction_potential: reductionPotential, 106: final_root_files: rootFiles.length - reductionPotential, 107: reduction_percentage: rootFiles.length > 0 108: ? Math.round((reductionPotential / rootFiles.length) * 100) 109: : 0, 110: scan_duration_ms: Date.now() - startTime 111: }; 112: const result: RootDirectoryAnalysis = { 113: root_files: rootFiles, 114: categorized, 115: dependencies, 116: recommendations, 117: statistics 118: }; 119: this.logger.info( 120: `[RootDirectoryAnalyzer] Analysis complete: ${rootFiles.length} files, ${statistics.reduction_potential} can be moved (${statistics.reduction_percentage}% reduction)` 121: ); 122: return result; 123: } catch (error) { 124: this.logger.error(`[RootDirectoryAnalyzer] Analysis failed: ${(error as Error).message}`); 125: throw error; 126: } 127: } 128: async getRootFiles(repoPath: string): Promise<RootFile[]> { 129: const entries = await fs.readdir(repoPath, { withFileTypes: true }); 130: return entries 131: .filter(entry => entry.isFile() && !entry.name.startsWith('.')) 132: .map(entry => ({ 133: name: entry.name, 134: path: path.join(repoPath, entry.name), 135: extension: path.extname(entry.name) 136: })); 137: } 138: categorizeFiles(files: RootFile[]): CategorizedFiles { 139: const categories: CategorizedFiles = { 140: python: [], 141: shell: [], 142: javascript: [], 143: typescript: [], 144: config: [], 145: data: [], 146: documentation: [], 147: packageManager: [], 148: deployment: [], 149: other: [] 150: }; 151: const extensionMap: Record<string, FileCategoryKey> = { 152: '.py': 'python', 153: '.sh': 'shell', 154: '.bash': 'shell', 155: '.js': 'javascript', 156: '.mjs': 'javascript', 157: '.cjs': 'javascript', 158: '.ts': 'typescript', 159: '.tsx': 'typescript', 160: '.json': 'config', 161: '.yml': 'config', 162: '.yaml': 'config', 163: '.toml': 'config', 164: '.ini': 'config', 165: '.db': 'data', 166: '.sqlite': 'data', 167: '.sqlite3': 'data', 168: '.md': 'documentation', 169: '.txt': 'documentation' 170: }; 171: const packageFiles = new Set([ 172: 'package.json', 173: 'package-lock.json', 174: 'yarn.lock', 175: 'pnpm-lock.yaml', 176: 'requirements.txt', 177: 'Pipfile', 178: 'Pipfile.lock', 179: 'pyproject.toml', 180: 'poetry.lock', 181: 'Cargo.toml', 182: 'Cargo.lock', 183: 'go.mod', 184: 'go.sum' 185: ]); 186: const deploymentFiles = new Set([ 187: 'Dockerfile', 188: 'docker-compose.yml', 189: 'docker-compose.yaml', 190: 'render.yaml', 191: 'vercel.json', 192: 'netlify.toml', 193: 'Procfile', 194: 'doppler.yaml', 195: '.env.example' 196: ]); 197: for (const file of files) { 198: if (packageFiles.has(file.name)) { 199: categories.packageManager.push(file); 200: } else if (deploymentFiles.has(file.name)) { 201: categories.deployment.push(file); 202: } else { 203: const category: FileCategoryKey = extensionMap[file.extension] ?? 'other'; 204: categories[category].push(file); 205: } 206: } 207: return categories; 208: } 209: async analyzeImportDependencies(repoPath: string, categorized: CategorizedFiles): Promise<Dependencies> { 210: const dependencies: Dependencies = { 211: python: {}, 212: javascript: {} 213: }; 214: for (const file of categorized.python) { 215: const imports = await this.analyzePythonImports(file.path, repoPath); 216: dependencies.python[file.name] = { 217: imports_from_root: imports.fromRoot, 218: imported_by: [], 219: can_move: imports.fromRoot.length === 0 220: }; 221: } 222: for (const [fileName, data] of Object.entries(dependencies.python)) { 223: for (const imported of data.imports_from_root) { 224: const importedFile = imported.replace(/^from /, '').replace(/ import.*/, '') + '.py'; 225: if (dependencies.python[importedFile]) { 226: dependencies.python[importedFile].imported_by.push(fileName); 227: } 228: } 229: } 230: return dependencies; 231: } 232: async analyzePythonImports(filePath: string, repoPath: string): Promise<PythonImports> { 233: try { 234: const content = await fs.readFile(filePath, 'utf-8'); 235: const lines = content.split('\n'); 236: const imports: PythonImports = { 237: fromRoot: [], 238: fromPackages: [], 239: relative: [] 240: }; 241: for (const line of lines) { 242: const trimmed = line.trim(); 243: const fromMatch = trimmed.match(/^from\s+([^\s]+)\s+import/); 244: if (fromMatch) { 245: const module = fromMatch[1]; 246: if (module.startsWith('.')) { 247: imports.relative.push(trimmed); 248: } else if (!module.includes('.') && this.isLocalModule(module, repoPath)) { 249: imports.fromRoot.push(trimmed); 250: } else { 251: imports.fromPackages.push(trimmed); 252: } 253: } 254: const importMatch = trimmed.match(/^import\s+([^\s]+)/); 255: if (importMatch) { 256: const module = importMatch[1]; 257: if (this.isLocalModule(module, repoPath)) { 258: imports.fromRoot.push(trimmed); 259: } else { 260: imports.fromPackages.push(trimmed); 261: } 262: } 263: } 264: return imports; 265: } catch { 266: return { fromRoot: [], fromPackages: [], relative: [] }; 267: } 268: } 269: isLocalModule(moduleName: string, repoPath: string): boolean { 270: const filePath = path.join(repoPath, `${moduleName}.py`); 271: try { 272: fs.access(filePath); 273: return true; 274: } catch { 275: return false; 276: } 277: } 278: generateRecommendations(categorized: CategorizedFiles, dependencies: Dependencies): Recommendation[] { 279: const recommendations: Recommendation[] = []; 280: const pythonLibFiles = categorized.python.filter(f => { 281: const baseName = f.name.replace('.py', ''); 282: const dep = dependencies.python[f.name]; 283: // Identify as library if: 284: // - Has "lib", "utils", "helper", "middleware", "config" in name 285: // - Is imported by other root files 286: // - Is not a main entry point (server, app, main) 287: const isLibrary = 288: /(lib|util|helper|middleware|config|auth|rate|sentry)/.test(baseName) || 289: (dep !== undefined && dep.imported_by.length > 0); 290: const isEntryPoint = /(server|app|main|run|start)/.test(baseName); 291: return isLibrary && !isEntryPoint; 292: }); 293: if (pythonLibFiles.length > 0) { 294: recommendations.push({ 295: id: 'move_python_lib', 296: title: 'Move Python Library Files to lib/', 297: description: 'Move shared Python modules to lib/ directory for better organization', 298: files: pythonLibFiles, 299: target_directory: 'lib', 300: impact: 'medium', 301: requires_import_updates: true, 302: import_changes: this.calculateImportChanges(pythonLibFiles, 'lib', dependencies), 303: commands: this.generateMoveCommands(pythonLibFiles, 'lib') 304: }); 305: } 306: const scriptFiles: RootFile[] = [ 307: ...categorized.python.filter(f => { 308: const baseName = f.name.replace('.py', ''); 309: return /(migrate|configure|inject|setup|test|script)/.test(baseName); 310: }), 311: ...categorized.shell 312: ]; 313: if (scriptFiles.length > 0) { 314: recommendations.push({ 315: id: 'move_scripts', 316: title: 'Move Scripts to scripts/', 317: description: 'Move utility scripts and shell scripts to scripts/ directory', 318: files: scriptFiles, 319: target_directory: 'scripts', 320: impact: 'low', 321: requires_import_updates: false, 322: commands: this.generateMoveCommands(scriptFiles, 'scripts') 323: }); 324: } 325: if (categorized.data.length > 0) { 326: recommendations.push({ 327: id: 'move_data', 328: title: 'Move Data Files to data/', 329: description: 'Move database and data files to data/ directory', 330: files: categorized.data, 331: target_directory: 'data', 332: impact: 'medium', 333: requires_import_updates: true, 334: path_updates_required: 'Update hardcoded paths to data files', 335: commands: this.generateMoveCommands(categorized.data, 'data') 336: }); 337: } 338: const movableConfigs = categorized.config.filter(f => 339: !['package.json', 'package-lock.json', 'tsconfig.json'].includes(f.name) 340: ); 341: if (movableConfigs.length > this.thresholds.configFiles) { 342: recommendations.push({ 343: id: 'move_configs', 344: title: 'Move Configuration Files to config/', 345: description: 'Move project-specific config files to config/ directory', 346: files: movableConfigs, 347: target_directory: 'config', 348: impact: 'medium', 349: requires_import_updates: true, 350: path_updates_required: 'Update config file path references in code', 351: commands: this.generateMoveCommands(movableConfigs, 'config') 352: }); 353: } 354: const tempFiles = categorized.other.filter(f => 355: f.name.includes('repomix-output') || 356: f.name.endsWith('.log') || 357: f.name.endsWith('.tmp') 358: ); 359: if (tempFiles.length > 0) { 360: recommendations.push({ 361: id: 'remove_temp', 362: title: 'Remove or Move Temporary Files', 363: description: 'Remove generated files or add to .gitignore', 364: files: tempFiles, 365: target_directory: 'docs (or delete)', 366: impact: 'low', 367: requires_import_updates: false, 368: commands: tempFiles.map(f => `mv ${f.name} docs/ # or add to .gitignore`) 369: }); 370: } 371: return recommendations; 372: } 373: calculateImportChanges(files: RootFile[], targetDir: string, dependencies: Dependencies): ImportChange[] { 374: const changes: ImportChange[] = []; 375: for (const file of files) { 376: const dep = dependencies.python[file.name]; 377: if (!dep) continue; 378: for (const importer of dep.imported_by) { 379: const baseName = file.name.replace('.py', ''); 380: changes.push({ 381: file: importer, 382: old_import: `from ${baseName} import`, 383: new_import: `from ${targetDir}.${baseName} import` 384: }); 385: } 386: // This module's imports from other root files need updating 387: for (const imported of dep.imports_from_root) { 388: const match = imported.match(/from\s+([^\s]+)/); 389: if (match) { 390: const module = match[1]; 391: changes.push({ 392: file: file.name, 393: old_import: `from ${module}`, 394: new_import: `from ${targetDir}.${module}` 395: }); 396: } 397: } 398: } 399: return changes; 400: } 401: generateMoveCommands(files: RootFile[], targetDir: string): string[] { 402: return files.map(f => `git mv ${f.name} ${targetDir}/`); 403: } 404: generateReport(analysis: RootDirectoryAnalysis): string { 405: const lines = [ 406: '# Root Directory Cleanup Analysis', 407: '', 408: `**Generated:** ${new Date().toISOString()}`, 409: `**Total Root Files:** ${analysis.statistics.total_root_files}`, 410: `**Reduction Potential:** ${analysis.statistics.reduction_potential} files (${analysis.statistics.reduction_percentage}%)`, 411: `**Final Root Files:** ${analysis.statistics.final_root_files}`, 412: '', 413: '## Current State', 414: '', 415: '### Files by Category', 416: '' 417: ]; class SemanticFeatures: 12: http_status_codes: Set[int] = field(default_factory=set) 13: logical_operators: Set[str] = field(default_factory=set) 14: semantic_methods: Set[str] = field(default_factory=set) 15: def extract_semantic_features(source_code: str) -> SemanticFeatures: 16: features = SemanticFeatures() 17: if not source_code: 18: return features 19: status_pattern = r"\.status\((\d{3})\)" 20: for match in re.finditer(status_pattern, source_code): 21: status_code = int(match.group(1)) 22: features.http_status_codes.add(status_code) 23: # Extract logical operators (===, !==, ==, !=, !, &&, ||) 24: # Order matters: match longer operators first to avoid partial matches 25: operator_patterns = [ 26: (r"!==", "!=="), # Strict inequality 27: (r"===", "==="), # Strict equality 28: (r"!=", "!="), # Loose inequality 29: (r"==", "=="), # Loose equality 30: (r"!\s*[^=]", "!"), # Logical NOT (followed by non-=) 31: (r"&&", "&&"), # Logical AND 32: (r"\|\|", "||"), # Logical OR 33: ] 34: for pattern, operator_name in operator_patterns: 35: if re.search(pattern, source_code): 36: features.logical_operators.add(operator_name) 37: # Extract semantic methods (Math.max, Math.min, console.log, etc.) 38: semantic_patterns = { 39: "Math.max": r"Math\.max\s*\(", 40: "Math.min": r"Math\.min\s*\(", 41: "Math.floor": r"Math\.floor\s*\(", 42: "Math.ceil": r"Math\.ceil\s*\(", 43: "Math.round": r"Math\.round\s*\(", 44: "console.log": r"console\.log\s*\(", 45: "console.error": r"console\.error\s*\(", 46: "console.warn": r"console\.warn\s*\(", 47: ".reverse": r"\.reverse\s*\(", 48: ".toUpperCase": r"\.toUpperCase\s*\(", 49: ".toLowerCase": r"\.toLowerCase\s*\(", 50: } 51: for method_name, pattern in semantic_patterns.items(): 52: if re.search(pattern, source_code): 53: features.semantic_methods.add(method_name) 54: return features 55: def normalize_code(source_code: str) -> str: 56: """ 57: Normalize code by removing variable-specific information. 58: This allows structural comparison by focusing on code structure 59: rather than specific names or values. 60: """ 61: if not source_code: 62: return "" 63: # Remove comments 64: normalized = re.sub( 65: r"//.*?$", "", source_code, flags=re.MULTILINE 66: ) # Single-line comments 67: normalized = re.sub( 68: r"/\*.*?\*/", "", normalized, flags=re.DOTALL 69: ) # Multi-line comments 70: # Normalize whitespace (collapse to single spaces) 71: normalized = re.sub(r"\s+", " ", normalized) 72: # Normalize string literals (replace with placeholder) 73: normalized = re.sub(r"'[^']*'", "'STR'", normalized) 74: normalized = re.sub(r'"[^"]*"', '"STR"', normalized) 75: normalized = re.sub(r"`[^`]*`", "`STR`", normalized) 76: # Normalize numbers (replace with placeholder) 77: normalized = re.sub(r"\b\d+\b", "NUM", normalized) 78: # Semantic operator and method whitelist 79: # These methods have semantic meaning and should be preserved during normalization 80: SEMANTIC_METHODS = { 81: # Array functional methods (already preserved) 82: "map", 83: "filter", 84: "reduce", 85: "forEach", 86: "find", 87: "some", 88: "every", 89: "slice", 90: "splice", 91: "push", 92: "pop", 93: "shift", 94: "unshift", 95: "join", 96: "split", 97: "includes", 98: "indexOf", 99: # Object methods (already preserved) 100: "get", 101: "set", 102: "has", 103: "delete", 104: "keys", 105: "values", 106: "entries", 107: # Async patterns (already preserved) 108: "then", 109: "catch", 110: "finally", 111: "async", 112: "await", 113: # Array transformations (already preserved) 114: "reverse", 115: "sort", 116: "concat", 117: # NEW: Math operations (opposite semantics) 118: "max", 119: "min", 120: "abs", 121: "floor", 122: "ceil", 123: "round", 124: # NEW: String operations (semantic meaning) 125: "trim", 126: "toLowerCase", 127: "toUpperCase", 128: "replace", 129: # NEW: HTTP/API methods (semantic differences) 130: "status", 131: "json", 132: "send", 133: "redirect", 134: # NEW: Properties with semantic value 135: "length", 136: "name", 137: "value", 138: "id", 139: "type", 140: } 141: # Preserve important objects 142: SEMANTIC_OBJECTS = { 143: "Math", 144: "Object", 145: "Array", 146: "String", 147: "Number", 148: "Boolean", 149: "console", 150: "process", 151: "JSON", 152: "Date", 153: "Promise", 154: } 155: # Build a pattern that matches identifiers but NOT important methods/objects 156: # First, mark important objects for preservation (Math, Object, etc.) 157: for obj in SEMANTIC_OBJECTS: 158: normalized = re.sub( 159: rf"\b{obj}\b", f"__PRESERVE_OBJ_{obj.upper()}__", normalized 160: ) 161: # Second, mark important methods so they're not replaced 162: for method in SEMANTIC_METHODS: 163: normalized = re.sub( 164: rf"\b{method}\b", f"__PRESERVE_{method.upper()}__", normalized 165: ) 166: normalized = re.sub(r"\b[a-z][a-zA-Z0-9_]*\b", "var", normalized) 167: normalized = re.sub(r"\b[A-Z][A-Z0-9_]*\b", "CONST", normalized) 168: for obj in SEMANTIC_OBJECTS: 169: normalized = normalized.replace(f"__PRESERVE_OBJ_{obj.upper()}__", obj) 170: for method in SEMANTIC_METHODS: 171: normalized = normalized.replace(f"__PRESERVE_{method.upper()}__", method) 172: normalized = re.sub(r"\s*([(){}[\];,.])\s*", r"\1", normalized) 173: normalized = re.sub(r"\s*(=>|===?|!==?|[+\-*/%<>=&|])\s*", r" \1 ", normalized) 174: # Collapse multiple spaces 175: normalized = re.sub(r"\s+", " ", normalized) 176: # Trim 177: normalized = normalized.strip() 178: return normalized 179: def calculate_ast_hash(source_code: str) -> str: 180: """ 181: Calculate a hash based on normalized code structure. 182: This creates a structural fingerprint that's the same for 183: code with the same structure but different variable names. 184: """ 185: normalized = normalize_code(source_code) 186: return hashlib.sha256(normalized.encode()).hexdigest() 187: def calculate_levenshtein_similarity(str1: str, str2: str) -> float: 188: """ 189: Calculate similarity using Levenshtein distance via SequenceMatcher. 190: Returns a similarity ratio between 0.0 and 1.0. 191: """ 192: if not str1 or not str2: 193: return 0.0 194: return SequenceMatcher(None, str1, str2).ratio() 195: def extract_logical_operators(source_code: str) -> set: 196: """ 197: Extract logical operators from source code for semantic comparison. 198: Returns: 199: Set of logical operators found (e.g., {'===', '!=='}) 200: """ 201: operators = set() 202: # Find all logical operators 203: # Check compound operators first (order matters) 204: if "!==" in source_code: 205: operators.add("!==") 206: if "===" in source_code: 207: operators.add("===") 208: if "!=" in source_code and "!==" not in source_code: 209: operators.add("!=") 210: if "==" in source_code and "===" not in source_code: 211: operators.add("==") 212: # Match standalone ! (not part of !== or !=) 213: if re.search(r"(?<![!=])!(?![=])", source_code): 214: operators.add("!") 215: return operators 216: def extract_http_status_codes(source_code: str) -> set: 217: """ 218: Extract HTTP status codes from response patterns. 219: Returns: 220: Set of HTTP status codes found in response patterns 221: """ 222: status_codes = set() 223: # Pattern: res.status(200), response.status(404), etc. 224: pattern = r"(?:res|response)\.status\((\d{3})\)" 225: matches = re.finditer(pattern, source_code) 226: for match in matches: 227: status_codes.add(int(match.group(1))) 228: return status_codes 229: def extract_semantic_methods(source_code: str) -> set: 230: """ 231: Extract critical semantic methods that indicate opposite or different behavior. 232: Returns: 233: Set of semantic methods found (e.g., {'Math.max', 'Math.min'}) 234: """ 235: methods = set() 236: # Critical Math methods with opposite semantics 237: math_opposites = ["max", "min", "floor", "ceil"] 238: for method in math_opposites: 239: if f"Math.{method}" in source_code: 240: methods.add(f"Math.{method}") 241: return methods 242: def extract_method_chain(source_code: str) -> list: 243: """ 244: Extract method chain structure from code. 245: Returns: 246: List of methods in chain order (e.g., ['filter', 'map', 'reverse']) 247: """ 248: # Pattern: .method1().method2().method3() 249: # Handles both arr.filter().map() and arr.filter(fn).map(fn) 250: chains = [] 251: # Find chained method calls 252: # Pattern: .method_name( ... ).method_name( ... ) 253: pattern = r"\.([a-zA-Z_][a-zA-Z0-9_]*)\s*\(" 254: matches = list(re.finditer(pattern, source_code)) 255: if not matches: 256: return [] 257: # Build chains by tracking consecutive method calls 258: current_chain = [] 259: last_pos = -1 260: for i, match in enumerate(matches): 261: method_name = match.group(1) 262: # Check if this is part of a chain (close to previous match) 263: # Allow up to METHOD_CHAIN_MAX_GAP characters between methods for complex arguments 264: if ( 265: current_chain 266: and match.start() - last_pos > ExtractionDefaults.METHOD_CHAIN_MAX_GAP 267: ): 268: # Too far apart, start a new chain 269: if len(current_chain) > 1: # Only save actual chains (2+ methods) 270: chains.append(current_chain) 271: current_chain = [method_name] 272: else: 273: current_chain.append(method_name) 274: # Find end of this method call (rough approximation) 275: # Look for closing paren or next method 276: if i < len(matches) - 1: 277: last_pos = matches[i + 1].start() 278: else: 279: last_pos = len(source_code) 280: # Add the last chain if it has multiple methods 281: if len(current_chain) > 1: 282: chains.append(current_chain) 283: # Return longest chain (most significant) 284: return max(chains, key=len) if chains else [] 285: def compare_method_chains(code1: str, code2: str) -> float: 286: """ 287: Compare method chain structure between two code blocks. 288: Returns: 289: Similarity score 0.0-1.0 based on chain overlap 290: """ 291: chain1 = extract_method_chain(code1) 292: chain2 = extract_method_chain(code2) 293: if not chain1 and not chain2: 294: return 1.0 # No chains in either 295: if not chain1 or not chain2: 296: return ( 297: StructuralDefaults.CHAIN_MISSING_SIMILARITY 298: ) # One has chain, other doesn't 299: # Exact match 300: if chain1 == chain2: 301: return 1.0 302: # Check if one is a subset of the other (additional operation) 303: if len(chain1) != len(chain2): 304: # Different length chains → likely different behavior 305: # e.g., [filter, map] vs [filter, map, reverse] 306: # Check if shorter is prefix of longer 307: shorter = chain1 if len(chain1) < len(chain2) else chain2 308: longer = chain1 if len(chain1) > len(chain2) else chain2 309: if longer[: len(shorter)] == shorter: 310: # One is extension of other → partial match 311: # Similarity based on overlap ratio 312: return len(shorter) / len(longer) 313: else: 314: # Different chains entirely 315: return 0.0 316: # Same length but different methods → check overlap 317: overlap = sum(1 for m1, m2 in zip(chain1, chain2) if m1 == m2) 318: return overlap / len(chain1) 319: def calculate_semantic_penalty( 320: features1: SemanticFeatures, features2: SemanticFeatures 321: ) -> float: 322: """ 323: Calculate combined semantic penalty based on extracted features. 324: Penalties are multiplicative - each mismatch reduces similarity: 325: - HTTP status codes: STATUS_CODE_PENALTY multiplier 326: - Logical operators: LOGIC_OPERATOR_PENALTY multiplier 327: - Semantic methods: SEMANTIC_METHOD_PENALTY multiplier 328: Args: 329: features1: Semantic features from first code block 330: features2: Semantic features from second code block 331: Returns: 332: Penalty multiplier (0.0-1.0). Returns 1.0 if no penalties apply. 333: Example: 334: Different status codes + different operators (=== vs !==) 335: = STATUS_CODE_PENALTY * LOGIC_OPERATOR_PENALTY final similarity 336: """ 337: penalty = 1.0 338: # Penalty 1: HTTP Status Code Mismatch (30% penalty) 339: if features1.http_status_codes and features2.http_status_codes: 340: if features1.http_status_codes != features2.http_status_codes: 341: # Different status codes indicate different semantic intent 342: # (e.g., 200 OK vs 201 Created vs 404 Not Found) 343: penalty *= StructuralDefaults.STATUS_CODE_PENALTY 344: # Penalty 2: Logical Operator Mismatch (20% penalty) 345: if features1.logical_operators and features2.logical_operators: 346: if features1.logical_operators != features2.logical_operators: 347: # Different logical operators indicate different boolean logic 348: # (e.g., === vs !==, && vs ||) 349: penalty *= StructuralDefaults.LOGIC_OPERATOR_PENALTY 350: # Penalty 3: Semantic Method Mismatch (25% penalty) 351: if features1.semantic_methods and features2.semantic_methods: 352: if features1.semantic_methods != features2.semantic_methods: 353: # Different semantic methods indicate different operations 354: # (e.g., Math.max vs Math.min, toUpperCase vs toLowerCase) 355: penalty *= StructuralDefaults.SEMANTIC_METHOD_PENALTY 356: return penalty 357: def calculate_structural_similarity( 358: code1: str, 359: code2: str, 360: threshold: float = StructuralDefaults.DEFAULT_SIMILARITY_THRESHOLD, 361: ) -> Tuple[float, str]: 362: """ 363: Calculate structural similarity between two code blocks using unified penalty system. 364: Priority: Structural Similarity (structural layer of multi-layer algorithm) 365: Returns: 366: (similarity_score, method) 367: - similarity_score: 0.0 to 1.0 368: - method: 'exact', 'structural', or 'different' 369: Algorithm (multi-phase flow): 370: - Exact match: Compare hashes → 1.0 similarity 371: - Extract semantic features from ORIGINAL code (BEFORE normalization) 372: - Normalize code and calculate base structural similarity 373: - Apply unified semantic penalties using original features 374: - Return final similarity score and method 375: """ 376: if not code1 or not code2: 377: return 0.0, "different" 378: hash1 = hashlib.sha256(code1.encode()).hexdigest() 379: hash2 = hashlib.sha256(code2.encode()).hexdigest() 380: if hash1 == hash2: 381: return 1.0, "exact" 382: features1 = extract_semantic_features(code1) 383: features2 = extract_semantic_features(code2) 384: normalized1 = normalize_code(code1) 385: normalized2 = normalize_code(code2) 386: if normalized1 == normalized2: 387: base_similarity = ( 388: StructuralDefaults.NORMALIZED_IDENTICAL 389: ) 390: else: 391: base_similarity = calculate_levenshtein_similarity(normalized1, normalized2) 392: chain_similarity = compare_method_chains(code1, code2) 393: if chain_similarity < 1.0: 394: base_similarity = ( 395: base_similarity * StructuralDefaults.CHAIN_LEVENSHTEIN_WEIGHT 396: ) + (chain_similarity * StructuralDefaults.CHAIN_STRUCTURE_WEIGHT) 397: penalty = calculate_semantic_penalty(features1, features2) 398: final_similarity = base_similarity * penalty 399: if final_similarity >= threshold: 400: return final_similarity, "structural" 401: else: 402: return final_similarity, "different" 403: def are_structurally_similar( 404: code1: str, 405: code2: str, 406: threshold: float = StructuralDefaults.DEFAULT_SIMILARITY_THRESHOLD, 407: ) -> bool: 408: score, _ = calculate_structural_similarity(code1, code2, threshold) 409: return score >= threshold 1: """Type stubs for structural similarity module.""" 2: 3: from dataclasses import dataclass 4: from typing import Set, Tuple 5: 6: @dataclass 7: class SemanticFeatures: 8: """Semantic features extracted from original code before 43: } 44: const files = await fs.readdir(this.cacheDir); 45: const secretFiles = files.filter(f => f.startsWith('.secrets-') && f.endsWith('.json')); 46: if (secretFiles.length === 0) { 47: logger.warn({ cacheDir: this.cacheDir }, 'Doppler fallback directory exists but contains no secret files'); 48: return { 49: healthy: false, 50: cacheAgeMs: 0, 51: cacheAgeHours: 0, 52: cacheAgeMinutes: 0, 53: lastModified: null, 54: usingFallback: false, 55: fileCount: 0, 56: severity: 'warning' 57: }; 58: } 59: let newestFile: string | null = null; 66: newestFile = file; 67: } 68: } 69: const cacheAge = Date.now() - newestMtime; 70: const cacheAgeHours = Math.floor(cacheAge / TIME.HOUR); 71: const cacheAgeMinutes = Math.floor((cacheAge % (60 * 60 * 1000)) / TIME.MINUTE); 72: const status: CacheHealthStatus = { 73: healthy: cacheAge <= this.maxCacheAge, 74: cacheAgeMs: cacheAge, 75: cacheAgeHours, 76: cacheAgeMinutes, 77: lastModified: new Date(newestMtime).toISOString(), 78: newestFile: newestFile ?? undefined, 79: fileCount: secretFiles.length, 80: usingFallback: true, 81: severity: this.getSeverity(cacheAge) 82: }; 83: if (cacheAge > this.maxCacheAge) { 84: logger.error({ 85: cacheAgeHours, 86: lastModified: new Date(newestMtime), 87: newestFile, 88: fileCount: secretFiles.length 89: }, 'Doppler cache is critically stale - secrets may be outdated'); 90: Sentry.captureMessage('Doppler cache critically stale', { 91: level: 'error', 92: extra: { 93: cacheAgeHours, 94: cacheAgeMinutes, 95: lastModified: new Date(newestMtime).toISOString(), 96: threshold: this.maxCacheAge / TIME.HOUR, 97: newestFile, 98: fileCount: secretFiles.length 99: }, 100: tags: { 101: component: 'doppler-health-monitor', 102: severity: 'critical' 103: } 104: }); 105: } else if (cacheAge > this.warningThreshold) { 106: logger.warn({ 107: cacheAgeHours, 108: lastModified: new Date(newestMtime), 109: newestFile 110: }, 'Doppler cache is aging - approaching staleness threshold'); 111: Sentry.captureMessage('Doppler cache aging', { 112: level: 'warning', 113: extra: { 114: cacheAgeHours, 115: cacheAgeMinutes, 116: lastModified: new Date(newestMtime).toISOString(), 117: threshold: this.warningThreshold / TIME.HOUR, 118: newestFile, 119: fileCount: secretFiles.length 120: }, 121: tags: { 122: component: 'doppler-health-monitor', 123: severity: 'warning' 124: } 125: }); 126: } else { 127: logger.debug({ 128: cacheAgeHours, 129: cacheAgeMinutes, 130: fileCount: secretFiles.length 131: }, 'Doppler cache health check - within thresholds'); 132: } 133: return status; 134: } catch (error) { 135: const nodeError = error as NodeJS.ErrnoException; 136: if (nodeError.code === 'ENOENT') { 137: logger.debug({ cacheDir: this.cacheDir }, 'No Doppler fallback cache found - likely using live API only'); 138: return { 139: healthy: true, 140: cacheAgeMs: 0, 141: cacheAgeHours: 0, 142: cacheAgeMinutes: 0, 143: lastModified: null, 144: usingFallback: false, 145: fileCount: 0, 146: severity: 'healthy' 147: }; 148: } 149: logError(logger, error, 'Failed to check Doppler cache health', { cacheDir: this.cacheDir }); 150: Sentry.captureException(error, { 151: tags: { 152: component: 'doppler-health-monitor', 153: operation: 'check-cache-health' 154: } 155: }); 156: return { 157: healthy: false, 158: error: nodeError.message, 159: errorCode: nodeError.code, 160: severity: 'error', 161: cacheAgeMs: 0, 162: cacheAgeHours: 0, 163: cacheAgeMinutes: 0, 164: lastModified: null, 165: usingFallback: false, 166: fileCount: 0 167: }; 168: } 169: } 170: getSeverity(cacheAge: number): CacheSeverity { 171: if (cacheAge > this.maxCacheAge) { 172: return 'critical'; 173: } else if (cacheAge > this.warningThreshold) { 174: return 'warning'; 542: cross_repository_duplicates?: CrossRepositoryDuplicate[]; 543: cross_repository_suggestions?: CrossRepositorySuggestion[]; 544: metrics?: ScanMetrics; 545: }; 546: const results: Array<ScanResult | { error: string; repository_path: string }> = []; 547: for (const repoScan of interProjectResult.repository_scans) { 548: if (repoScan.error) { 549: results.push({ 550: error: repoScan.error, 551: repository_path: repoScan.repository_path 552: }); 553: } else if (repoScan.scan_result) { 554: results.push(repoScan.scan_result); 555: } 556: } 557: return { 558: repositories: results, 559: total_scanned: repoPaths.length, 560: successful: results.filter(r => !('error' in r)).length, 561: failed: results.filter(r => 'error' in r).length, 562: cross_repository_duplicates: interProjectResult.cross_repository_duplicates, 563: cross_repository_suggestions: interProjectResult.cross_repository_suggestions, 564: scan_type: 'inter-project', 565: metrics: interProjectResult.metrics 566: }; 567: } catch (error) { 568: logger.error({ error }, 'Multi-repository scan failed'); 569: logger.warn('Falling back to individual repository scans without cross-repository analysis'); 570: const results: Array<ScanResult | { error: string; repository_path: string }> = []; 571: for (const repoPath of repoPaths) { 572: try { 573: const result = await this.scanRepository(repoPath, scanConfig); 574: results.push(result); 575: } catch (scanError) { 576: logger.warn({ repoPath, error: scanError }, 'Repository scan failed, continuing'); 577: results.push({ 578: error: (scanError as Error).message, 579: repository_path: repoPath 580: }); 581: } 582: } 583: return { 584: repositories: results, 585: total_scanned: repoPaths.length, 586: successful: results.filter(r => !('error' in r)).length, 587: failed: results.filter(r => 'error' in r).length, 588: scan_type: 'single-project' 589: }; 590: } 591: } 592: } 593: export class ScanError extends Error { 594: constructor(message: string, options?: { cause?: unknown }) { 595: super(message); 596: if (options?.cause) { 597: (this as unknown as Record<string, unknown>).cause = options.cause; 598: } 599: this.name = 'ScanError'; 600: } 601: } 1: # Universal Repository Cleanup Script 2: 3: **Location:** `~/code/jobs/sidequest/universal-repo-cleanup.sh` 4: **Created:** 2025-11-17 5: **Purpose:** Clean up common bloat from any repository 6: 7: ## Quick Start 8: 9: ```bash 10: # Clean current directory 11: ~/code/jobs/sidequest/universal-repo-cleanup.sh 12: 13: # Clean specific directory 14: ~/code/jobs/sidequest/universal-repo-cleanup.sh /path/to/repo 15: 16: # Clean a project 17: ~/code/jobs/sidequest/universal-repo-cleanup.sh ~/projects/myapp 18: ``` 19: 20: ## What It Does 21: 22: This script automatically detects and removes common bloat from repositories: 23: 24: ### 1. Python Virtual Environments 25: Removes directories matching common venv patterns: 26: - `venv/`, `.venv/`, `env/`, `.env/` 27: - `virtualenv/`, `*.venv/` 28: - Custom patterns (e.g., `personal_site/`) 29: 30: ### 2. Temporary/Cache Files 31: Removes system and editor temporary files: 32: - `.DS_Store` (macOS) 33: - `__pycache__/`, `*.pyc`, `*.pyo` (Python) 34: - `*.swp`, `*.swo`, `*~` (Vim/editor) 35: - `Thumbs.db`, `desktop.ini` (Windows) 36: 37: ### 3. Output/Generated Files 38: Removes generated output files: 39: - `repomix-output.xml` (nested, keeps root) 40: - `*.log`, `npm-debug.log*` 41: - `yarn-debug.log*`, `yarn-error.log*` 42: 43: ### 4. Build Artifacts 44: Removes common build directories: 45: - `.jekyll-cache`, `.sass-cache`, `.bundle` (Jekyll) 46: - `dist/`, `build/`, `out/`, `.output/` (Build outputs) 47: - `.next/`, `.nuxt/` (Framework caches) 48: - `node_modules/.cache/` (NPM cache) 49: - `target/`, `.gradle/` (Java/Gradle) 50: 51: ### 5. Redundant Directories 52: Removes common redundant directories: 53: - `drafts/`, `temp/`, `tmp/` 54: - `backup/`, `backups/`, `old/` 55: - `archive/`, `deprecated/` 56: 57: ## Features 58: 59: ✅ **Safe & Interactive** 60: - Preview what will be removed before deletion 61: - Shows sizes and counts 62: - Requires explicit "yes" confirmation 63: 64: ✅ **Intelligent Scanning** 65: - Skips `node_modules/` when looking for venvs 66: - Preserves root-level `repomix-output.xml` 67: - Detects `.gitignore` entries 68: 69: ✅ **Universal Compatibility** 70: - Works on any repository type 71: - Configurable patterns 72: - Language agnostic 73: 74: ✅ **Visual Feedback** 75: - Color-coded output (green/yellow/red/blue) 76: - Progress indicators (✓ ✗ ⚠ →) 77: - Size calculations for removed items 78: 79: ## Usage Examples 80: 81: ### Example 1: Personal Website 82: ```bash 83: cd ~/projects/PersonalSite 84: ~/code/jobs/sidequest/universal-repo-cleanup.sh 85: ``` 86: 87: **Output:** 88: ``` 89: ======================================== 90: Repository Cleanup Preview 91: ======================================== 92: 93: Target Directory: /Users/you/projects/PersonalSite 94: Current Size: 616M 95: 96: Python Virtual Environments (2 found): 97: - personal_site (40M) 98: - utils/numpy (42M) 99: 100: Temporary/Cache Files (3 found): 101: - 3 files (.DS_Store, __pycache__, .swp, etc.) 102: 103: Build Artifacts (2 found): 104: - .jekyll-cache (5M) 105: - dist (123K) 106: 107: Total items to remove: 7 108: 109: Do you want to proceed with cleanup? (yes/no): 110: ``` 111: 112: ### Example 2: React Project 113: ```bash 114: ~/code/jobs/sidequest/universal-repo-cleanup.sh ~/projects/my-react-app 115: ``` 116: 117: Cleans: 118: - `.next/` cache 119: - `dist/` build output 120: - `.DS_Store` files 121: - `npm-debug.log` files 122: 123: ### Example 3: Python Project 124: ```bash 125: ~/code/jobs/sidequest/universal-repo-cleanup.sh ~/projects/django-app 126: ``` 127: 128: Cleans: 129: - `venv/`, `.venv/` directories 130: - `__pycache__/` directories 131: - `*.pyc` files 132: - `*.log` files 133: 134: ## Customization 135: 136: Edit the script to customize what gets cleaned: 137: 138: ```bash 139: # Add custom venv patterns 140: VENV_PATTERNS=( 141: "venv" 142: ".venv" 143: "your_custom_venv_name" # Add here 144: ) 145: 146: # Add custom build artifacts 147: BUILD_ARTIFACTS=( 148: "dist" 149: "build" 150: "your_build_dir" # Add here 151: ) 152: 153: # Add custom redundant directories 154: REDUNDANT_DIRS=( 155: "drafts" 156: "temp" 157: "your_backup_dir" # Add here 158: ) 159: ``` 160: 161: ## Safety Features 162: 163: ### Preview Before Deletion 164: Always shows what will be removed with sizes before asking for confirmation 165: 166: ### No Accidental Deletions 167: - Requires explicit "yes" to proceed 168: - Exit codes on errors (`set -e`) 169: - Validates directory exists 170: 171: ### Preserves Important Files 172: - Skips `node_modules/` when scanning 173: - Keeps root-level `repomix-output.xml` 174: - Respects `.gitignore` patterns 175: 176: ### Error Handling 177: - Validates target directory exists 178: - Handles missing files gracefully 179: - Reports errors clearly 180: 181: ## Output Format 182: 183: ### Summary Report 184: ``` 185: ======================================== 186: Cleanup Summary 187: ======================================== 188: 189: Cleanup completed successfully! 190: 191: Target Directory: /Users/you/projects/repo 192: Final Size: 150M 193: 194: Cleaned up: 195: ✓ Python virtual environments 196: ✓ Temporary/cache files (.DS_Store, __pycache__, etc.) 197: ✓ Output/generated files (logs, repomix files, etc.) 198: ✓ Build artifacts (.jekyll-cache, dist/, etc.) 199: ✓ Redundant directories (drafts/, temp/, backup/, etc.) 200: ``` 201: 202: ### Recommendations 203: ``` 204: ======================================== 205: Recommendations 206: ======================================== 207: 208: Consider adding these patterns to .gitignore if not already present: 209: 210: # Python 211: venv/ 212: .venv/ 213: *.pyc 214: __pycache__/ 215: 216: # System files 217: .DS_Store 218: Thumbs.db 219: 220: # Build artifacts 221: dist/ 222: build/ 223: *.log 224: ``` 225: 226: ## Common Use Cases 227: 228: ### 1. Before Committing 229: Clean up before committing to ensure no build artifacts or venvs are added: 230: ```bash 231: ~/code/jobs/sidequest/universal-repo-cleanup.sh 232: git status 233: ``` 234: 235: ### 2. After Cloning 236: Clean up a cloned repo that has committed bloat: 237: ```bash 238: git clone https://github.com/user/repo 239: cd repo 240: ~/code/jobs/sidequest/universal-repo-cleanup.sh 241: ``` 242: 243: ### 3. Periodic Maintenance 244: Run monthly to keep repos clean: 245: ```bash 246: # Add to cron or run manually 247: ~/code/jobs/sidequest/universal-repo-cleanup.sh ~/projects/myapp 248: ``` 249: 250: ### 4. Disk Space Recovery 251: Find and clean the biggest repos: 252: ```bash 253: # Find large directories 254: du -sh ~/projects/* | sort -hr | head -10 255: 256: # Clean the largest 257: ~/code/jobs/sidequest/universal-repo-cleanup.sh ~/projects/largest-repo 258: ``` 259: 260: ## Integration with Git 261: 262: ### Pre-commit Hook 263: Add to `.git/hooks/pre-commit`: 264: ```bash 265: #!/bin/bash 266: ~/code/jobs/sidequest/universal-repo-cleanup.sh "$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)" 267: ``` 268: 269: ### Alias 270: Add to `~/.bashrc` or `~/.zshrc`: 271: ```bash 272: alias cleanup='~/code/jobs/sidequest/universal-repo-cleanup.sh' 273: 274: # Usage: 275: # cd my-repo 276: # cleanup 277: ``` 278: 279: ## Comparison with Original Script 280: 281: | Feature | Original (PersonalSite) | Universal | 282: |---------|------------------------|-----------| 283: | Target | Single repo | Any directory | 284: | Patterns | Jekyll-specific | Universal | 285: | Usage | `./utils/cleanup-repository.sh` | `script.sh [directory]` | 286: | Customization | Hardcoded | Configurable arrays | 287: | Scanning | Manual checks | Automatic detection | 288: | Portability | Project-specific | Portable | 289: 290: ## Troubleshooting 291: 292: ### "Directory does not exist" 293: ```bash 294: # Check the path 295: ls -la /path/to/repo 296: 297: # Use absolute path 298: ~/code/jobs/sidequest/universal-repo-cleanup.sh /full/path/to/repo 299: ``` 300: 301: ### "No items found to clean" 302: Repository is already clean! This is normal if: 303: - Already ran the script 304: - Repo has good .gitignore 305: - No bloat present 306: 307: ### Script shows items but doesn't remove 308: Make sure you typed "yes" exactly (case-sensitive): 309: ``` 310: Do you want to proceed with cleanup? (yes/no): yes 311: ``` 312: 313: ### Want to skip confirmation (dangerous) 314: ```bash 315: # Auto-confirm (use with caution!) 316: echo "yes" | ~/code/jobs/sidequest/universal-repo-cleanup.sh /path/to/repo 317: ``` 318: 319: ## Advanced Usage 320: 321: ### Dry Run (Preview Only) 322: The script always shows preview first - just answer "no" to confirmation: 323: ``` 324: Do you want to proceed with cleanup? (yes/no): no 325: ``` 326: 327: ### Clean Multiple Repos 328: ```bash 329: #!/bin/bash 330: for repo in ~/projects/*; do 331: if [ -d "$repo/.git" ]; then 332: echo "Cleaning $repo..." 333: echo "yes" | ~/code/jobs/sidequest/universal-repo-cleanup.sh "$repo" 334: fi 335: done 336: ``` 337: 338: ### Selective Cleanup 339: Comment out functions in `main()` to skip categories: 340: ```bash 341: main() { 342: # ... 343: cleanup_venvs 344: cleanup_temp_files 345: # cleanup_output_files # Skip this 346: # cleanup_build_artifacts # Skip this 347: cleanup_redundant_dirs 348: # ... 349: } 350: ``` 351: 352: ## Performance 353: 354: ### Speed 355: - Small repos (<100MB): ~1-2 seconds 356: - Medium repos (500MB): ~5-10 seconds 357: - Large repos (1GB+): ~30-60 seconds 358: 359: ### Resource Usage 360: - Memory: Minimal (<10MB) 361: - CPU: Low (scanning files) 362: - Disk: Frees up space 363: 364: ## Best Practices 365: 366: 1. **Run before committing** - Clean before adding files to git 367: 2. **Review preview** - Always check what will be removed 368: 3. **Update .gitignore** - Add patterns to prevent reintroduction 369: 4. **Backup first** - For unfamiliar repos, backup before running 370: 5. **Test builds** - After cleanup, test that builds still work 371: 372: ## Version History 373: 374: | Version | Date | Changes | 375: |---------|------|---------| 376: | 1.0.0 | 2025-11-17 | Initial universal version from PersonalSite cleanup script | 377: 378: ## Related Scripts 379: 380: - **Original:** `/Users/alyshialedlie/code/PersonalSite/utils/cleanup-repository.sh` 381: - PersonalSite-specific version 382: - More detailed verification 383: - Jekyll-focused 384: 385: - **Universal:** `~/code/jobs/sidequest/universal-repo-cleanup.sh` 386: - Works on any repo 387: - Configurable patterns 388: - Portable 389: 390: ## Support 391: 392: For issues or improvements: 393: 1. Review this documentation 394: 2. Check script comments for customization 395: 3. Edit pattern arrays for specific needs 396: 4. 50: const DESCRIPTION_MIN_TEXT_LENGTH = 10; 51: const HOWTO_TYPE = 'HowTo'; 52: const API_REFERENCE_TYPE = 'APIReference'; 53: const SOFTWARE_APP_TYPE = 'SoftwareApplication'; 54: const SOFTWARE_SOURCE_TYPE = 'SoftwareSourceCode'; 55: const TECH_ARTICLE_TYPE = 'TechArticle'; 56: function inferSchemaType(readmePath: string, content: string, context: SchemaContext): string { 57: const pathLower = readmePath.toLowerCase(); 58: const contentLower = content.toLowerCase(); 59: if (pathLower.includes('test') || contentLower.includes('testing guide')) { 60: return HOWTO_TYPE; 61: } 62: if (pathLower.includes('api') || contentLower.includes('api reference') || contentLower.includes('endpoints')) { 63: return API_REFERENCE_TYPE; 64: } 65: if (context.hasPackageJson || context.hasPyproject) { 66: return SOFTWARE_APP_TYPE; 67: } 68: if (contentLower.includes('tutorial') || contentLower.includes('getting started') || contentLower.includes('guide')) { 69: return HOWTO_TYPE; 70: } 71: if (context.gitRemote) { 72: return SOFTWARE_SOURCE_TYPE; 73: } 74: return TECH_ARTICLE_TYPE; 75: } 76: function getSchemaName(readmePath: string, content: string): string { 77: const titleMatch = content.match(/^#\s+(.+)$/m); 87: } 88: if (hasDescription) { 89: return true; 90: } 91: return false; 92: } 93: function truncateDescription(description: string): string { 94: if (description.length <= DESCRIPTION_MAX_LENGTH) { 95: return description; 96: } 97: return description.substring(0, DESCRIPTION_TRUNCATED_LENGTH) + '...'; 98: } 99: function extractDescriptionFromContent(content: string): string { 100: const lines = content.split('\n'); 101: let foundTitle = false; 102: let description = ''; 103: for (const line of lines) { 104: const trimmed = line.trim(); 105: if (trimmed.startsWith('#')) { 106: foundTitle = true; 107: continue; 108: } 109: if (isDescriptionLineSkippable(trimmed, Boolean(description))) { 110: if (description) { 111: break; 128: } 129: if (context.gitRemote) { 130: schema.codeRepository = context.gitRemote; 131: } 132: if (context.languages && context.languages.length > 0) { 133: schema.programmingLanguage = context.languages.map(language => ({ 134: '@type': 'ComputerLanguage', 135: name: language 136: })); 137: } 138: if (schemaType === SOFTWARE_APP_TYPE) { 139: schema.applicationCategory = 'DeveloperApplication'; 140: schema.operatingSystem = 'Cross-platform'; 141: } 142: } 143: function addArticleSchemaFields(schema: SchemaObject, schemaType: string): void { 144: if (schemaType !== TECH_ARTICLE_TYPE && schemaType !== HOWTO_TYPE) { 145: return; 182: } 183: if (!schema.description) { 184: warnings.push('Missing description property'); 185: } 186: try { 187: JSON.stringify(schema); 188: } catch (error) { 189: errors.push(`Invalid JSON: ${(error as Error).message}`); 199: if (schema.name) { 200: improvements.push('Added structured name/title'); 201: } 202: if (schema.description) { 203: improvements.push('Added structured description'); 204: } 205: if (schema.codeRepository) { 206: improvements.push('Linked to code repository'); 207: } 208: if (schema.programmingLanguage) { 209: improvements.push('Specified programming languages'); 210: } 211: return improvements; 212: } 213: function collectRichResultsEligibility(schemaType: string): string[] { 214: const richResults: string[] = []; 215: if (schemaType === HOWTO_TYPE) { 216: richResults.push('How-to rich results'); 217: } 218: if (schemaType === SOFTWARE_APP_TYPE) { 219: richResults.push('Software app rich results'); 220: } 221: if (schemaType === TECH_ARTICLE_TYPE) { 222: richResults.push('Article rich results'); 223: } 224: return richResults; 225: } 226: function computeImpactScore(impact: SchemaImpact, schema: SchemaObject): number { 227: let score = 0; 228: score += impact.seoImprovements.length * 15; 229: score += impact.richResultsEligibility.length * 20; 237: } 238: if (score >= 60) { 239: return 'Good'; 240: } 241: if (score >= 40) { 242: return 'Fair'; 243: } 244: return 'Needs Improvement'; 245: } 246: function analyzeSchemaImpactData(originalContent: string, enhancedContent: string, schema: SchemaObject): SchemaImpact { 247: const schemaType = schema['@type']; 248: const impact: SchemaImpact = { 249: timestamp: new Date().toISOString(), 250: schemaType, 251: metrics: { 252: contentSize: { 253: original: originalContent.length, 254: enhanced: enhancedContent.length, 255: increase: enhancedContent.length - originalContent.length 256: }, 257: schemaProperties: Object.keys(schema).length, 258: structuredDataAdded: true 259: }, 260: seoImprovements: collectSeoImprovements(schema), 261: richResultsEligibility: collectRichResultsEligibility(schemaType) 262: }; 263: const impactScore = computeImpactScore(impact, schema); 264: impact.impactScore = impactScore; 265: impact.rating = getRatingForScore(impactScore); 266: return impact; 267: } 268: function createJsonLdScriptTag(schema: SchemaObject): string { 269: const jsonString = JSON.stringify(schema, null, 2); 149: results.stages.push({ name: 'bug-detective', status: 'completed', output: detectiveResult.stdout }); 150: await fs.writeFile(path.join(outputDir, '02-bug-detective-report.md'), detectiveResult.stdout); 160: logStage(logger, '4/5: Running ceo-quality-controller-agent'); 161: const qualityPrompt = 'Review all audit reports and validate quality standards before implementing fixes'; 162: const qualityResult = await this.runClaudeAgent('ceo-quality-controller-agent', qualityPrompt, repoPath); 163: results.stages.push({ name: 'ceo-quality-controller', status: 'completed', output: qualityResult.stdout }); 164: await fs.writeFile(path.join(outputDir, '04-quality-control.md'), qualityResult.stdout); 170: logStage(logger, '5/5: Running refractor plugin to implement fixes'); 171: const refractorResult = await this.runSlashCommand('/refractor:refractor', repoPath); 172: results.stages.push({ name: 'refractor', status: 'completed', output: refractorResult.stdout }); 153: this.thresholds = { 154: maxPlugins: 30, 155: warnPlugins: 20, 156: maxHookExecutionTime: TIMEOUTS.POLL_INTERVAL_MS, 157: minDiskSpace: 1024 * 1024 * 100, 158: maxLogSize: 1024 * 1024 * 10 159: }; 171: const recommendations = this.generateRecommendations(analysis); 172: const result: HealthCheckResult = { 173: success: true, 174: timestamp: new Date().toISOString(), 175: duration: Date.now() - startTime, 176: checks, 177: analysis, 178: recommendations, 179: summary: this.generateSummary(analysis, recommendations) 180: }; 439: } catch { 440: counts.activeTasks = 0; 441: } 442: const archiveDir = path.join(this.devDir, 'archive'); 443: try { 444: const archivedTasks = await fs.readdir(archiveDir); 445: counts.archivedTasks = archivedTasks.filter(f => !f.startsWith('.')).length; 228: } 229: } 230: // Find duplicated assertions (3+ occurrences) 231: for (const [assertion, count] of assertionCounts) { 232: if (count >= 3) { 233: result.patterns.duplicateAssertions.push(assertion); 234: } 235: } 236: // Generate recommendations 237: if (result.patterns.renderWaitFor > 5) { 238: result.recommendations.push('Create renderAndWait helper to reduce render + waitFor boilerplate'); 239: } 240: if (result.patterns.linkValidation > 5) { 241: result.recommendations.push('Create link assertion helpers (expectExternalLink, expectInternalLink, etc.)'); 242: } 243: if (result.patterns.semanticChecks > 5) { 244: result.recommendations.push('Create semantic validators (expectSectionWithId, expectHeadingLevel, etc.)'); 245: } 246: if (result.patterns.formInteractions > 5) { 247: result.recommendations.push('Create form helpers (fillContactForm, expectFormAccessibility, etc.)'); 248: } 249: if (result.patterns.hardcodedStrings.length > 10) { 250: result.recommendations.push('Extract hardcoded strings to test-constants.ts'); 251: } 252: return result; 253: } 254: /** 255: * Generate utility files based on analysis 256: */ 257: async generateUtilityFiles( 258: projectPath: string, 259: utilsDir: string, 260: e2eDir: string, 261: framework: string, 262: analysis: Analysis 263: ): Promise<string[]> { 264: const utilsPath = path.join(projectPath, utilsDir); 265: const generatedFiles: string[] = []; 266: // Ensure utils directory exists 267: await fs.mkdir(utilsPath, { recursive: true }); 268: // Generate assertions.ts 269: if (analysis.patterns.linkValidation > 0) { 270: const assertionsPath = path.join(utilsPath, 'assertions.ts'); 271: if (!await this.fileExists(assertionsPath)) { 272: await fs.writeFile(assertionsPath, this.generateAssertionsContent(framework)); 273: generatedFiles.push('assertions.ts'); 274: } 275: } 276: // Generate semantic-validators.ts 277: if (analysis.patterns.semanticChecks > 0) { 278: const validatorsPath = path.join(utilsPath, 'semantic-validators.ts'); 279: if (!await this.fileExists(validatorsPath)) { 280: await fs.writeFile(validatorsPath, this.generateSemanticValidatorsContent(framework)); 281: generatedFiles.push('semantic-validators.ts'); 282: } 283: } 284: // Generate form-helpers.ts 285: if (analysis.patterns.formInteractions > 0) { 286: const formHelpersPath = path.join(utilsPath, 'form-helpers.ts'); 287: if (!await this.fileExists(formHelpersPath)) { 288: await fs.writeFile(formHelpersPath, this.generateFormHelpersContent()); 289: generatedFiles.push('form-helpers.ts'); 290: } 291: } 292: // Generate test-constants.ts 293: if (analysis.patterns.hardcodedStrings.length > 0) { 294: const constantsPath = path.join(utilsPath, 'test-constants.ts'); 295: if (!await this.fileExists(constantsPath)) { 296: await fs.writeFile(constantsPath, this.generateConstantsContent(analysis.patterns.hardcodedStrings)); 297: generatedFiles.push('test-constants.ts'); 298: } 299: } 300: // Generate index.ts 301: const indexPath = path.join(utilsPath, 'index.ts'); 302: if (!await this.fileExists(indexPath) && generatedFiles.length > 0) { 303: await fs.writeFile(indexPath, this.generateIndexContent(generatedFiles)); 304: generatedFiles.push('index.ts'); 305: } 306: // Generate E2E fixtures if e2e directory exists 307: const e2ePath = path.join(projectPath, e2eDir); 308: if (await this.fileExists(e2ePath)) { 309: const fixturesPath = path.join(e2ePath, 'fixtures'); 310: await fs.mkdir(fixturesPath, { recursive: true }); 311: const navFixturesPath = path.join(fixturesPath, 'navigation.ts'); 312: if (!await this.fileExists(navFixturesPath)) { 313: await fs.writeFile(navFixturesPath, this.generateE2EFixturesContent()); 314: generatedFiles.push('e2e/fixtures/navigation.ts'); 315: } 316: } 317: return generatedFiles; 318: } 319: /** 320: * Check if file exists 321: */ 322: async fileExists(filePath: string): Promise<boolean> { 323: try { 324: await fs.access(filePath); 325: return true; 326: } catch { 327: return false; 328: } 329: } 330: /** 331: * Generate assertions.ts content 332: */ 333: generateAssertionsContent(framework: string): string { 334: const importStatement = framework === 'vitest' 335: ? "import { expect } from 'vitest';" 336: : "import { expect } from '@jest/globals';"; 337: return `/** 338: * Test Assertions Utilities 339: * Generated by AlephAuto TestRefactorWorker 340: */ 341: ${importStatement} 342: /** 343: * expectExternalLink. 344: */ 345: export function expectExternalLink(element: HTMLElement, href: string) { 346: const link = element.closest('a'); 347: expect(link).toHaveAttribute('href', href); 348: expect(link).toHaveAttribute('target', '_blank'); 349: expect(link).toHaveAttribute('rel', 'noopener noreferrer'); 350: } 351: /** 352: * expectInternalLink. 353: */ 354: export function expectInternalLink(element: HTMLElement, href: string) { 355: const link = element.closest('a'); 356: expect(link).toHaveAttribute('href', href); 357: } 358: /** 359: * expectMailtoLink. 360: */ 361: export function expectMailtoLink(element: HTMLElement, email: string) { 362: const link = element.closest('a'); 363: expect(link).toHaveAttribute('href', \`mailto:\${email}\`); 364: } 365: /** 366: * expectSectionLink. 367: */ 368: export function expectSectionLink(element: HTMLElement, sectionId: string) { 369: expectInternalLink(element, \`#\${sectionId}\`); 370: } 371: /** 372: * expectAriaLabel. 373: */ 374: export function expectAriaLabel(element: HTMLElement, label: string) { 375: expect(element).toHaveAttribute('aria-label', label); 376: } 377: `; 378: } 379: /** 380: * Generate semantic-validators.ts content 381: */ 382: generateSemanticValidatorsContent(framework: string): string { 383: const importStatement = framework === 'vitest' 384: ? "import { expect } from 'vitest';" 385: : "import { expect } from '@jest/globals';"; 386: return `/** 387: * Semantic Validation Utilities 388: * Generated by AlephAuto TestRefactorWorker 389: */ 390: import { screen } from '@testing-library/react'; 391: ${importStatement} 392: /** 393: * expectSectionWithId. 394: */ 395: export function expectSectionWithId(id: string) { 396: const section = document.querySelector(\`section#\${id}\`); 397: expect(section).toBeInTheDocument(); 398: return section; 399: } 400: /** 401: * expectHeadingLevel. 402: */ 403: export function expectHeadingLevel(level: 1 | 2 | 3 | 4 | 5 | 6, name: string) { 404: const heading = screen.getByRole('heading', { level, name }); 405: expect(heading).toBeInTheDocument(); 406: return heading; 407: } 408: /** 409: * expectArticleCount. 410: */ 411: export function expectArticleCount(count: number) { 412: const articles = screen.getAllByRole('article'); 413: expect(articles).toHaveLength(count); 414: return articles; 415: } 416: /** 417: * expectContentInfo. 418: */ 419: export function expectContentInfo() { 420: const footer = screen.getByRole('contentinfo'); 421: expect(footer).toBeInTheDocument(); 422: return footer; 423: } 424: /** 425: * expectHeadingCount. 426: */ 427: export function expectHeadingCount(level: 1 | 2 | 3 | 4 | 5 | 6, count: number) { 428: const headings = screen.getAllByRole('heading', { level }); 429: expect(headings).toHaveLength(count); 430: return headings; 431: } 432: /** 433: * expectListCount. 434: */ 435: export function expectListCount(minCount: number) { 436: const lists = screen.getAllByRole('list'); 437: expect(lists.length).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(minCount); 438: return lists; 439: } 440: /** 441: * expectImageWithAlt. 442: */ 443: export function expectImageWithAlt(altText: string) { 444: const img = screen.getByAltText(altText); 445: expect(img).toBeInTheDocument(); 446: return img; 447: } 448: `; 449: } 450: /** 451: * Generate form-helpers.ts content 452: */ 453: generateFormHelpersContent(): string { 454: return `/** 455: * Form Testing Utilities 456: * Generated by AlephAuto TestRefactorWorker 457: */ 458: import { screen, within, waitFor } from '@testing-library/react'; 459: import { expect } from 'vitest'; 460: import type { UserEvent } from function getFormInputs(form: HTMLElement) { 471: return within(form).getAllByRole('textbox'); 472: } 473: /** 474: * getSubmitButton. 475: */ 476: export function getSubmitButton(form: HTMLElement) { 477: return within(form).getByRole('button', { name: /send|submit/i }); 478: } 479: export async function fillContactForm( 480: user: UserEvent, 481: data: { name: string; email: string; organization?: string; message?: string } 482: ) { 483: const form = getForm(); 484: const inputs = getFormInputs(form); 485: await user.type(inputs[0], data.name); 486: await user.type(inputs[1], data.email); 487: if (data.organization && inputs[2]) { 488: await user.type(inputs[2], data.organization); 489: } 490: return { form, inputs }; 491: } 492: /** 493: * expectFormAccessibility. 494: */ 495: export function expectFormAccessibility(form: HTMLElement) { 496: expect(form).toHaveAttribute('noValidate'); 497: expect(form).toHaveAttribute('aria-describedby'); 498: } 499: /** 500: * waitForForm. 501: */ 502: export async function waitForForm() { 503: await waitFor(() => { 504: expect(screen.getByRole('form')).toBeInTheDocument(); 505: }); 506: return getForm(); 507: } 508: `; 509: } 510: /** 511: * Categorize strings into meaningful groups based on content patterns 512: */ 513: categorizeStrings(strings: string[]): { 514: navigation: string[]; 515: actions: string[]; 516: accessibility: string[]; 517: messages: string[]; 518: headings: string[]; 519: labels: string[]; 520: misc: string[]; 521: } { 522: const categories = { 523: navigation: [] as string[], 524: actions: [] as string[], 525: accessibility: [] as string[], 526: messages: [] as string[], 527: headings: [] as string[], 528: labels: [] as string[], 529: misc: [] as string[] 530: }; 531: const navPatterns = /^(home|about|contact|services|portfolio|blog|faq|help|login|signup|sign up|sign in|register|logout|sign out|menu|nav)/i; 532: const actionPatterns = /^(submit|cancel|save|delete|edit|update|add|remove|create|confirm|continue|next|back|previous|send|close|open|reset|clear|apply|done)/i; 533: const a11yPatterns = /(open menu|close menu|toggle|expand|collapse|skip to|screen reader|aria|accessibility|keyboard|focus)/i; 534: const messagePatterns = /(error|success|warning|info|failed|invalid|required|please|thank you|completed|saved|deleted|updated|loading|processing)/i; 535: const headingPatterns = /^(welcome|get started|our |the |why |how |what |about us|contact us|frequently asked)/i; 536: const labelPatterns = /^(name|email|phone|address|password|username|message|subject|company|title|description|date|time|location|price|quantity|total)$/i; 537: for (const str of strings) { 538: const trimmed = str.trim(); 539: if (!trimmed) continue; 540: if (navPatterns.test(trimmed)) { 541: categories.navigation.push(trimmed); 542: } else if (actionPatterns.test(trimmed)) { 543: categories.actions.push(trimmed); 544: } else if (a11yPatterns.test(trimmed)) { 545: categories.accessibility.push(trimmed); 546: } else if (messagePatterns.test(trimmed)) { 547: categories.messages.push(trimmed); 548: } else if (headingPatterns.test(trimmed)) { 549: categories.headings.push(trimmed); 550: } else if (labelPatterns.test(trimmed)) { 551: categories.labels.push(trimmed); 552: } else { 553: categories.misc.push(trimmed); 554: } 555: } 556: return categories; 557: } 558: /** 559: * Generate a constant export for an array of strings 560: */ 561: generateArrayExport(name: string, strings: string[], comment: string): string { 562: if (strings.length === 0) return ''; 563: const escaped = strings.map(s => ` '${s.replace(/'/g, "\\'")}',`).join('\n'); 564: return ` 565: } 566: generateConstantsContent(hardcodedStrings: string[]): string { 567: const uniqueStrings = [...new Set(hardcodedStrings)].slice(0, 50); 568: const categories = this.categorizeStrings(uniqueStrings); 569: const sections: string[] = []; 570: sections.push(this.generateArrayExport('NAV_STRINGS', categories.navigation, 'Navigation labels')); 571: sections.push(this.generateArrayExport('ACTION_STRINGS', categories.actions, 'Button and action labels')); 572: sections.push(this.generateArrayExport('A11Y_STRINGS', categories.accessibility, 'Accessibility labels')); 573: sections.push(this.generateArrayExport('MESSAGE_STRINGS', categories.messages, 'User-facing messages')); 574: sections.push(this.generateArrayExport('HEADING_STRINGS', categories.headings, 'Headings and titles')); 575: sections.push(this.generateArrayExport('LABEL_STRINGS', categories.labels, 'Form field labels')); 576: sections.push(this.generateArrayExport('MISC_STRINGS', categories.misc, 'Other extracted strings')); 577: const nonEmpty = sections.filter(s => s.length > 0).join('\n'); 578: return `/** 579: * Test Constants 580: * Generated by AlephAuto TestRefactorWorker 581: * 582: * Auto-categorized by string content patterns. 583: */ 584: ${nonEmpty} 585: // Combined export for convenience 586: export const ALL_STRINGS = [ 587: ...NAV_STRINGS, 588: ...ACTION_STRINGS, 589: ...A11Y_STRINGS, 590: ...MESSAGE_STRINGS, 591: ...HEADING_STRINGS, 592: ...LABEL_STRINGS, 593: ...MISC_STRINGS, 594: ] as const; 595: `; 596: } 597: generateIndexContent(generatedFiles: string[]): string { 598: const exports = generatedFiles 599: .filter(f => f !== 'index.ts' && !f.includes('/')) 600: .map(f => `export * from './${f.replace('.ts', '')}';`) 601: .join('\n'); 602: return `/** 603: * Test Utilities Index 604: * Generated by AlephAuto TestRefactorWorker 605: */ 606: ${exports} 607: `; 608: } 609:
