It provides event-driven job management with SQLite persistence, Sentry integration, and Git wo...", 9: "additionalType": "https://schema.org/TechArticle" 10: } 11: </script> 12: 13: 14: The **sidequest** directory contains the core job queue framework for AlephAuto automation pipelines. It provides event-driven job management with SQLite persistence, Sentry integration, and Git workflow automation. 15: 16: ## Architecture 17: 18: ``` 19: sidequest/ 20: ├── core/ # Base framework components 21: │ ├── server.ts # SidequestServer base class (job queue engine) 22: │ ├── database.ts # SQLite persistence layer 23: │ ├── config.ts # Centralized configuration 24: │ └── index.ts # Module exports 25: │ 26: ├── pipeline-core/ # Business logic for pipelines 27: │ ├── cache/ # Git-aware caching (Redis integration) 28: │ ├── config/ # Repository configuration loader 29: │ ├── errors/ # Error classification & retry logic 30: │ ├── extractors/ # Python code block extraction 31: │ ├── git/ # Branch management, PR creation 32: │ ├── models/ # Python Pydantic models (CodeBlock, DuplicateGroup) 33: │ ├── reports/ # HTML/JSON/Markdown report generators 34: │ ├── scanners/ # AST pattern detection, repository scanning 35: │ ├── similarity/ # Multi-layer similarity algorithm (Python) 36: │ ├── types/ # TypeScript type definitions 37: │ └── utils/ # Error helpers 38: │ 39: ├── pipeline-runners/ # Pipeline entry points 40: │ ├── duplicate-detection-pipeline.ts # 7-stage duplicate detection 41: │ ├── claude-health-pipeline.ts # Claude Code health checks 42: │ ├── git-activity-pipeline.ts # Git activity reports 43: │ ├── gitignore-pipeline.ts # .gitignore management 44: │ ├── repo-cleanup-pipeline.ts # Repository cleanup 45: │ ├── schema-enhancement-pipeline.ts # Schema.org injection 46: │ ├── plugin-management-pipeline.ts # Plugin management 47: │ ├── bugfix-audit-pipeline.ts # Bugfix audit workflow 48: │ ├── dashboard-populate-pipeline.ts # Dashboard data population 49: │ ├── collect_git_activity.py # Git activity collector 50: │ └── test-refactor-pipeline.ts # Test suite refactoring 51: │ 52: ├── workers/ # Worker implementations (extend SidequestServer) 53: │ ├── duplicate-detection-worker.ts # Duplicate detection jobs 54: │ ├── claude-health-worker.ts # Health check jobs 55: │ ├── git-activity-worker.ts # Git activity jobs 56: │ ├── gitignore-worker.ts # .gitignore jobs 57: │ ├── repo-cleanup-worker.ts # Cleanup jobs 58: │ ├── repomix-worker.ts # Repomix jobs 59: │ ├── schema-enhancement-worker.ts # Schema jobs 60: │ ├── bugfix-audit-worker.ts # Bugfix audit jobs 61: │ ├── dashboard-populate-worker.ts # Dashboard populate jobs 62: │ └── test-refactor-worker.ts # Test refactor jobs 63: │ 64: ├── pipeline-core/types/ # TypeScript type definitions 65: │ ├── scan-orchestrator-types.ts # Scan orchestrator interfaces 66: │ ├── migration-types.ts # Migration definitions 67: │ └── duplicate-detection-types.ts # Zod schemas + types 68: │ 69: ├── utils/ # Utility modules 70: │ ├── logger.ts # Pino component logger 71: │ ├── doppler-resilience.ts # Doppler fallback handling 72: │ ├── directory-scanner.ts # Directory scanning 73: │ ├── pipeline-names.ts # Pipeline name constants 74: │ ├── plugin-manager.ts # Plugin management 75: │ ├── report-generator.ts # Report utilities 76: │ ├── gitignore-repomix-updater.ts # Gitignore sync 77: │ ├── schema-mcp-tools.ts # Schema MCP tools 78: │ └── refactor-test-suite.ts # Test refactoring utility 79: │ 80: └── config files 81: ├── .env.example # Environment template 82: ├── .gitignore # Git ignore rules 83: ├── git-report-config.json # Git report config 84: └── repomix.config.json # Repomix configuration 85: ``` 86: 87: ## Core Framework 88: 89: ### SidequestServer (core/server.ts) 90: 91: The base class for all workers with: 92: 93: - **Event-driven lifecycle**: `created → queued → running → completed/failed` 94: - **Concurrency control**: Configurable max concurrent jobs (default: 3) 95: - **SQLite persistence**: Job history stored in `data/jobs.db` 96: - **Sentry integration**: Error tracking and performance monitoring 97: - **Git workflow**: Optional branch creation, commits, and PR automation 98: - **Auto-retry with circuit breaker**: Classifies errors as retryable/non-retryable 99: 100: ```typescript 101: import { SidequestServer } from './core/server.ts'; 102: 103: class MyWorker extends SidequestServer { 104: constructor(options) { 105: super({ ...options, jobType: 'my-job-type' }); 106: } 107: 108: async runJobHandler(job) { 109: // Implement job logic 110: return { success: true }; 111: } 112: } 113: ``` 114: 115: ### Error Classification (pipeline-core/errors/error-classifier.ts) 116: 117: Intelligent error handling with retry logic: 118: 119: - **Retryable**: ETIMEDOUT, ECONNRESET, 5xx HTTP errors, rate limits 120: - **Non-retryable**: ENOENT, EACCES, 4xx HTTP errors, validation errors 121: - **Suggested delays**: Rate limits (60s), timeouts (10s), connection errors (5s) 122: 123: ## Pipelines 124: 125: ### 1. Duplicate Detection Pipeline 126: 127: 7-stage pipeline combining JavaScript (stages 1-2) and Python (stages 3-7): 128: 129: ```bash 130: doppler run -- RUN_ON_STARTUP=true npx tsx pipeline-runners/duplicate-detection-pipeline.ts 131: ``` 132: 133: **Stages:** 134: 1. Repository scanning (JS) 135: 2. AST pattern detection with ast-grep (JS) 136: 3. Code block extraction (Python) 137: 4. Semantic annotation (Python) 138: 5. Multi-layer similarity calculation (Python) 139: 6. Duplicate grouping (Python) 140: 7. Report generation (HTML/JSON/Markdown) 141: 142: **Similarity Algorithm Layers:** 143: - Layer 0: Complexity filtering (trivial code exclusion) 144: - Layer 1: Exact matching (hash-based, O(n)) 145: - Layer 2: Structural similarity (AST-based, configurable threshold) 146: - Layer 3: Semantic validation (category, tags, method chains) 147: - Layer 4: Quality filtering (group quality score) 148: 149: ### 2. Claude Health Pipeline 150: 151: Health checks for Claude Code configuration: 152: 153: ```bash 154: npm run claude:health # Immediate check 155: npm run claude:health:detailed # With component details 156: npm run claude:health:schedule # Cron scheduler (daily 8 AM) 157: ``` 158: 159: **Checks:** 160: - Configuration validation (settings.json, skill-rules.json) 161: - Component inventory (skills, agents, hooks, plugins) 162: - Hook permissions and executability 163: - Performance log analysis (slow hooks, failures) 164: - Plugin duplicate detection 165: 166: ### 3. Git Activity Pipeline 167: 168: Weekly/monthly git activity reports: 169: 170: ```bash 171: node --strip-types pipeline-runners/git-activity-pipeline.ts --run --weekly 172: node --strip-types pipeline-runners/git-activity-pipeline.ts --run --monthly 173: python3 pipeline-runners/collect_git_activity.py --weekly 174: ``` 175: 176: **Features:** 177: - Multi-repository scanning 178: - Language distribution analysis 179: - Commit frequency metrics 180: - Jekyll-formatted markdown output 181: 182: **`git-report-config.json` defaults:** 183: - Scans `~/code`, `~/reports`, plus `~/schema-org-file-system`, `~/claude-tool-use`, and `~/dotfiles` 184: - Includes dotfiles, uses max depth `2`, and excludes common build/dependency folders (`node_modules`, `.git`, `dist`, `build`, etc.) 185: - Report schedules: weekly `Sunday 20:00`, monthly `1st of month 08:00`, quarterly disabled 186: - Outputs reports into `~/code/PersonalSite/_work` with SVG visualizations under `assets/images/git-activity-{year}` 187: - Weekly and monthly report generation are enabled; both default to markdown output and no auto-commit/push 188: 189: ### 4. Repository Cleanup Pipeline 190: 191: Automated cleanup of build artifacts and virtual environments: 192: 193: ```bash 194: npm run cleanup:dryrun # Preview changes 195: npm run cleanup:once # Execute cleanup 196: ``` 197: 198: **Targets:** 199: - Python venvs (.venv, __pycache__, *.pyc) 200: - Node modules (node_modules, .npm-cache) 201: - Build artifacts (dist, build, *.log) 202: - Temporary files (temp, tmp, *.swp) 203: 204: ### 5. Schema Enhancement Pipeline 205: 206: Inject Schema.org structured data into HTML documents. 207: 208: ### 6. GitIgnore Pipeline 209: 210: Sync and validate .gitignore files across repositories. 211: 212: ## Python Components 213: 214: Located in `pipeline-core/` with dependencies on: 215: - **Pydantic**: Data models (CodeBlock, DuplicateGroup, ScanReport) 216: - **Standard library**: hashlib, re, json, sys 217: 218: ### Models (pipeline-core/models/) 219: 220: ```python 221: from code_block import CodeBlock, SourceLocation 222: from duplicate_group import DuplicateGroup 223: from consolidation_suggestion import ConsolidationSuggestion 224: from scan_report import ScanReport, ScanMetrics 225: ``` 226: 227: ### Similarity Module (pipeline-core/similarity/) 228: 229: ```python 230: from similarity.structural import calculate_structural_similarity, normalize_code 231: from similarity.grouping import group_by_similarity 232: from similarity.semantic import are_semantically_compatible, validate_duplicate_group 233: ``` 234: 235: **Configuration** (via environment variables): 236: - `STRUCTURAL_THRESHOLD`: Minimum similarity (default: 0.90) 237: - `MIN_GROUP_QUALITY`: Group quality threshold (default: 0.70) 238: - `MIN_LINE_COUNT`: Minimum lines (default: 1) 239: - `MIN_UNIQUE_TOKENS`: Minimum tokens (default: 3) 240: 241: ## Configuration 242: 243: ### Environment Variables 244: 245: Managed via Doppler (`bottleneck` project): 246: 247: ```bash 248: doppler setup --project bottleneck --config dev 249: doppler run -- npx tsx pipeline-runners/duplicate-detection-pipeline.ts 250: ``` 251: 252: **Key variables:** 253: - `JOBS_API_PORT`: API server port (default: 8080) 254: - `SENTRY_DSN`: Sentry error tracking 255: - `ENABLE_GIT_WORKFLOW`: Enable branch/PR creation 256: - `ENABLE_PR_CREATION`: Auto-create PRs for changes 257: - `PIPELINE_DEBUG`: Enable verbose Python debug output 258: 259: ### Repository Configuration (config/scan-repositories.json) 260: 261: ```json 262: { 263: "repositories": [ 264: { 265: "name": "my-repo", 266: "path": "/path/to/repo", 267: "enabled": true, 268: "priority": "high", 269: "scanFrequency": "daily" 270: } 271: ], 272: "groups": [ 273: { 274: "name": "web-projects", 275: "repositories": ["repo1", "repo2"], 276: "scanType": "inter-project" 277: } 278: ] 279: } 280: ``` 281: 282: ## Reports 283: 284: Generated in multiple formats: 285: 286: ### HTML Reports 287: Interactive dashboards with: 288: - Metrics cards (code blocks, duplicate groups, LOC reduction) 289: - Distribution charts (by strategy, complexity) 290: - Duplicate group details with impact scores 291: - Consolidation suggestions with ROI scores 292: 293: ### JSON Reports 294: Machine-readable output for integration: 295: ```json 296: { 297: "scan_type": "intra-project", 298: "scan_metadata": { ... }, 299: "metrics": { ... }, 300: "duplicate_groups": [ ... ], 301: "suggestions": [ ... ] 302: } 303: ``` 304: 305: ### Markdown Reports 306: Human-readable summaries for documentation. 307: 308: ## Development 309: 310: ### Adding a New Worker 311: 312: 1. Create worker in `workers/`: 313: ```typescript 314: import { SidequestServer } from '../core/server.ts'; 315: 316: export class MyWorker extends SidequestServer { 317: constructor(options = {}) { 318: super({ ...options, jobType: 'my-worker' }); 319: } 320: 321: async runJobHandler(job) { 322: // Job implementation 323: return { status: 'completed' }; 324: } 325: } 326: ``` 327: 328: 2. Create pipeline in `pipeline-runners/`: 329: ```typescript 330: import { MyWorker } from '../workers/my-worker.ts'; 331: 332: const worker = new MyWorker({ maxConcurrent: 1 }); 333: worker.addJob({ data: 'example' }); 334: ``` 335: 336: ### Type Definitions 337: 338: Use Zod schemas with TypeScript inference: 339: ```typescript 340: import { z } from 'zod'; 341: 342: export const MyJobDataSchema = z.object({ 343: type: z.literal('my-job'), 344: target: z.string() 345: }).strict(); 346: 347: export type MyJobData = z.infer<typeof MyJobDataSchema>; 348: ``` 349: 350: ## Testing 351: 352: ```bash 353: npm test # Unit tests 354: npm run test:integration # Integration tests 355: npm run typecheck # TypeScript checks 356: ``` 357: 358: ## Related Documentation 359: 360: - `../docs/API_REFERENCE.md` - REST API endpoints 361: - `../docs/architecture/ERROR_HANDLING.md` - Retry logic details 362: - `../docs/architecture/TYPE_SYSTEM.md` - Type patterns 363: - `../docs/runbooks/troubleshooting.md` - Debugging guide 1: import eslint from '@eslint/js'; 67: async function scanDir(dir) { 68: const entries = await fs.readdir(dir, { withFileTypes: true }); 69: for (const entry of entries) { 70: const fullPath = path.join(dir, entry.name); 71: if (entry.isDirectory()) { 72: await scanDir(fullPath); 73: } else if (entry.name.endsWith('.error.json')) { 74: const age = await getFileAgeDays(fullPath); 75: const size = (await fs.stat(fullPath)).size; 76: errorLogs.push({ 77: path: fullPath, 78: name: entry.name, 79: ageDays: age, 80: sizeBytes: size, 81: directory: path.relative(baseDir, dir) 82: }); 83: } 84: } 85: } 86: await scanDir(baseDir); 87: return errorLogs; 88: } 89: async function scanArchivedLogs(archiveDir) { 90: try { 91: await fs.access(archiveDir); 92: } catch { 93: return []; 94: } 95: const archivedLogs = []; 108: } 109: } 110: return archivedLogs; 111: } 112: function formatBytes(bytes) { 113: if (bytes === 0) return '0 B'; 114: const k = 1024; 115: const sizes = ['B', 'KB', 'MB', 'GB']; 116: const i = Math.floor(Math.log(bytes) / Math.log(k)); 117: return parseFloat((bytes / Math.pow(k, i)).toFixed(2)) + ' ' + sizes[i]; 118: } 119: async function archiveOldLogs(errorLogs, retentionDays, dryRun, verbose) { 120: const archiveDir = path.join(LOGS_BASE_DIR, 'archive'); 121: const toArchive = errorLogs.filter(log => log.ageDays > retentionDays); 122: if (toArchive.length === 0) { 123: console.log('✓ No error logs need archiving'); 124: return { archived: 0, totalSize: 0 }; 125: } 126: if (!dryRun) { 127: await fs.mkdir(archiveDir, { recursive: true }); 128: } 129: let archivedCount = 0; 130: let totalSize = 0; 131: for (const log of toArchive) { 132: const timestamp = new Date().toISOString().replace(/:/g, '-').split('.')[0]; 133: const archiveName = `${path.basename(log.name, '.json')}-${timestamp}.json.gz`; 134: const archivePath = path.join(archiveDir, archiveName); 135: if (dryRun) { 136: if (verbose) { 137: console.log(` [DRY RUN] Would archive: ${log.name} (${log.ageDays.toFixed(1)} days old)`); 138: } 139: } else { 140: try { 141: await compressFile(log.path, archivePath); 142: await fs.unlink(log.path); 143: if (verbose) { 144: console.log(` ✓ Archived: ${log.name} → ${archiveName}`); 145: } 146: archivedCount++; 147: totalSize += log.sizeBytes; 148: } catch (error) { 149: logError(logger, error, 'Failed to archive error log', { file: log.name }); 150: } 151: } 152: } 153: const action = dryRun ? '[DRY RUN] Would archive' : 'Archived'; 154: console.log(`${action} ${toArchive.length} error logs (${formatBytes(totalSize)})`); 155: return { archived: archivedCount, totalSize }; 156: } 157: async function deleteOldArchives(archivedLogs, retentionDays, dryRun, verbose) { 158: const toDelete = archivedLogs.filter(log => log.ageDays > retentionDays); 159: if (toDelete.length === 0) { 160: console.log('✓ No old archives need deletion'); 161: return { deleted: 0, totalSize: 0 }; 162: } 163: let deletedCount = 0; 164: let totalSize = 0; 165: for (const log of toDelete) { 166: if (dryRun) { 167: if (verbose) { 168: console.log(` [DRY RUN] Would delete: ${log.name} (${log.ageDays.toFixed(1)} days old)`); 169: } 170: } else { 171: try { 172: await fs.unlink(log.path); 173: if (verbose) { 174: console.log(` ✓ Deleted: ${log.name}`); 175: } 176: deletedCount++; 177: totalSize += log.sizeBytes; 178: } catch (error) { 179: logError(logger, error, 'Failed to delete archived log', { file: log.name }); 180: } 181: } 182: } 183: const action = dryRun ? '[DRY RUN] Would delete' : 'Deleted'; 184: console.log(`${action} ${toDelete.length} old archives (${formatBytes(totalSize)})`); 185: return { deleted: deletedCount, totalSize }; 186: } 187: function printCleanupSummary(archiveResult: { archived: number; totalSize: number }, deleteResult: { deleted: number; totalSize: number }, dryRun: boolean) { 188: console.log('\n' + '='.repeat(50)); 189: console.log('📊 Summary'); 190: console.log('='.repeat(50)); 191: console.log(`Error logs archived: ${archiveResult.archived}`); 192: console.log(`Old archives deleted: ${deleteResult.deleted}`); 193: console.log(`Total space reclaimed: ${formatBytes(archiveResult.totalSize + deleteResult.totalSize)}`); 194: if (dryRun) console.log('\n⚠️ This was a dry run - no files were modified'); 195: } 196: async function cleanup(options) { 197: console.log('\n🧹 Error Log Cleanup\n'); 198: console.log('='.repeat(50)); 199: console.log(`Retention period: ${options.retentionDays} days`); 200: console.log(`Archive retention: ${ARCHIVE_RETENTION_DAYS} days`); 201: console.log(`Mode: ${options.dryRun ? 209: console.log(` Old (>${options.retentionDays} days): ${errorLogs.length - recent}\n`); 210: } 211: const archiveResult = await archiveOldLogs(errorLogs, options.retentionDays, options.dryRun, options.verbose); self.duplicate_group_ids.append(group_id) 208: def add_suggestion_id(self, suggestion_id: str) -> None: 209: if suggestion_id not in self.suggestion_ids: 210: self.suggestion_ids.append(suggestion_id) 211: def generate_executive_summary(self) -> str: 212: repos_text = ( 213: f"{len(self.repositories)} repository" 214: if len(self.repositories) == 1 215: else f"{len(self.repositories)} repositories" calculate_code_complexity(source_code: str) -> dict: 102: import re 103: lines = [line.strip() for line in source_code.split("\n") if line.strip()] 104: line_count = len(lines) 105: tokens = re.findall(r"\b\w+\b", source_code) 106: unique_tokens = len(set(tokens)) 107: control_flow_keywords = [ 108: "if", 109: "else", 110: "for", 111: "while", 112: "switch", 113: "case", 114: "try", 115: "catch", 116: ] 117: has_control_flow = any(keyword in source_code for keyword in control_flow_keywords) 118: return { 119: "line_count": line_count, 120: "unique_tokens": unique_tokens, 121: MIN_COMPLEXITY_THRESHOLD["min_line_count"]: 126: return False 127: if complexity["unique_tokens"] < MIN_COMPLEXITY_THRESHOLD["min_unique_tokens"]: 128: if not complexity["has_control_flow"]: 129: return False 130: return True 131: def calculate_group_quality_score( 132: group_blocks: List["CodeBlock"], similarity_score: float 133: ) -> float: 134: if not group_blocks or len(group_blocks) < 2: 135: return 0.0 136: w_sim = SimilarityConfig.QUALITY_WEIGHT_SIMILARITY 137: w_size = SimilarityConfig.QUALITY_WEIGHT_SIZE 138: w_complexity = SimilarityConfig.QUALITY_WEIGHT_COMPLEXITY 139: w_semantic = SimilarityConfig.QUALITY_WEIGHT_SEMANTIC 140: similarity_factor = similarity_score * w_sim 141: size_norm = SimilarityConfig.SIZE_NORMALIZATION 142: size_factor = min(len(group_blocks) / size_norm, 1.0) * w_size 143: avg_line_count = sum(b.line_count for b in group_blocks) / len(group_blocks) 144: complexity_norm = SimilarityConfig.COMPLEXITY_NORMALIZATION 145: complexity_factor = min(avg_line_count / complexity_norm, 1.0) * w_complexity 146: categories = set(b.category for b in group_blocks) 147: pattern_ids = set(b.pattern_id for b in group_blocks) 148: if len(categories) == 1 and len(pattern_ids) == 1: 149: semantic_score = SimilarityConfig.SEMANTIC_PERFECT_CONSISTENCY 150: elif len(categories) == 1: 151: semantic_score = SimilarityConfig.SEMANTIC_SAME_CATEGORY 152: elif len(pattern_ids) == 1: 153: semantic_score = SimilarityConfig.SEMANTIC_SAME_PATTERN 154: else: 155: semantic_score = SimilarityConfig.SEMANTIC_MIXED 156: semantic_factor = semantic_score * w_semantic 157: total_quality = ( 158: similarity_factor + size_factor + complexity_factor + semantic_factor 159: ) 160: return total_quality 161: def validate_exact_group_semantics(group_blocks: 180: return ( 181: False, 182: f"{result.reason}: {result.details[0]} vs {result.details[1]}", 183: ) 184: return True, "semantically_compatible" 185: def _try_accept_group( 186: group_blocks: SemanticWeights.BOTH_EMPTY_SIMILARITY 400: if not set1 or not set2: 401: return SemanticWeights.EMPTY_SET_SIMILARITY 402: intersection = len(set1 & set2) 403: union = len(set1 | set2) 404: return _calculate_jaccard_similarity(ann1.patterns, ann2.patterns) 411: type_sim = _calculate_jaccard_similarity(ann1.data_types, ann2.data_types) 412: return ( 413: op_sim * SEMANTIC_WEIGHTS["operations"] 414: + domain_sim * SEMANTIC_WEIGHTS["domains"] 415: + pattern_sim * SEMANTIC_WEIGHTS["patterns"] 416: + type_sim * SEMANTIC_WEIGHTS["data_types"] 417: ) 418: def _intents_compatible(intent1: str, "${GREEN}✓ $1${NC}" 76: } 77: print_warning() { 78: echo -e "${YELLOW}⚠ $1${NC}" 79: } 80: print_error() { 81: echo -e "${RED}✗ $1${NC}" 82: } 83: print_info() { 84: echo -e "${BLUE}→ $1${NC}" 85: } 86: get_size() { 87: local path="$1" 88: if [ -e "$path" ]; then 89: du -sh "$path" 2>/dev/null | cut -f1 90: else 91: echo "N/A" 92: fi 93: } 94: count_files() { 95: local path="$1" 96: if [ -d "$path" ]; then 97: find "$path" -type f 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ' 98: else 99: echo "0" 100: fi 101: } 102: scan_venvs() { 103: print_info "Scanning for Python virtual environments..." >&2 104: local found=() 105: for pattern in "${VENV_PATTERNS[@]}"; do 106: while IFS= read -r -d '' dir; do 107: # Skip if inside node_modules 108: if [[ ! "$dir" =~ node_modules ]]; then 109: found+=("$dir") 110: fi 111: done < <(find "$TARGET_DIR" -maxdepth 3 -type d -name "$pattern" -print0 2>/dev/null) 112: done 113: # Remove duplicates (handle empty array safely) 114: [ ${#found[@]} -gt 0 ] && printf '%s\n' "${found[@]}" | sort -u 115: } 116: scan_temp_files() { 117: print_info "Scanning for temporary/cache files..." >&2 118: local found=() 119: for pattern in "${TEMP_FILE_PATTERNS[@]}"; do 120: while IFS= read -r -d '' file; do 121: found+=("$file") 122: done < <(find "$TARGET_DIR" -name "$pattern" -print0 2>/dev/null) 123: done 124: [ ${#found[@]} -gt 0 ] && printf '%s\n' "${found[@]}" | sort -u 125: } 126: scan_output_files() { 127: print_info "Scanning for output/generated files..." >&2 128: local found=() 129: for pattern in "${OUTPUT_FILE_PATTERNS[@]}"; do 130: while IFS= read -r -d '' file; do 131: # Keep root-level repomix-output.xml 132: if [[ "$file" != "$TARGET_DIR/repomix-output.xml" ]]; then 133: found+=("$file") 134: fi 135: done < <(find "$TARGET_DIR" -name "$pattern" -print0 2>/dev/null) 136: done 137: [ ${#found[@]} -gt 0 ] && printf '%s\n' "${found[@]}" | sort -u 138: } 139: scan_build_artifacts() { 140: print_info "Scanning for build artifacts..." >&2 141: local found=() 142: for artifact in "${BUILD_ARTIFACTS[@]}"; do 143: local path="$TARGET_DIR/$artifact" 144: if [ -d "$path" ]; then 145: found+=("$path") 146: fi 147: done 148: [ ${ 149: } 150: scan_redundant_dirs() { 151: print_info "Scanning for redundant directories..." >&2 152: local found=() 153: for dir_name in "${REDUNDANT_DIRS[@]}"; do 154: local path="$TARGET_DIR/$dir_name" 155: if [ -d "$path" ]; then 156: if [ -f "$TARGET_DIR/.gitignore" ] && grep -q "^${dir_name}/\?$" "$TARGET_DIR/.gitignore" 2>/dev/null; then 157: found+=("$path (in .gitignore)") 158: else 159: found+=("$path") 160: fi 161: fi 162: done 163: [ ${ 164: } 165: show_preview() { 166: print_header "Repository Cleanup Preview" 167: echo "Target Directory: $TARGET_DIR" 168: echo "Current Size: $(get_size "$TARGET_DIR")" 169: echo "" 170: # Scan all categories 171: local -a venvs=() 172: local -a temp_files=() 173: local -a output_files=() 174: local -a build_artifacts=() 175: local -a redundant_dirs=() 176: local previous_ifs="$IFS" 177: IFS=$'\n' 178: venvs=($(scan_venvs || true)) 179: temp_files=($(scan_temp_files || true)) 180: output_files=($(scan_output_files || true)) 181: build_artifacts=($(scan_build_artifacts || true)) 182: redundant_dirs=($(scan_redundant_dirs || true)) 183: IFS="$previous_ifs" 184: local total_items=0 185: if [ ${ 186: echo -e "${YELLOW}Python Virtual Environments (${#venvs[@]} found):${NC}" 187: for venv in "${venvs[@]}"; do 188: [ -n "$venv" ] && echo " - $venv ($(get_size "$venv"))" && ((total_items++)) 189: done 190: echo "" 191: fi 192: # Temporary files 193: if [ ${#temp_files[@]} -gt 0 ]; then 194: echo -e "${YELLOW}Temporary/Cache Files (${ 195: local count=0 196: for file in "${temp_files[@]}"; do 197: [ -n "$file" ] && ((count++)) && ((total_items++)) 198: done 199: echo " - $count files (.DS_Store, __pycache__, .swp, etc.)" 200: echo "" 201: fi 202: # Output files 203: if [ ${#output_files[@]} -gt 0 ]; then 204: echo -e "${YELLOW}Output/Generated Files (${ 205: for file in "${output_files[@]}"; do 206: [ -n "$file" ] && echo " - $file" && ((total_items++)) 207: done 208: echo "" 209: fi 210: # Build artifacts 211: if [ ${#build_artifacts[@]} -gt 0 ]; then 212: echo -e "${YELLOW}Build Artifacts (${ 213: for artifact in "${build_artifacts[@]}"; do 214: [ -n "$artifact" ] && echo " - $artifact ($(get_size "$artifact"))" && ((total_items++)) 215: done 216: echo "" 217: fi 218: # Redundant directories 219: if [ ${#redundant_dirs[@]} -gt 0 ]; then 220: echo -e "${YELLOW}Redundant Directories (${ 221: for dir in "${redundant_dirs[@]}"; do 222: [ -n "$dir" ] && echo " - $dir" && ((total_items++)) 223: done 224: echo "" 225: fi 226: if [ $total_items -eq 0 ]; then 227: print_success "No items found to clean - repository is already clean!" 228: exit 0 229: fi 230: echo -e "${YELLOW}Total items to remove: $total_items${NC}" 231: echo "" 232: # Store for cleanup functions (newline-delimited to preserve spaces in paths) 233: if [ ${#venvs[@]} -gt 0 ]; then 234: FOUND_VENVS=$(printf '%s\n' "${venvs[@]}") 235: else 236: FOUND_VENVS="" 237: fi 238: if [ ${#temp_files[@]} -gt 0 ]; then 239: FOUND_TEMP_FILES=$(printf '%s\n' "${temp_files[@]}") 240: else 241: FOUND_TEMP_FILES="" 242: fi 243: if [ ${#output_files[@]} -gt 0 ]; then 244: FOUND_OUTPUT_FILES=$(printf '%s\n' "${output_files[@]}") 245: else 246: FOUND_OUTPUT_FILES="" 247: fi 248: if [ ${#build_artifacts[@]} -gt 0 ]; then 249: FOUND_BUILD_ARTIFACTS=$(printf '%s\n' "${build_artifacts[@]}") 250: else 251: FOUND_BUILD_ARTIFACTS="" 252: fi 253: if [ ${#redundant_dirs[@]} -gt 0 ]; then 254: FOUND_REDUNDANT_DIRS=$(printf '%s\n' "${redundant_dirs[@]}") 255: else 256: FOUND_REDUNDANT_DIRS="" 257: fi 258: export FOUND_VENVS FOUND_TEMP_FILES FOUND_OUTPUT_FILES FOUND_BUILD_ARTIFACTS FOUND_REDUNDANT_DIRS 259: } 260: confirm_cleanup() { 261: read -p "Do you want to proceed with cleanup? (yes/no): " response 262: if [[ ! "$response" =~ ^[Yy][Ee][Ss]$ ]]; then 263: print_warning "Cleanup cancelled by user" 264: exit 0 265: fi 266: echo "" 267: } 268: ################################################################################ 269: # Cleanup Functions 270: ################################################################################ 271: cleanup_venvs() { 272: print_header "Step 1: Removing Python Virtual Environments" 273: local -a venvs=() 274: if [ -n "${FOUND_VENVS:-}" ]; then 275: local previous_ifs="$IFS" 276: IFS=$'\n' 277: venvs=($FOUND_VENVS) 278: IFS="$previous_ifs" 279: fi 280: local removed=0 281: if [ ${ 282: print_info "No virtual environments to remove" 283: return 284: fi 285: for venv in "${venvs[@]}"; do 286: if [ -n "$venv" ] && [ -d "$venv" ]; then 287: local size=$(get_size "$venv") 288: print_info "Removing $(basename "$venv") ($size)..." 289: rm -rf "$venv" 290: print_success "Removed $venv" 291: ((removed++)) 292: fi 293: done 294: print_success "Removed $removed virtual environment(s)" 295: } 296: cleanup_temp_files() { 297: print_header "Step 2: Removing Temporary/Cache Files" 298: local -a temp_files=() 299: if [ -n "${FOUND_TEMP_FILES:-}" ]; then 300: local previous_ifs="$IFS" 301: IFS=$'\n' 302: temp_files=($FOUND_TEMP_FILES) 303: IFS="$previous_ifs" 304: fi 305: local removed=0 306: if [ ${ 307: print_info "No temporary files to remove" 308: return 309: fi 310: for file in "${temp_files[@]}"; do 311: if [ -n "$file" ] && [ -e "$file" ]; then 312: rm -rf "$file" 2>/dev/null && ((removed++)) 313: fi 314: done 315: print_success "Removed $removed temporary file(s)" 316: } 317: cleanup_output_files() { 318: print_header "Step 3: Removing Output/Generated Files" 319: local -a output_files=() 320: if [ -n "${FOUND_OUTPUT_FILES:-}" ]; then 321: local previous_ifs="$IFS" 322: IFS=$'\n' 323: output_files=($FOUND_OUTPUT_FILES) 324: IFS="$previous_ifs" 325: fi 326: local removed=0 327: if [ ${ 328: print_info "No output files to remove" 329: return 330: fi 331: for file in "${output_files[@]}"; do 332: if [ -n "$file" ] && [ -f "$file" ]; then 333: print_info "Removing $(basename "$file")..." 334: rm -f "$file" 335: print_success "Removed $file" 336: ((removed++)) 337: fi 338: done 339: print_success "Removed $removed output file(s)" 340: } 341: cleanup_build_artifacts() { 342: print_header "Step 4: Removing Build Artifacts" 343: local -a build_artifacts=() 344: if [ -n "${FOUND_BUILD_ARTIFACTS:-}" ]; then 345: local previous_ifs="$IFS" 346: IFS=$'\n' 347: build_artifacts=($FOUND_BUILD_ARTIFACTS) 348: IFS="$previous_ifs" 349: fi 350: local removed=0 351: if [ ${ 352: print_info "No build artifacts to remove" 353: return 354: fi 355: for artifact in "${build_artifacts[@]}"; do 356: if [ -n "$artifact" ] && [ -d "$artifact" ]; then 357: local size=$(get_size "$artifact") 358: print_info "Removing $(basename "$artifact") ($size)..." 359: rm -rf "$artifact" 360: print_success "Removed $artifact" 361: ((removed++)) 362: fi 363: done 364: print_success "Removed $removed build artifact(s)" 365: } 366: cleanup_redundant_dirs() { 367: print_header "Step 5: Removing Redundant Directories" 368: local -a redundant_dirs=() 369: if [ -n "${FOUND_REDUNDANT_DIRS:-}" ]; then 370: local previous_ifs="$IFS" 371: IFS=$'\n' 372: redundant_dirs=($FOUND_REDUNDANT_DIRS) 373: IFS="$previous_ifs" 374: fi 375: local removed=0 376: if [ ${ 377: print_info "No redundant directories to remove" 378: return 379: fi 380: for dir in "${redundant_dirs[@]}"; do 381: dir="${dir% (in .gitignore)}" 382: if [ -n "$dir" ] && [ -d "$dir" ]; then 383: local size=$(get_size "$dir") 384: print_info "Removing $(basename "$dir") ($size)..." 385: rm -rf "$dir" 386: print_success "Removed $dir" 387: ((removed++)) 388: fi 389: done 390: print_success "Removed $removed redundant director(ies)" 391: } 392: print_summary() { 393: print_header "Cleanup Summary" 394: local final_size=$(get_size "$TARGET_DIR") 395: echo "Cleanup completed successfully!" 396: echo "" 397: echo "Target Directory: $TARGET_DIR" 398: echo "Final Size: $final_size" 399: echo "" 400: echo "Cleaned up:" 401: echo " ✓ Python virtual environments" 402: echo " ✓ Temporary/cache files (.DS_Store, __pycache__, etc.)" 403: echo " ✓ Output/generated files (logs, repomix files, etc.)" 404: echo " ✓ Build artifacts (.jekyll-cache, dist/, etc.)" 405: echo " ✓ Redundant directories (drafts/, temp/, backup/, etc.)" 406: echo "" 407: print_success "Repository cleanup completed!" 408: } 409: recommend_gitignore() { 410: print_header "Recommendations" 411: if [ ! -f "$TARGET_DIR/.gitignore" ]; then 412: print_warning "No .gitignore found - consider creating one" 413: return 414: fi 415: echo "Consider adding these patterns to .gitignore if not already present:" 416: echo "" 417: echo " 418: echo " venv/" 419: echo " .venv/" 420: echo " *.pyc" 421: echo " __pycache__/" 422: echo "" 423: echo " 424: echo " .DS_Store" 425: echo " Thumbs.db" 426: echo "" 427: echo " 428: echo " dist/" 429: echo " build/" 430: echo " *.log" 431: echo "" 432: echo " 433: echo " temp/" 434: echo " tmp/" 435: echo " *.swp" 436: echo "" 437: } 438: ################################################################################ 439: # Main Execution 440: ################################################################################ 441: main() { 442: print_header "Universal Repository Cleanup Script" 443: echo "Target: $TARGET_DIR" 444: echo "" 445: # Show preview and get confirmation 446: show_preview 447: confirm_cleanup 448: # Execute cleanup tasks 449: cleanup_venvs 450: cleanup_temp_files 451: cleanup_output_files 452: cleanup_build_artifacts 453: cleanup_redundant_dirs 454: # Print summary 455: print_summary 456: # Print recommendations 457: recommend_gitignore 458: print_success "All cleanup tasks completed successfully!" 459: } 460: # Run main function 461: main "$@" 1: #!/usr/bin/env npx ts-node 2: import * as fs from 'fs'; 3: import * as path from 'path'; 4: import { glob } from 'glob'; 5: import { LIMITS } from '../core/constants.ts'; 6: import { createComponentLogger } from './logger.ts'; 7: const logger = createComponentLogger('RefactorTestSuite'); 8: interface RefactorConfig { 9: projectPath: string; 10: testsDir: string; 11: utilsDir: string; 12: e2eDir: string; 13: framework: 'vitest' | 'jest' | 'playwright'; 14: } 15: interface AnalysisResult { 16: testFiles: string[]; 17: patterns: { 18: renderWaitFor: number; 19: linkValidation: number; 20: semanticChecks: number; 21: formInteractions: number; 22: hardcodedStrings: string[]; 23: duplicateAssertions: string[]; 24: }; 25: recommendations: string[]; 26: } 27: const defaultConfig: Partial<RefactorConfig> = { 28: testsDir: 'tests', 29: utilsDir: 'tests/utils', 30: e2eDir: 'tests/e2e', 31: framework: 'vitest' 32: }; 33: const REFACTOR_ANALYSIS_LIMITS = { 34: DUPLICATE_MIN_OCCURRENCES: LIMITS.HARD_CODED_STRING_MIN_OCCURRENCES, 35: MIN_HARDCODED_STRING_LENGTH: 5, 36: RECOMMENDATION_PATTERN_THRESHOLD: 5, 37: HARDCODED_STRINGS_RECOMMEND_THRESHOLD: 10, 38: MAX_EXTRACTED_CONSTANT_STRINGS: 50, 39: } as const; 40: function detectFramework(projectPath: string): 'vitest' | 'jest' | 'playwright' { 41: const packageJsonPath = path.join(projectPath, 'package.json'); 42: if (!fs.existsSync(packageJsonPath)) { 43: return 'vitest'; 44: } 45: const packageJson = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(packageJsonPath, 'utf-8')); 46: const deps = { ...packageJson.dependencies, ...packageJson.devDependencies }; 47: if (deps['vitest']) return 'vitest'; 48: if (deps['@playwright/test']) return 'playwright'; 49: return 'jest'; 50: } 51: async function findTestFiles(config: RefactorConfig): Promise<string[]> { 52: const patterns = [ 53: `${config.testsDir}/**/*.test.{ts,tsx,js,jsx}`, 54: `${config.testsDir}/**/*.spec.{ts,tsx,js,jsx}`, 55: `src/**/*.test.{ts,tsx,js,jsx}`, 56: `src/**/*.spec.{ts,tsx,js,jsx}` 57: ]; 58: const files: string[] = []; 59: for (const pattern of patterns) { 60: const matches = await glob(pattern, { 61: cwd: config.projectPath, 62: ignore: ['**/node_modules/**'] 63: }); 64: files.push(...matches); 65: } 66: return [...new Set(files)]; 67: } 68: async function analyzeTestFiles(config: RefactorConfig, testFiles: string[]): Promise<AnalysisResult> { 69: const result: AnalysisResult = { 70: testFiles, 71: patterns: { 72: renderWaitFor: 0, 73: linkValidation: 0, 74: semanticChecks: 0, 75: formInteractions: 0, 76: hardcodedStrings: [], 77: duplicateAssertions: [] 78: }, 79: recommendations: [] 80: }; 81: const stringCounts = new Map<string, number>(); 82: const assertionCounts = new Map<string, number>(); 83: for (const file of testFiles) { 84: const filePath = path.join(config.projectPath, file); 85: const content = fs.readFileSync(filePath, 'utf-8'); 86: const renderWaitMatches = content.match(/render\s*\([^)]+\);\s*await\s+waitFor/g); 87: result.patterns.renderWaitFor += renderWaitMatches?.length || 0; 88: const linkMatches = content.match(/toHaveAttribute\s*\(\s*['"]href['"]/g); 89: result.patterns.linkValidation += linkMatches?.length || 0; 90: // Count semantic checks 91: const semanticMatches = content.match(/getByRole\s*\(\s*['"]heading['"]|querySelector\s*\(\s*['"]section#/g); 92: result.patterns.semanticChecks += semanticMatches?.length || 0; 93: // Count form interactions 94: const formMatches = content.match(/userEvent\.type|fireEvent\.click|getByRole\s*\(\s*['"]form['"]/g); 95: result.patterns.formInteractions += formMatches?.length || 0; 96: // Extract hardcoded strings (quoted strings in expect statements) 97: const stringMatches = content.matchAll(/expect\s*\([^)]+\)\.toBeInTheDocument\(\)|getByText\s*\(\s*['"]([^'"]+)['"]\)/g); 98: for (const match of stringMatches) { 99: if (match[1]) { 100: const count = stringCounts.get(match[1]) || 0; 101: stringCounts.set(match[1], count + 1); 102: } 103: } 104: // Extract assertion patterns 105: const assertionMatches = content.matchAll(/(expect\([^)]+\)\.[^;]+);/g); 106: for (const match of assertionMatches) { 107: const assertion = match[1].replace(/['"][^'"]+['"]/g, '""'); 108: const count = assertionCounts.get(assertion) || 0; 109: assertionCounts.set(assertion, count + 1); 110: } 111: } 112: // Find duplicated strings (appearing minimum times) 113: for (const [str, count] of stringCounts) { 114: if ( 115: count >= REFACTOR_ANALYSIS_LIMITS.DUPLICATE_MIN_OCCURRENCES 116: && str.length > REFACTOR_ANALYSIS_LIMITS.MIN_HARDCODED_STRING_LENGTH 117: ) { 118: result.patterns.hardcodedStrings.push(str); 119: } 120: } 121: // Find duplicated assertions (appearing minimum times) 122: for (const [assertion, count] of assertionCounts) { 123: if (count >= REFACTOR_ANALYSIS_LIMITS.DUPLICATE_MIN_OCCURRENCES) { 124: result.patterns.duplicateAssertions.push(assertion); 125: } 126: } 127: // Generate recommendations 128: if (result.patterns.renderWaitFor > REFACTOR_ANALYSIS_LIMITS.RECOMMENDATION_PATTERN_THRESHOLD) { 129: result.recommendations.push('Create renderAndWait helper to reduce render + waitFor boilerplate'); 130: } 131: if (result.patterns.linkValidation > REFACTOR_ANALYSIS_LIMITS.RECOMMENDATION_PATTERN_THRESHOLD) { 132: result.recommendations.push('Create link assertion helpers (expectExternalLink, expectInternalLink, etc.)'); 133: } 134: if (result.patterns.semanticChecks > REFACTOR_ANALYSIS_LIMITS.RECOMMENDATION_PATTERN_THRESHOLD) { 135: result.recommendations.push('Create semantic validators (expectSectionWithId, expectHeadingLevel, etc.)'); 136: } 137: if (result.patterns.formInteractions > REFACTOR_ANALYSIS_LIMITS.RECOMMENDATION_PATTERN_THRESHOLD) { 138: result.recommendations.push('Create form helpers (fillContactForm, expectFormAccessibility, etc.)'); 139: } 140: if (result.patterns.hardcodedStrings.length > REFACTOR_ANALYSIS_LIMITS.HARDCODED_STRINGS_RECOMMEND_THRESHOLD) { 141: result.recommendations.push('Extract hardcoded strings to test-constants.ts'); 142: } 143: if (result.patterns.duplicateAssertions.length > REFACTOR_ANALYSIS_LIMITS.RECOMMENDATION_PATTERN_THRESHOLD) { 144: result.recommendations.push('Extract duplicate assertions to custom assertion helpers'); 145: } 146: return result; 147: } 148: /** 149: * Generate the assertions utility file 150: */ 151: function generateAssertionsFile(framework: string): string { 152: const importStatement = framework === 'vitest' 153: ? "import { expect } from 'vitest';" 154: : "import { expect } from '@jest/globals';"; 155: return `/** 156: * Test Assertions Utilities 157: * 158: * Common assertion helpers for link validation and DOM element checks. 159: * Reduces duplication across component tests. 160: */ 161: ${importStatement} 162: /** 163: * Asserts that an element is an external link with proper security attributes 164: */ 165: export function expectExternalLink(element: HTMLElement, href: string) { 166: const link = element.closest('a'); 167: expect(link).toHaveAttribute('href', href); 168: expect(link).toHaveAttribute('target', '_blank'); 169: expect(link).toHaveAttribute('rel', 'noopener noreferrer'); 170: } 171: /** 172: * Asserts that an element is an internal link with correct href 173: */ 174: export function expectInternalLink(element: HTMLElement, href: string) { 175: const link = element.closest('a'); 176: expect(link).toHaveAttribute('href', href); 177: } 178: /** 179: * Asserts that an element is a mailto link with correct email 180: */ 181: export function expectMailtoLink(element: HTMLElement, email: string) { 182: const link = element.closest('a'); 183: expect(link).toHaveAttribute('href', \`mailto:\${email}\`); 184: } 185: /** 186: * Asserts that an element is a tel link with correct phone number 187: */ 188: export function expectTelLink(element: HTMLElement, phone: string) { 189: const link = element.closest('a'); 190: expect(link).toHaveAttribute('href', \`tel:\${phone}\`); 191: } 192: /** 193: * Asserts that all external links in a container have proper security attributes 194: */ 195: export function expectAllExternalLinksSecure(container: HTMLElement) { 196: const links = container.querySelectorAll('a[target="_blank"]'); 197: links.forEach(link => { 198: expect(link).toHaveAttribute('rel', 'noopener noreferrer'); 199: expect(link).toHaveAttribute('href'); 200: }); 201: return links.length; 202: } 203: export function expectSectionLink(element: HTMLElement, sectionId: string) { 204: expectInternalLink(element, \`#\${sectionId}\`); 205: } 206: export function expectAriaLabel(element: HTMLElement, label: string) { 207: expect(element).toHaveAttribute('aria-label', label); 208: } 209: export function expectAriaDescribedBy(element: HTMLElement) { 210: expect(element).toHaveAttribute('aria-describedby'); 211: } 212: export function expectButtonType(element: HTMLElement, type: 'submit' | 'button' | 'reset') { 213: expect(element).toHaveAttribute('type', type); 214: } 215: export function expectEnabled(element: HTMLElement) { 216: expect(element).not.toBeDisabled(); 217: } 218: export function expectDisabled(element: HTMLElement) { 219: expect(element).toBeDisabled(); 220: } 221: `; 222: } 223: /** 224: * Generate the semantic validators utility file 225: */ 226: function generateSemanticValidatorsFile(framework: string): string { 227: const importStatement = framework === 'vitest' 228: ? "import { expect } from 'vitest';" 229: : "import { expect } from '@jest/globals';"; 230: return ` 231: import { screen } from '@testing-library/react'; 232: ${importStatement} 233: export function expectSectionWithId(id: string) { 234: const section = document.querySelector(\`section#\${id}\`); 235: expect(section).toBeInTheDocument(); 236: return section; 237: } 238: export function expectHeadingLevel(level: 1 | 2 | 3 | 4 | 5 | 6, name: string) { 239: const heading = screen.getByRole('heading', { level, name }); 240: expect(heading).toBeInTheDocument(); 241: return heading; 242: } 243: export function expectArticleCount(count: number) { 244: const articles = screen.getAllByRole('article'); 245: expect(articles).toHaveLength(count); 246: return articles; 247: } 248: export function expectListItemCount(minCount: number) { 249: const listItems = screen.getAllByRole('listitem'); 250: expect(listItems.length).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(minCount); 251: return listItems; 252: } 253: export function expectNavigation(name?: string) { 254: const nav = name 255: ? screen.getByRole('navigation', { name }) 256: : screen.getByRole('navigation'); 257: expect(nav).toBeInTheDocument(); 258: return nav; 259: } 260: export function expectContentInfo() { 261: const footer = screen.getByRole('contentinfo'); 262: expect(footer).toBeInTheDocument(); 263: return footer; 264: } 265: export function expectAccessibleForm() { 266: const form = screen.getByRole('form'); 267: expect(form).toBeInTheDocument(); 268: expect(form).toHaveAttribute('noValidate'); 269: expect(form).toHaveAttribute('aria-describedby'); 270: return form; 271: } 272: export function expectHeadingCount(level: 1 | 2 | 3 | 4 | 5 | 6, count: number) { 273: const headings = screen.getAllByRole('heading', { level }); 274: expect(headings).toHaveLength(count); 275: return headings; 276: } 277: export function expectListCount(minCount: number) { 278: const lists = screen.getAllByRole('list'); 279: expect(lists.length).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(minCount); 280: return lists; 281: } 282: export function expectImageWithAlt(altText: string) { 283: const img = screen.getByAltText(altText); 284: expect(img).toBeInTheDocument(); 285: return img; 286: } 287: export function expectButton(name: string | RegExp) { 288: const button = screen.getByRole('button', { name }); 289: expect(button).toBeInTheDocument(); 290: return button; 291: } 292: export function expectLink(name: string | RegExp) { 293: const link = screen.getByRole('link', { name }); 294: expect(link).toBeInTheDocument(); 295: return link; 296: } 297: `; 298: } 299: /** 300: * Generate the form helpers utility file 301: */ 302: function generateFormHelpersFile(): string { 303: return ` 304: import { screen, within, waitFor } from '@testing-library/react'; 305: import { expect } from 'vitest'; 306: import type { UserEvent } from '@testing-library/user-event'; 307: export function getForm() { 308: return screen.getByRole('form'); 309: } 310: export function getFormInputs(form: HTMLElement) { 311: return within(form).getAllByRole('textbox'); 312: } 313: export function getSubmitButton(form: HTMLElement) { 314: return within(form).getByRole('button', { name: /send|submit/i }); 315: } 316: export async function fillContactForm( 317: user: UserEvent, 318: data: { 319: name: string; repomix.instruction:
# Repomix Repository Analysis Instructions
This repository bundle is generated by Repomix for AI-assisted code review and architecture analysis.
## Bundle characteristics
- Output format: XML - Compression enabled - Comments and empty lines removed - Directory structure and summaries included for navigation - Docs are intentionally excluded to reduce token bloat - Git diffs excluded; git logs disabled/minimized
Treat this bundle as read-only context. Prefer minimal, targeted fixes.
## What to focus on first
Prioritize executable/runtime code and entrypoints:
1. `api/` — backend routes, middleware, websockets, schemas, API utilities 2. `sidequest/` — pipelines, orchestrators, scanners, workers, report generation 3. `frontend/` — UI components, websocket hooks, API client, state/store 4. `scripts/` — automation and operational tooling 5. `config/` — runtime and scanning configuration
## Navigation strategy
1. Start with directory structure + top files list. 2. Identify entrypoints (servers, orchestrators, workers). 3. Follow imports/dependency roots outward. 4. Inspect error-handling, retries/timeouts, and async coordination. 5. Keep recommendations small, localized, and concrete.
## What to ignore
- Large documentation trees / archives - Generated artifacts (including previous repomix outputs) - Media/binaries and large datasets
